rating 1.0. DO NOT get these in bulk if the gifting is important I ordered 55 gift cards for a the staff in my department at a medical facility as Christmas gifts. They were organized as 3 batches by Amazon (2 batches of 20 and one of 15) but only two of the three batches shipped, and the other was listed as delayed (when originally ordered they did not require anyone to be home to receive the shipment). I contacted customer service who assured me the third batch would arrive within 24 hours. It did not. I contacted them again and they assured me someone would get back to me to sort this our. they did not. on the third call they connected me ( after much discussion) to someone who told me there were two options: either way they would cancel the lost (on their end) cards, and I could either have the funds refunded to my credit card (with a 5-6 day lag to credit the funds) or they could apply it (over the next 4 hours) to an Amazon Gift card credited to my amazon account. I was assured by the Amazon representative that this latter refund could be applied to a reorder of the cards. What they would not do (but what would have made the most sense) was cancel and reissue the lost cards. I chose the method of applying the funds to my amazon account. HOWEVER, when I went back on line to reorder 20 cards, first I discover they now required that someone had to be home to receive the cards, AND they would not allow the funds put into my account to be used for gift cards. I again called customer service and was told I would be called back but did not hear from them and so called back again at which time I was put on the phone with a 'manager'. This gentleman confirmed that the money funded back to me could not be applied to gift cards, and that the cards could not be reissued but would have to be repurchased. All he could do was cancel the 1000$ 'card' credited to my Amazon account and send the refund to my bank card. I would then need to reorder the gift cards (with another 1000$ from my credit card), and by the way, for my trouble he wanted to give me one month of Amazon on the house (really?!).

The story should have ended here, but it did not. It appears the person in customer service who put in the cancelation of the lost cards, applied that cancelation to the batch of 20 cards I actually received rather than the one that was lost, so more than half the cards I had (20+15) were now empty/cancelled. And of course I did not discover this until cards were distributed and could not be used. So I have paid for 55 cards of which at most only 35 work (not all the cards have been tried yet), and I still have not been able to reach anyone at Amazon who can make this right. Not only have I not gotten what I paid for, I have lost several hours on the phone trying to get this fixed, and people I really care about have effectively received coal in their stockings at exactly the time you want to let the folks who work for you know how much you care about them.....as I said at the beginning. Don't get this item for those you care about. And if you want good customer service, DON'T EXPECT IT FROM AMAZON!

rating 1.0. CARD DIDN'T WORK I gave this card as a gift. Thank God it was a family member who notified me the card was rejected. They would not issue me another card. I needed to give $100.00 out of my pocket and accept a credit on my account. THAT is not right. I wonder about other gift cards I gave. Did they work? How do I know? I'm a great customer of Amazon Prime and I believe when something like this happens they need to DO THE RIGHT THING, not the easy thing. I then called them after my Chat and talked to a very nice guy. He understood my concern and looked up all the cards I had purchased 11 months ago. Three of them were rejected. I have no idea who got them as I tend to give to waitresses at Christmas time. I'm embarrassed and pissed all at the same time. If you are going to give a gift card from Amazon, make sure the receiver notifies you if it doesn't go through. I spent $200.00 dollars for the three cards that didn'y go y
through. I look at this about the same way I look at stealing. :(

rating 4.0. The product itself is great and worked perfectly, but Amazon falsified the shipping time. To those who are having trouble with their cards make sure you receive an activation slip in your package so you know that it was scanned and will work. Next, you want to make sure you go to the website on the back of the card, enter your card information and register the card and setup your pin. Also make sure you check the card balance afterwards to make sure your card was activated and has the amount it's supposed to have.

I received my card yesterday morning, I followed the instructions on the website on the back of the card to register. It was very easy. No problems at the store using the card. However, when ordering my card Amazon said Guaranteed delivery by June 4th so I bought. Upon reviewing my order after purchase Amazon said Guaranteed delivery June 5th by 5pm. I attempted to contact Amazon via live chat but he was just making excuses.
The next day I reviewed my order yet again and it said Guaranteed delivery June 5th 9pm. Also, I'm a prime customer so I should have received it in a days time (I ordered it on the 3rd) but didn't receive it until the 5th. So basically it took over 2 days to get the package.

rating 4.0. Mystery Cards! As other reviewers have mentioned, the product itself is lovely and delivery is fast! However, the cards come in snowflake tins that are SEALED and there is NO indication of denomination anywhere.  Thus, one would either have to a) unwrap the gift card to determine its value or b) call Amazon and seek assistance.  After 20 minutes on the phone I was finally able to locate an Amazon employee who had me read each ( of SEVEN) card serial number to her so she could look up the value of each card. Come on, Amazon....I'm not the first one to complain about this.  I love you guys, but this is sloppy...

rating 1.0. Size Matters BE VERY CAREFUL.  If you order cards of different denominations, as I did. Don't order them all in the snowflake tin because there is no easy way to sort which tin is for which amount. They are individually shrinked wrapped with a covered claim code. The $100 have the denomination labelled on the card but can't be identified without a call to Amazon with the serial number or removing the individual tin shrink wrap. And even then the recipient you won't know the unlabelled card is for $25. Only after calling with the serial number and a long hold could I find out which were which. ARRGGHHH.  This is frustration plus wrapping, especially for someone with arthritis.  I  decided to return all the $25 cards.

rating 2.0. Don't fall for this I ordered this gift card believing I would get a $50.00 gift card for $25.00.  NOPE.  Gift cards value was $25.00.  Just wrong to advertise this way.

rating 3.0. I ordered two gift cards in different amounts. They ... I ordered two gift cards in different amounts.  They were both in snowflake gift tins.  I assumed they would be marked so I would know the amount of each.  That is not the case.  The cards have serial numbers so I looked on the invoice to see the serial number for amount.  Not any help there either.  How do I check the value of each card.  I went to "amazon.com redeem" and it seemed the only thing I could do was attach the card to my account.  Not helpful at all.  If you are going to order gift cards from amazon, make sure you get different gift cards or tins, and write down what amount goes into which card.  Or make sure all the amounts are the same.

input: rating 1.0. Terrible Customer Service Usually I am very satisfied with Amazon.  I ordered a gift card to be sent to me to give as a gift. I ordered a $50 card but received a $100 card with a gift note from some one I didn't know to some in LA. Instead of just keeping the $100 card, I decided to do the right thing and return it. I called customer service, spoke to some one right away, but was put on hold three times with the same awful music. At first he couldn't find my order although I could see it just fine. After he scrolled half way down the screen, viola, there it was. It was, afterall,  the second order rom the top. Finally he cancelled the card I got, and is sending me the correct one. At least I hope so. I was on the phone a total of 35 minutes, most of that time on hold. Worst ever Amazon experience!

rating 5.0. Great gift for anyone - packaged by Amazon for easy giving Gift cards are always the scapegoat gift but allows people to buy what they need. rating 5.0. Elegant Gift for the Amazon lover - No gift wrap required I recently gifted this to a family member for Christmas, and was very pleased with the delivery.

Here's why to get this:

1. If you're going to get an Amazon gift card, why not get a gift card which is wrapped in an elegant little box and is guaranteed to be delivered within one business day for FREE? The card arrived on time, and with the guaranteed delivery, I knew I could time it to be exactly right for the occasion rather than taking a chance of being too early or too late by mail.

2. It saved me the hassle of gift wrapping it. Don't bother with the 'gift wrap' option during checkout either. The box is elegantly wrapped as it is.

3. It came in a large envelope rather than a full blown Amazon box, so the family member really didn't have a clue as to what it was until she opened her box.

4. The variety of gift card designs that you can pick from that can be boxed gives you the flexibility to choose the right card for your occassion.

5. My gift recipient is a huge Kindle fan. With the gift card, she got herself some books that she enjoyed rather than some odd gift I might have picked. She also shopped for some other stuff on Amazon that she would not have otherwise. So needless to say, this gift worked well for her.

If you were gifting this to someone in person rather than having it delivered, then you may want to get a small greeting card to place inside the box as an identifier. There is no place to note your name or greetings on the box itself. This isn't a fault. It's what you expect from a great gift box.

I needed a last minute Christmas present  for a family member with whom I thought we had agreed that we were not exchanging gifts this year. She called me three days before Christmas and discussed, to my horror, that she could not wait for me to see what she had gotten for me. I knew that she has a Kindle fire, so I looked locally in a mad dash at local retailers and could not find any Amazon related gift cards. They were sold out. I came home and ordered through my Amazon Prime membership and received the card the next day in a nice little gift box already wrapped. Fast and hassle free. I had already made up a card explaining that I had not received her gift yet, but I did not need to use the card. She immediately bought a book that evening. Really a life saver, thanks Amazon for the quick delivery.

The reason I am giving this three stars is that there isn't anywhere on the card where it says it is worth $10 each, so you kind of have to tell the recipient in some way that it is only a $10 card. Secondly, the glue they use to keep them together in the package leaves a residue on the back that is impossible to remove. This makes the card less than desirable.

Next time I will just go and buy individual cards. These were teacher gifts so I didn't have to see the teacher they were given to. At least they got something (from my son, who is in middle school, and even the P.E. teachers got them). So, that's my two cents on the product.

input: rating 1.0. So much for free next day delivery! I ordered this online because they said it would ship next day for free.  I ordered it on Dec. 20th, and now it's saying it's not going to be here until the 24th!  The other annoying thing is that it was a $50 gift card and yet they wouldn't count it towards my minimum $25 purchase for an add on item.  SO much for Amazon Prime ...

rating 1.0. Amazon didn't deliver as promised! Amazon promised to deliver the 2 gift cards I ordered next day!  It didn't show up next day so I waited... 2nd day goes by and still no delivery?  When asked they mentioned bad weather, yet I received 2 other packages from other companies on the same day despite the weather.  Also, the weather was fine the next day.  Amazon dropped the ball on this one and didn't even try to make it right.  Very disappointed!!

rating 4.0. Arrived quickly and was in the cute little package; barcode shows (kinda weird) I liked the packaging on this item -- it was pretty cute! I ended up sticking it inside of a Christmas bag still because -- there is a barcode that shoes on the bottom of the box! That just was weird to me. I'm sure that it is so Amazon is able to process and ship them quickly, but I thought it looked ugly, so into a bag it went.

rating 1.0. Terrible service - Not printing out the gift message and did not indicate who sent the gift to the receiver Socially it is rude that a gift receiver does not acknowledge to the sender after receiving the gift. After not receiving any word from the receiver, I waited very long time before making a step to check with the receiver about the gift. It turned out that my written message for good wishes was not delivered by Amazon.com. Socially it could be very damage to the future relationship between a gift giver and its receiver, had it not been handled correctly. It could defeat the good intention of sending a gift.

rating 1.0. One-Day Delivery Didn't Happen This card was billed as one-day delivery.  It arrived in our city within the specified time, but was delivered at least 22 hours after that.  I normally don't choose rush delivery, but we wanted to include the gift card in a package.  Because we tried to wait for the card, we had to pay very pricey delivery charges on the main package -- and we still have to mail the gift card.  I know we could have done it direct but we were trying to surprise someone.  Lesson learned.

rating 2.0. Ordered in time but didn't arrive until after Christmas I Ordered this in time but it didn't arrive until after Christmas.

Next time, I hope get a delivery handler that does a better job.

rating 5.0. Highly recommend, you cannot go wrong with Amazon Gift Cards Can't get much better than an Amazon gift card. It's one of the most generic presents that can end up being the most coveted. Amazon has absolutely everything and because of that, it's not looked at (at least in my mind) as a "lazy" gift. It can be used on anything a person could want, so it's a great gift for someone so they can get EXACTLY what they want. You don't waste money on something they won't use, and they are super happy because they get what they want.

Also, the gift cards and boxes are incredibly high quality and make GREAT presentations and gifts.

rating 4.0. Happy to have extra $5.00 in my gift card balance. As I was making a purchase thru Amazon, I was offered additional $5.00 to my gift card if I placed an order for $100.00 to my gift card balance and my remaining balance owed would be $107.00 on an item costing $112.00.  I had never done this and was not aware of how it worked.  I followed the blue links to read about it because I wanted to use all of my remaining gift card balance to make my purchase, not just the $5.00 being offered.  I read the reviews.  One review stated you can only use your gift card balance to make purchases.  Another stated they never received their additional $5.00 credit after reloading their gift card.  I went ahead and reloaded $100.00 on my gift card, and immediately received email that $100.00 was added to my gift card balance.  I was expecting it to say $105.00 was being added.  I went ahead and purchased my $112.00 item in full with my gift card balance and immediately received email notice that my additional $5.00 credit had been applied to my gift card balance.  I made another purchase today for another item on Amazon with the intention to use reward points on one of my credit cards already on file as one of my payment methods.  When I placed the order, the payment method had defaulted to use the remaining balance on my gift card balance.  I selected the blue link to change payment method to use my reward points on my credit card with any remaining balance to be paid with my credit card and removed the black dot on the radial button for my gift card balance by clicking on it and was able to save the remaining of my gift card balance with this purchase.  I reviewed everything in my order to make sure it was correct before submitting it.  I am giving a 4 star review because the system had defaulted to encourage me to use my gift card balance using $5.00 with my first order and I wanted to pay in full with my gift card balance since I had just made purchase of $100.00 to reload it and also because the second purchase I made defaulted to pay with my remaining gift card balance.  I had to make changes to suit me each time I placed my order.  Otherwise, I am grateful to have the extra $5.00 in my gift card balance.

rating 5.0. Use to consolidate pre-paid credit card balances I use these gift cards to consolidate the remaining balance on pre-paid credit cards into a single account balance because Amazon won't let you use multiple cards for a single purchase. Just use your old PP credit card to purchase a gift card for yourself for the CC balance and redeem it into your Amazon account. Then Amazon will apply your account balance to any new purchase and you can use your new PP credit card to pay the remaining amount. It's the best method I've found to get rid of old PP credit card balances.

rating 1.0. Be careful when registering this card!  Only use the website listed on the back of the card. Please Read.  My dad had $100 worth of points through his Discover card.  So I got this card for him.  The card got hacked for Play Store Purchases. He was going to use it at our local Pick n Save grocery store, it was declined.  Very embarassing!  The gift card company said to make sure to use the website listed on the card, (I thought I did) Do not goggle their website to register the card because there are a lot of scammers imitating their website.  The giftcard company said they would replace it, but they never did until I contacted the Better Business Bureau.  It took awhile but I finally got a replacement card.  Do not give up! I immediately registered the card using the website listed on the back of the card and bought a gift card for 100 for the grocery store, using the giftcard from the giftcard company.  I wanted to make sure the funds were transferred immediately to the grocery store gift card.  I hope this helps.  Also when you contact the giftcard company say over and over again "agent" so you can talk to a person.

rating 2.0. Do not purchase multiples at the same time If you are only buying one (or several of the same quantity), you can ignore this low review.

I purchased several of these gift cards as gifts for my son's teachers and therapists for end of the school year gifts. I purchased six cards in three different amounts ($15,$20,$40). When the cards arrived, there was no way to tell which amount was on which card. There was a note that said that I could line up the card number with the order number. Unfortunately as they were all connected to same order number, it was not possible to tell. I contacted customer service for help, however they were very reluctant to assist. I'm not sure if it was just the agent, or an issue with gift cards in general, but it was not good customer service, that's for sure.

Anyhow, if you are purchasing multiples in different amounts, purchase each card separately to avoid such issues.

The card itself is cute.

rating 4.0. Bent and included extra card the gift card arrived bent... not too badly, but still not ideal for presentation. ALSO- I opened the shrink wrap around the card and found TWO gift cards enclosed. I didn't know if perhaps they split the balance among 2 cards, or if one was invalid. (Or perhaps BOTH were invalid). So I had to remove the security strip to get the claim code... to then check the balance of the cards. And indeed, one included the full balance, the other was blank. This was a problem I was able to solve. But had I not opened the shrink wrap, the gift recipient would have been confused, and would have had to do that legwork on her own… Again, it's not the end of the world. I did receive the product I ordered.
I have ordered many Amazon gift cards in the past, and will continue to do so. But this experience was not perfect.

rating 1.0. These cards are blank!! Don’t gift.. You’ll look like a fool! I ordered six of these cards for my daughters teachers for teachers appreciation week. They arrived on time but apparently they were not activated by Amazon!
Just imagine you gift this card to somebody and when they try to redeem it states the balance on this is zero dollars! How embarrassing!!
I’m glad I checked with one of the teachers to see if it worked because it was a large amount $250 contributed by all the students’ parents and she said that the card was invalid!! The other teachers might have even found it awkward to tell us that the card was blank. It was a gift after all !
I called amazon customer service and ask them to activate these cards. They told me that they need to have the teachers call them directly with claim codes so they can apply the balance to their individual accounts.
Amazon was not able to activate them on their end without each person I gifted it to calling them. It was really a bad experience - first by giving somebody a gift card that was blank and then having them deal with the customer service themselves to get the money they were gifted. I am really really embarrassed and extremely unhappy with the experience. please please please do not make a fool of yourself by getting these!!

rating 4.0. Got you cardboard flowers... [[VIDEOID:c113e753e31ece0da8873aa8805634a7]] Got you cardboard flowers...and an Amazon gift card!!

First thing to note is that a little setup is required. Card was placed on back for some reason and must move to the card slot up front. Something to keep in mind when gifting.

Material for card stock and print is of very high quality.

Slider can be a bit loose. Not a nice smooth transition every time depending on how you slide it, but does give the card reveal nevertheless.

Amazon gift card itself is very basic. Just Amazon logos on a white backdrop.

Redeeming card is easy. There's a peel off sticker (instead of scratcher) on back of the card, no need to worry about scratching off the text of the code. If you use the amazon app you can redeem the amount in seconds: click the camera option and align the boxes with the text code.

rating 1.0. Don't believe Amazon promotional ads in the emails I purchased these gift cards as part of a promotion Amazon was running. I will upload a photo of the email I received. In the email it stated "Get $15.00 Amazon credit with a $50 Amazon gift card purchase". It's the end of the school year, so I purchased 3 $50.00 gift card for my kids teachers. I figured that was a great deal for both of us, I spend $35.00 and they get $50.00 for their classroom.

Four days later I attempt to purchase a birthday present and was reminded to check on my $45.00 credit I thought I had coming. I couldn't find it, used all their "self help" links, and finally found a phone number to speak to a human and get help. First lady was wonderful, told me the reason I didn't get it was because I had purchased all three in one transaction, instead of 3. I thought that was silly so I asked for a supervisor.

The supervisor, Deo located in the Philippines, was the supervisor I was connected to. He informed me what the email really meant was ONE $15.00 credit for purchasing OVER $50.00 in gift cards. Clearly that email is open to interpretation. He said it was written in the terms and conditions...however when I tried to read them for myself I was sent to a different screen (picture attached). I asked to speak with his supervisor, to which he informed me he is the very top of the customer service center. I asked for his boss. He said he was only authorized to give me a mailing address. Dumbfounded I asked him if Amazon corporate office in Washington does not have phones or email. He said they are only allowed to contact them via their computer system.

Most of my conversation with Deo was in silence. I'm assuming he was typing up everything I was saying for amazon's records. 54 minutes into my conversation the line disconnected and they never called back. I called back to get amazon's address and was surprisingly connected to a customer service rep who worked in the same department as Deo. He spoke with Deo (who was unable to speak with me since he was busy with another customer). The new customer service rep, Ian, could not have been kinder and more professional. I felt like he was actually listening to my complaint verse Deo who just wanted to record every little thing I said.

Ian helped me get the contact information I needed, and also gave me the email address of where I could forward a copy of the email in question. Ian also provided me with the PO Box for Amazon Corporate Office in Seattle. Since I can not voice my compliant to Amazon corporate office directly, and I must write a letter, I figured I would share it with all who would listen. I hope your Amazon experience is better than mine. And Deo, you might want to pay attention to Ian and Mercy - they are customer service pros.

Long story short, Amazon - who is worth $430 BILLION - would rather lose a long time customer over $30. But as for the product, it was adorable and looked great in my candy bouquets. I'll attach a picture of that too.

***UPDATE*** After I emailed Amazon as part of a survey they sent, I received the following response:
Hello,

I understand you're concerned about $15 credit for purchasing $50 gift cards on our website.

I can completely understand how frustrating and annoying the situation can be when something like this happens but believe us it was never our intention.

After reviewing your previous correspondences with our customer service, I can confirm the information provided to you was correct. As my colleague mentioned, the promotional credit will be provided only one per customer.

You can view the restrictions for promotion code here:

http://www.amazon.com/promos

I realize this is frustrating; however we aren't able to offer any additional insight or action on this matter and any further inquiries regarding this issue won't receive a response.

We appreciate your understanding.

Best regards,
Madhan P.
Amazon.com

I did go to that link, and there is nothing that addresses my concern or complaint. I wrote back asking them to highlight the part that actually has to do with my complaint. Don't worry, I'll update this if they ever respond.

rating 5.0. Fast and Easy! Amazon.com Gift Card Balance Reload:

This was SUPER easy to do! I usually just load more gift cards to my account and this was the first time I had added funds to my balance through bank info. rating 5.0. Better than the bank fees This makes ordering so much easier for me. I got mine because they offered me a free extra five dollars to use on Amazon. Hey, makes up for five of the hundreds my bank charged me.
Next it's a softer blow at checkout. At least it is for me. To see that the cost is already taken care of on money I already spent seems to soften the blow somehow. Amazon communication is another reason. They always let me know how much I spent and how much I have left to spend.

rating 5.0. Free Box with Gift Card Purchase Instead of being in a envelope and/or attached to a thank you, congratulations card; it's a card within premium looking gift box.

Box itself is very sturdy, has a premium look and feel to it.

The cloth and card holder inside; not so much. Cloth is wrapped around a plastic card holder that's glued to the box.

Cloth is very thin, but functional for what's it's intended for. If you swap in-and-out cards multiple times, the cloth may rip; but for initial first impression it won't.

Box has a slide out design to it. Has a stop gap so doesn't slide all the way out unless you intentionally use more force than you have to.

Very nice finger ribbon to pull out the contents like a drawer

Back of the box has a space for name of person of sender and receiver.

Includes UPC and Serial No. number and bar code if you find an error with the order.

Gift card design is very basic; just red with amazon logos on it. Redeeming card is easy. There's a peel off sticker (instead of scratcher), so no need to worry about scratching off the text of the code. If you use the amazon app you can redeem the amount in seconds: click the camera option and align the boxes with the text code.

Doesn't show the amount on the card seeing it's customizable on the website. But it does give a pack in slip of paper to verify amount.

Aside from plastic card holder and cloth fabric being low quality; it still gives off a premium first impression

rating 1.0. CARD DIDN'T WORK I gave this card as a gift. Thank God it was a family member who notified me the card was rejected. They would not issue me another card. I'm a great customer of Amazon Prime and I believe when something like this happens they need to DO THE RIGHT THING, not the easy thing. I then called them after my Chat and talked to a very nice guy. He understood my concern and looked up all the cards I had purchased 11 months ago. Three of them were rejected. I have no idea who got them as I tend to give to waitresses at Christmas time. I'm embarrassed and pissed all at the same time. If you are going to give a gift card from Amazon, make sure the receiver notifies you if it doesn't go through. I spent $200.00 dollars for the three cards that didn'y go y
through. I look at this about the same way I look at stealing. :(

rating 1.0. DO NOT get these in bulk if the gifting is important I ordered 55 gift cards for a the staff in my department at a medical facility as Christmas gifts. They were organized as 3 batches by Amazon (2 batches of 20 and one of 15) but only two of the three batches shipped, and the other was listed as delayed (when originally ordered they did not require anyone to be home to receive the shipment). I contacted customer service who assured me the third batch would arrive within 24 hours. It did not. I contacted them again and they assured me someone would get back to me to sort this our. they did not. on the third call they connected me ( after much discussion) to someone who told me there were two options: either way they would cancel the lost (on their end) cards, and I could either have the funds refunded to my credit card (with a 5-6 day lag to credit the funds) or they could apply it (over the next 4 hours) to an Amazon Gift card credited to my amazon account. I was assured by the Amazon representative that this latter refund could be applied to a reorder of the cards. What they would not do (but what would have made the most sense) was cancel and reissue the lost cards. I chose the method of applying the funds to my amazon account. HOWEVER, when I went back on line to reorder 20 cards, first I discover they now required that someone had to be home to receive the cards, AND they would not allow the funds put into my account to be used for gift cards. I again called customer service and was told I would be called back but did not hear from them and so called back again at which time I was put on the phone with a 'manager'. This gentleman confirmed that the money funded back to me could not be applied to gift cards, and that the cards could not be reissued but would have to be repurchased. All he could do was cancel the 1000$ 'card' credited to my Amazon account and send the refund to my bank card. I would then need to reorder the gift cards (with another 1000$ from my credit card), and by the way, for my trouble he wanted to give me one month of Amazon on the house (really?!).

The story should have ended here, but it did not. It appears the person in customer service who put in the cancelation of the lost cards, applied that cancelation to the batch of 20 cards I actually received rather than the one that was lost, so more than half the cards I had (20+15) were now empty/cancelled. And of course I did not discover this until cards were distributed and could not be used. So I have paid for 55 cards of which at most only 35 work (not all the cards have been tried yet), and I still have not been able to reach anyone at Amazon who can make this right. Not only have I not gotten what I paid for, I have lost several hours on the phone trying to get this fixed, and people I really care about have effectively received coal in their stockings at exactly the time you want to let the folks who work for you know how much you care about them.....as I said at the beginning. Don't get this item for those you care about. And if you want good customer service, DON'T EXPECT IT FROM AMAZON!

input: rating 5.0. Great way to buy without having to input your credit card number. The grocery store had Amazon gift cards so I bought a couple and easily input the information on my account.  My pending orders were shipped on time. Thanks Amazon rep for suggesting this temporary fix!  I know this would also be a great idea for those who don't want to put their credit card numbers on line.

rating 5.0. Use to consolidate pre-paid credit card balances I use these gift cards to consolidate the remaining balance on pre-paid credit cards into a single account balance because Amazon won't let you use multiple cards for a single purchase. Just use your old PP credit card to purchase a gift card for yourself for the CC balance and redeem it into your Amazon account. Then Amazon will apply your account balance to any new purchase and you can use your new PP credit card to pay the remaining amount. It's the best method I've found to get rid of old PP credit card balances.

rating 2.0. Cheap looking Thick paper cards that may peel off These gift cards are made of thick stock paper/cardboard. They add a tacky substance to prevent the cards from moving around. Tacky substance + paper material = peeling. They are not good gift cards to give out as they feel and look cheap, and that's assuming you can get that tacky substance off without peeling part of the gift card (some of which will actually peel off the card number). You should expect that at least 1 out of the 4 will be un-giftable because the tacky will stick so much that the surface from the gift card will peel off with the tacky substance.  You are much better off getting another product where they use the standard plastic gift cards (like the ones they sell in the Starbucks stores)

input: rating 1.0. Thank You For Superb Ignorance, Amazon I really liked the idea of being able to gift different denominations to family members. But gift cards received did not have an amount indicated on the packages. Following the instructions listed on Amazon's website and with each shipped gift card to determine amounts, there was no information I was able to acquire and it continued to redirect me to the product listing on their site to purchase the item. This was after I was being directed to my cart to view products. One would have presumed that a bar code/ redemption code would be listed and you could match it up to a physical card. Furthermore, finally locating where I could enter this information in, it would only ADD to my card balance through card redemption. These are gift cards I purchased for others and there is no way to determine that without Amazon wanting to add the card amounts to my card balance. Severely disappointed. Thanks for ruining Christmas, Bezos.

Pay attention if you purchase with an incentive I like having a gift card balance from which payment is made for small ticket items and tend to uncheck the box for applying a gift card balance for purchases where a credit card payment is more beneficial, as when an extended warranty is offered.  Since this has no expiration date, I find that it allows me to save time when balancing my credit card statements at the end of the month, particularly since I can check my gift card balance and see exactly when and for what amount the payment was made.  When Amazon offers an incentive to reload is when I typically make my purchases, and although it has not applied the additional amount instantaneously, I have found that after a phone call, it miraculously tends to appear.  For this reason, I have to deduct a star from my review.

current_product_reviews:
rating 5.0. Slide up to reveal card [[VIDEOID:2ef078ae9120aa06532c2d30d4018141]] Very inviting design. Slide up to reveal card.

Card is in it's own insert slot so won't get stuck while sliding

Card design is very generic, just the Amazon logo (probably because you can select your own amount)

On back there's a cutout for UPC, Serial number scanning code

Redeeming card is easy. There's a peel off sticker (instead of scratcher) on back of the card, no need to worry about scratching off the text of the code. If you use the amazon app you can redeem the amount in seconds: click the camera option and align the boxes with the text code.

Shipping is ridiculously fast. Can setup for next day delivery for no additional charge.

rating 5.0. Free Tin Box with Gift card purchase Standard Amazon gift card in a metal tin with textured diamond plate pattern

Redeeming card is easy. There's a peel off sticker (instead of scratcher), so no need to worry about scratching off the text of the code. If you use the amazon app you can redeem the amount in seconds: click the camera option and align the boxes with the text code.

rating 5.0. Teaser of What's to Come It's just a mini shipping box containing a gift card inside.

Redeeming card is easy. There's a peel off sticker (instead of scratcher), so no need to worry about scratching off the text of the code. If you use the amazon app you can redeem the amount in seconds: click the camera option and align the boxes with the text code.

*Unless you get a yellow mailing bag instead lol

rating 5.0. Fun Gift Card Slider [[VIDEOID:362fcac7daee16967f9e95cee3ffa16c]] Great gift card slider for those who like to switch things up form other physical gift card distribution methods like boxes, bi-fold cards and/or envelopes

Middle section gives off a penguin stare, but changes into a smile-like eye appearance when tab is pulled.

When fully open; left side displays over a dozen penguins also giving a smile-like gester...with two balls and a dot (from original penguin eye stare).

Right side also have dozen plus penguins along with a gift card attached via non-residue like glue.

Back of the slider when fully expanded shows back of the penguins on the right and left side minus the eyes.

Sliding is very fluid. Can see a toddler having tons of fun sliding the card in and out

Redeeming card is easy. There's a peel off sticker (instead of scratcher), so no need to worry about scratching off the text of the code. If you use the amazon app you can redeem the amount in seconds: click the camera option and align the boxes with the text code.

Overall, very unique designed card.

rating 5.0. Got this for Lunar New Year Instead of handing out traditional red envelopes with cash for Lunar New Year, decided to hand out $10 amazon gift cards instead.

Doesn't have much to do with Lunar New Year themes with exception of the red coloring on the envelopes.

Can also be used for distributing in bulk at parties or other special occasions; giving every one the same gift so no one feels left out or start comparing which person got the better gift card design. Seeing the golden snow flake imprints, more catered towards the winter season.

Paper/cardboard for the envelope is of good quality. Has slight texture to it.

Card is held onto the envelope via light glue, which can be removed without leaving residue.

There is a cutout on back displaying UPC code or serial number.

Not really re-usable unless you have glue or double sided tape. But it does give off a premium first impression.

Gift card design itself is very basic; just white with red amazon logos on it. Has the amount specified on the corner which is a nice touch and don't keep the receiver guessing how much is inside

Redeeming card is easy. There's a peel off sticker (instead of scratcher), so no need to worry about scratching off the text of the code. If you use the amazon app you can redeem the amount in seconds: click the camera option and align the boxes with the text code.

Overall, great gift card set to distribute in bulk

rating 3.0. Not What I Expected Did not receive bookmark sleeve or the ribbon in stock photos

Code is on the back of the bookmark. From the photos thought it was a separate card or voucher. Fell odd when in use; when you flip it over, you see the gift card amount and bar codes. Guess it can be a motivator to finish a book, rewarding yourself after it's done, but only can be used once.

Code redemption is a scratch off which is disappointing. Thought they went pass that, and gone with a peel off design. Must have a coin or key handy.

If you use the amazon app you can redeem the amount in seconds: click the camera option and align the boxes with the text code.

Shows the amount on the back of the bookmark, so no surprises.

Quality of the bookmark is good. Sturdy plastic. Slightly thinner than regular Amazon Gift Card. Is tear-proof, but can be cut with scissors.

Essentially just an Amazon Gift Card but in a different form factor: in a shape that can be used as a bookmark

rating 5.0. Free Box with Gift Card Purchase Instead of being in a envelope and/or attached to a thank you, congratulations card; it's a card within premium looking gift box.

Box itself is very sturdy, has a premium look and feel to it.

The cloth and card holder inside; not so much. Cloth is wrapped around a plastic card holder that's glued to the box.

Cloth is very thin, but functional for what's it's intended for. If you swap in-and-out cards multiple times, the cloth may rip; but for initial first impression it won't.

Box has a slide out design to it. Has a stop gap so doesn't slide all the way out unless you intentionally use more force than you have to.

Very nice finger ribbon to pull out the contents like a drawer

Back of the box has a space for name of person of sender and receiver.

Includes UPC and Serial No. number and bar code if you find an error with the order.

Gift card design is very basic; just red with amazon logos on it. Redeeming card is easy. There's a peel off sticker (instead of scratcher), so no need to worry about scratching off the text of the code. If you use the amazon app you can redeem the amount in seconds: click the camera option and align the boxes with the text code.

Doesn't show the amount on the card seeing it's customizable on the website. But it does give a pack in slip of paper to verify amount.

Aside from plastic card holder and cloth fabric being low quality; it still gives off a premium first impression

rating 5.0. Free Socks (size 10-13) With Gift Card Purchase Update: They now added sock size in description

First thing to note is that the sock size is 10-13 US. Both my friend and I received the same size.
Description on product page states "Gift Card is affixed inside a gift box with a pair of Happy Socks (one size)" so I assume everyone will receive the same. If not, or want to confirm (if purchased) please put a comment to let others know.

Quality of the socks are standard; not too thin and not too sweat absorbent (like sport socks). Great for formal wear and those who want to draw attention to their feet, ankles, and/or shoes with the bright colors/patterns.

Happy socks usually go for around $16 a pair, so that's how much you are potentially saving

Gift card design is very basic; just red with amazon logos on it. Redeeming card is easy. There's a peel off sticker (instead of scratcher), so no need to worry about scratching off the text of the code. If you use the amazon app you can redeem the amount in seconds: click the camera option next to search bar, swipe up for gift card redeem photo option and align the boxes with the text code.

Box is very presentable; very good gift idea for a family or friends

Overall if you're going to spend money on Amazon throughout the year, or generous enough to give someone a pair of socks + $100+ gift card; then this is a excellent purchase; just keep in mind the sock size

Redeeming card is easy. There's a peel of sticker (instead of scratcher), so no need to worry about scratching off the text of the code. rating 5.0. Layers of suprise Love the design of the pop-up box as it has a lot of layers to it.

Outward appearance as a nice triangle pattern design tied with a bow

Upon opening the receiver is surprised with balloons and a birthday greeting.

The balloon and happy birthday pop-up won't stand on it's own (unless you force it back later on*), but good as a surprise upon initial opening nevertheless

*For later display?

The card it self has a nice design to it also as it's not just a blank background with amazon logo. it has several objects displayed on the card like a bike, shoes, guitar. Doesn't have a happy birthday greeting which makes sense as you don't want to be repetitive with your presentation

Redeeming card is easy. There's a peel of sticker (instead of scratcher), so no need to worry about scratching off the text of the code. If you use the amazon app you can redeem the amount in seconds: click the camera option

Overall, will make an amazing gift for someone's birthday (especially when you don't really know what to get them)

rating 5.0. Brighten Up Someone's Day [[VIDEOID:eadb31d3ed793581d0a7c14cce88309]] Bunny Slider is very cute; change from expressionless/slight frown to overjoyed/happy after pulling down the tab.

Slider movement is very fluid and will not snag when pulling down. Pushing it back up on the other hand may cause some friction at times, but not to the point that it'll get permanently stuck

Card itself is plain and simple in the front; being all white with amazon logos

Redeeming card is easy. There's a peel of sticker (instead of scratcher), so no need to worry about scratching off the text of the code. If you use the amazon app you can redeem the amount in seconds: click the camera option next to search bar, swipe up for gift card redeem photo option and align the boxes with the text code.

Very good gift to brighten someone's day

rating 5.0. Free Bear With Gift Card Purchase You get a free holiday teddy bear with a gift card purchase. Great incentive if you purchase items off Amazon regularly, or plan to give a $100+ gift card to a friend or family member.

Supposedly this is the 10th edition of the Holiday Teddy Bear. I started collecting these 2 years ago. Last year was a polar bear with a snow flake santa hat and gloves as opposed to grizzly bear with a Santa hat got this year. Year before that was a darker grizzly bear with a beanie.

Hat is not removable (unless you mess with the stitching). There is an ear under there for those wondering. The bear is super soft and huggable. Ideal for toddlers who get attached to soft objects during cold weather. There's also a amazon logo tattoo on it's left foot

Gift card is just a neckless attached to the bear. This time, the gift code is a peel off instead of a scratch off in back. Code is very easy to apply with Amazon app, just use the camera feature instead of the traditional input method.

Minimum gift card amount is $100. From previous years track record, best to get it early or the lower demonization may sell out

Overall if you're going to spend money on Amazon throughout the year or generous enough to give someone a bear + $100+ gift card; then this is a excellent purchase

rating 5.0. Gift Card With Style Just your standard Amazon Giftcard inside a box that has the word 'Amazon' embossed on the front cover with an orange bow.

There are finger grooves for the front cover and inside the box for easy access.

Claiming the gift card once opened is also very easy. There's a peel off sticker so you don't need to bother with getting a coin or sharp metal object. Also you don't need to worry about scratching off the text for the gift card code.

If the person you gift it to have the Amazon app; they can click on the camera icon (after logging in) next to the search bar, and scan the code instead of typing each letter/number individually to claim the total amount.

Overall very simplistic design and a has great presentation. Great if you're tired of gifting gift cards in a standard envelope format.

rating 5.0. Free Bear With Gift Card Purchase You get a free holiday teddy bear with a giftcard purchase. Great incentive if you purchase items off Amazon regularly or plan to give a $100+ giftcard to a friend or family member.

Supposedly this is the 9th edition of the Holiday Teddy Bear. I started collecting these last year. Last year was a grizzly bear with the beanie as opposed to the polar bear with a Santa hat and gloves we got this year. Another difference is that the 2015 model had the Amazon (dot) com logo on the left foot and the 2016 bear has just the amazon arrow logo.

Gloves and hat are not removable (unless you mess with the stitching). The bear is super soft and huggable. Ideal for toddlers who get attached to soft objects during cold weather.

Gift card is just a neckless attached to the bear. This time it's in the shape of a gift box which is nice. Gift code is a scratch off in back.

Overall if you're going to spend money on Amazon throughout the year or generous enough to give someone a bear + $100 to someone then this is a excellent purchase

** Note: I white out the UPC and Serial Number in the photos just in case i need to use it as reference later

rating 5.0. Season's Greetings Get a free Limited Edition Gund Teddy Bear with gift card purchase.

Gift card is per usual, scratch off the foil cover from the back with your keys, coin etc and reveal the gift card code. The bar codes in front serve no purpose to the end customer.

The teddy bear is very high quality, from the fur to the hat. The bear is very fun to hug especially if you have small children.

The only downside I'd say is that the stomach area is a bit bear (pun intended) if you remove the gift card neckless attachment and wish they included some sort of holiday shirt

Overall great gift for a child and/or loved one for the holidays.

rating 5.0. Elegant Gift for the Amazon lover - No gift wrap required I recently gifted this to a family member for Christmas, and was very pleased with the delivery.

Here's why to get this:

1. If you're going to get an Amazon gift card, why not get a gift card which is wrapped in an elegant little box and is guaranteed to be delivered within one business day for FREE? The card arrived on time, and with the guaranteed delivery, I knew I could time it to be exactly right for the occasion rather than taking a chance of being too early or too late by mail.

2. It saved me the hassle of gift wrapping it. Don't bother with the 'gift wrap' option during checkout either. The box is elegantly wrapped as it is.

3. It came in a large envelope rather than a full blown Amazon box, so the family member really didn't have a clue as to what it was until she opened her box.

4. The variety of gift card designs that you can pick from that can be boxed gives you the flexibility to choose the right card for your occassion.

5. My gift recipient is a huge Kindle fan. With the gift card, she got herself some books that she enjoyed rather than some odd gift I might have picked. She also shopped for some other stuff on Amazon that she would not have otherwise. So needless to say, this gift worked well for her.

If you were gifting this to someone in person rather than having it delivered, then you may want to get a small greeting card to place inside the box as an identifier. There is no place to note your name or greetings on the box itself. This isn't a fault. It's what you expect from a great gift box.

rating 2.0. Amazon Reload: Marketing: Meh; Technology: Yeesh! Amazon has changed up its Gift Card Reload Program. The Eye Candy leaves me, a regular Gift card reloader, underwhelmed.

Indeed, it looks and feels more confusing.

The underlying tech seems to have changed.
Previously both the amount of the reload and the 2% cashback showed up quicker.

Now the reload transaction took a little longer to show up. The 2% Cash Back took even longer to show up.

Amazon failed to have announced the changes ahead of time!

Given the changes, the transaction should not slowed down and the 2% Cash Back should have been instanteous as it was previously.

Can't say I can blame other customers more alarming bad reviews. I join those saying, if it wasn't broke, why change it?

The explanation could have been given in the announcement that didn't happen.

I'll continue to use the program and monitor the situation.

rating 5.0. AVOID FRUSTRATION AND READ THIS I buy these cards all the time. They help me with spending issues I've had in the past.  They work wonderfully. However, based on a lot of the reviews I've read, it's important you read the info pamphlet so you clearly understand how to go about using it.  This will avoid many of the frustrations I read about in other reviews.  First, this card can not be used to retrieve cash.  Second, if you plan on using it for online purchases you need to register it in your name which will link it to your zip code. When merchants run CC #'s, the 4 main things that need to match with the card are name, card #, zip code and most of the time the CVV # on the back of the card. As long as you registered it correctly any online purchase will always go through assuming there's enough $ on the card.  Third, every retailers CC machines operate differently. So, it's important to ask the cashier if their machine automatically subtracts the amount available on the card from your purchase balance or if you need to tell them the exact amount that's on the card so they can manually punch that in before you swipe the card.  Asking this will avoid the card from getting declined. If your purchase balance is less then what's available on the card, then you don't need to ask. Also, there's been a handful of times where mine got declined even though I knew I had plenty of funds. I just re-ran the card as debit instead of credit and it works.  If you haven't registered your card and need to run it as debit, the pin is the last 4 digits on the card.  Fourth, if you purchase items that may hv a chance of being returned, try very hard to keep that card on hand even if it's all used up.  Most retailers insist they reimburse your $ bk onto its original form of payment or you'll end up with store credit. There's been many times I went to return and threw out my card already and had to basically make a scene to get cash back.  Lastly, never ever ever pay at the pump when getting gas w these types of prepaid cards.  There's extra security set up on the CC machine at gas pumps. It knows its a prepaid card, so it will hold all the funds available on your card for probably 7 days. Any retailer that accepts CC has to batch out their machine before any of those funds are deposited into their bank account. This is why they hold the funds.  And honestly for whatever reason that I don't understand it seems that these cards can't be used to pay bills online or over the phone. But, that's no fault of the card. That will happen no matter what prepaid card you use.  I hope this helps as you scroll along other reviews regarding their troubles they experienced. I believe if you are aware of this then it will help avoid any frustrations and help you determine if this is the right type of card depending on your needs.

rating 1.0. Terrible product, difficult to use, no customer support You're probably thinking the prepaid Mastercard (MetaBank) is the same as the prepaid Visa (Sunrise), right?  Nope.  The prepaid MC (MetaBank) is barely supported by MasterCard.  My card would not work, so I called the number on the back on the card.  They have no live support.  Then I called MasterCard directly and was disconnected multiple times.  After 3-4 attempts I was able to speak to a live rep who transferred me to multiple outside lines.  It was a nightmare.  Finally I got a young, unprofessional person, presumably working out of a basement in Utah, who informed me that I couldn't send money across PayPal using this card.  Why on Earth not?!?  It's my money and my card.  Why can't I send money over PayPal?  I had to request an invoice just to transfer funds with the card.  I had previously used this card and the Visa sold on Amazon several times.  I will never, ever use this card again though.  It took several phone calls and hours of my time to finally use this card.  They're supposed to be activated at the time of purchase, yet this card was blocked and flagged, before I could finally use it.  Don't even think about trying to use it at a gas station or store.  Avoid MetaBank and the Blackhawk Network if possible.

rating 5.0. Money was on card Update! The card still works but some locations it just declines it no matter if it's ran as debit or credit..(dollar store). Then another gas station it declined it twice but the cashier tried manually entering the card number, exp date, etc and it worked. So if it's declining it see if the cashier will manually enter the info from the card, that seems to work.

I registered the card as soon as we got it. It tells how in the pamphlet, it let me check the balance on the same website and it was$200 just like I had ordered. It also let me set up a 4 digit pin on the website. The card works fine but it does tell you where and how to use it. If you're using it to get gas pay inside not at the pump. You can't pay over the phone using this card, and it also states you can't use it for online orders, because it doesn't have your name on the front so it's not verifiable. Otherwise, we got gas today and paid inside it worked fine, used at a drive thru and worked fine so I have no complaints. It's good for things like that, as far as paying bills I don't think it will work. rating 1.0. BYE AMAZON: your service sucks and i felt lost in the real Amazon forest among deadly snakes and spiders and all kinds of danger there is no minus 1 star rating here or i'd have rated this gift card thing as -10 or even lower!

ok, sure this may be a great option for the overly rich who shop on Amazon on a daily (perhaps hourly) basis and do many large or small transactions of all kinds ...

but not for me!

and besides, i never even wanted it really!

as i was just "window shopping" mostly as well as putting a few things in my shopping cart only to remove some later as well as save some for another round of shopping in the end, i was prompted by Amazon that i HAD TO for of the gift card or i would be stopped from moving further forward and do the rest of my shopping!

isn't that ridiculous?

it also 'promised' i won't be charged for any amount of money (a big lie!) until i really wanted to buy anything and pay for it too but that was certainly not the truth because as soon as i had entered my CC info, it had been charged right away for $100 out of my bank account and into Amazon's account!

either that was a malware attack of some kind (which it was not!) or Amazon's own scam into ripping off their customers (which it does appear to be!) i had to simply OBEY the prompt in order to go on with my window shopping or i would not be allowed to go forward from that point on!

then i realized i had actually been forced into giving away my credit card number and similar sensitive info without even willing to do so!

so, i just tried to decline the gift card and remove my CC info and stuff but the Amazon website did not let me do so with the "EXCUSE" of security reasons for my own good!

long story short, it took me nearly two weeks (yes: 14 days!) in order to contact my bank first (which directed me to the Amazon's own financial department right away and it was through them that i obtained Amazon's phone number which does not appear on every single page of their website btw!) and to get to the Amazon staff (most of them speaking English with a very hard to understand accent btw!) in order to be able to remove that stupid gift card as well as delete my own CC info off my Amazon account ... (which i couldn't do even after i declined the gift card and had to wait for that a couple of more days too! and i was nearly charged twice for the whole thing btw because of the lousy way in which Amazon's gift card offer works, or at least because of the way it worked for me anyway!)

i have not deleted this account of mine with Amazon (yet!) and will keep it for informative purposes only but i'm sure i'm not going to shop from Amazon again, NEVER! there are other choices available for online shopping too, which i have tried already and i have never been in so much trouble with as i was with Amazon this time! PERIOD!

BYE AMAZON: your service sucks and i felt lost in the real Amazon forest among deadly snakes and spiders and all kinds of dangerous predators!

rating 2.0. Amazon Holiday Giftcard On December 19, 2011 I ordered 2 boxes of Amazon Giftcards to be mailed to my son and daughter-in-law in Texas, each box contains 2 $50 giftcards. They live in a 3-story condo bldg, the ground floor is the parking garage. The UPS delivery person dropped off the boxes at 6:30pm on December 21, 2011 @ their front door without ringing the door bell or knocking at the door. It was dark already, both boxes were stolen, the thief took the cards and threw the empty boxes @ a neighbor's front door. I can't believe when I heard my son told me that the outside of the box has large print which reads AMAZON GIFT CARDS!! Who in the world will do such a dumb thing?

After talking to an Amazon reprsentative, my credit card account was credited for $200.00. The representative could not answer my question regarding whether the giftcard has any log in requirements before using it. I like Amazon, but I will never purchase any Amazon Giftcards by mail again. I have never mailed any giftcards to anybody and I will never do that either!! Do it electronically!! Lesson learned.

rating 1.0. Do not trust! I purchased this $50 JC Penney gift card. When I checked the balance on it before going to the store, the online balance inquiry didn't recognize the card number. So I called the JC Penney gift card balance phone line and they told me it was either "inactive or expired." I went through the Amazon contact us - call me process and was told I had to call JC Penney to activate it! I called JC Penney balance inquiry line again, this time speaking to a human (more or less) and was told that the original purchaser (Amazon) should have had the card activated on point of purchase and that the only way that I could have the card activated would be for Amazon to prove to JC Penney that they had purchased it. In other words, I'm being sent in a revolving door where Amazon & JC Penney need to speak to one another and where I can do nothing but sit back and hope for the best? I've since contacted Amazon again, this time via email, and well, the "best" is that I sit back and wait for a refund. This is really all wrong.

rating 1.0. Recipient recieved unloaded card.  The card had zero value. Total bummer. Not much fun to get a text that says, "By the way, I'm trying to use the Subway gift card you sent and the merchant is telling me it is not loaded with any value."  After 30 minutes on and off hold with Amazon customer service, I was finally given a refund but I would have much rather had the card work when the recipient tried to use it.  It was sent to someone on a really tight budget intended to be used as a reward for sticking to her budget. VERY DISAPPOINTED!

input:
rating 5.0. I've never seen anything like this! I bought this as a Mother's Day gift for my mom. I love the surprise reveal -- so clever and unique. I've never seen anything like this!
It arrived in a bubble envelope and shrink wrapped, which is nice that Amazon went the extra mile to keep it from getting damaged. This is such a high quality item that I can't believe it was FREE with the gift card. Can't wait to see my mom's reaction!
rating 5.0. The gift card was very nice. Would recommend this to anyone Was very well received. The gift card was very nice. Would recommend this to anyone.

rating 1.0. Pretty design but useless People usually give Gift Card in lieu of present so that the recipient can buy whatever she/he wants.  This design was very spring-like.  Perfect -- or so I thought.  When I got it, my heart sunk.  The Gift Card had no denomination printed on it.  There was no envelope enclosed and worst of all, there was very little room to write any message.  (It has the same design both front and back.)  This card was very pretty, but wished I had picked another design.

rating 5.0. Delighted by this gift card card! This pop up card for Amazon gift card presentation is so cheerful and clever. I just bought 2 as appreciation gifts and previously bought one as an employee recognition award. I include here a photo of the card as it looks before being popped up.

rating 5.0. Love the design Got those for my kids' teachers. I really love the design. Gift card with the flower pot reveal is genius, making recipients feel like they got flowers at the same time yet no mess. Good for any occasion especially for teachers appreciation day. Will definitely buy them again :D

rating 5.0. Very cute way to package a gift card! These cards were very cute! I bought three for my sons' teachers for Teacher Appreciation Week and they loved them! I like that Amazon provides these card holders at no cost when you buy the gift cards. What an awesome idea and there were many to choose from!  Thanks for making the buying of a gift that anyone can appreciate (a gift card) even easier by including the wrapping.

rating 4.0. Bent and included extra card the gift card arrived bent... not too badly, but still not ideal for presentation. ALSO- I opened the shrink wrap around the card and found TWO gift cards enclosed. I didn't know if perhaps they split the balance among 2 cards, or if one was invalid. (Or perhaps BOTH were invalid). So I had to remove the security strip to get the claim code... to then check the balance of the cards. And indeed, one included the full balance, the other was blank. This was a problem I was able to solve. But had I not opened the shrink wrap, the gift recipient would have been confused, and would have had to do that legwork on her own… Again, it's not the end of the world. I did receive the product I ordered.
I have ordered many Amazon gift cards in the past, and will continue to do so. But this experience was not perfect.
rating 1.0. DISAPPOINTED Very annoyed that the gift card wasn't included in the package.  I finally had to call the recipient to make sure she received it.? Very sloppy work indeed!!!

rating 5.0. Excellent way to give a gift card! Absolutely perfect gift for Mothers Day, or any occasion where the recipient loves flowers. My mother is a HUGE flower nut (who's isn't?) so to go along with the theme of other flower related gifts, I had to get her an Amazon gift card as well so she could pick out something she's really wanted. This flower pot reveal is simply perfect, and she loved it!!

The only qualm I may see that others have noted is that there really isn't much writing space, so you might not want to plan on using this to present your message. It is a truly awesome way to give someone a gift card.

rating 1.0. Don't believe Amazon promotional ads in the emails I purchased these gift cards as part of a promotion Amazon was running. I will upload a photo of the email I received. In the email it stated "Get $15.00 Amazon credit with a $50 Amazon gift card purchase". It's the end of the school year, so I purchased 3 $50.00 gift card for my kids teachers. I figured that was a great deal for both of us, I spend $35.00 and they get $50.00 for their classroom.

Four days later I attempt to purchase a birthday present and was reminded to check on my $45.00 credit I thought I had coming. I couldn't find it, used all their "self help" links, and finally found a phone number to speak to a human and get help. First lady was wonderful, told me the reason I didn't get it was because I had purchased all three in one transaction, instead of 3. I thought that was silly so I asked for a supervisor.

The supervisor, Deo located in the Philippines, was the supervisor I was connected to. He informed me what the email really meant was ONE $15.00 credit for purchasing OVER $50.00 in gift cards. Clearly that email is open to interpretation. He said it was written in the terms and conditions...however when I tried to read them for myself I was sent to a different screen (picture attached). I asked to speak with his supervisor, to which he informed me he is the very top of the customer service center. I asked for his boss. He said he was only authorized to give me a mailing address. Dumbfounded I asked him if Amazon corporate office in Washington does not have phones or email. He said they are only allowed to contact them via their computer system.

Most of my conversation with Deo was in silence. I'm assuming he was typing up everything I was saying for amazon's records. 54 minutes into my conversation the line disconnected and they never called back. I called back to get amazon's address and was surprisingly connected to a customer service rep who worked in the same department as Deo. He spoke with Deo (who was unable to speak with me since he was busy with another customer). The new customer service rep, Ian, could not have been kinder and more professional. I felt like he was actually listening to my complaint verse Deo who just wanted to record every little thing I said.

Ian helped me get the contact information I needed, and also gave me the email address of where I could forward a copy of the email in question. Ian also provided me with the PO Box for Amazon Corporate Office in Seattle. Since I can not voice my compliant to Amazon corporate office directly, and I must write a letter, I figured I would share it with all who would listen. I hope your Amazon experience is better than mine. And Deo, you might want to pay attention to Ian and Mercy - they are customer service pros.

Long story short, Amazon - who is worth $430 BILLION - would rather lose a long time customer over $30. But as for the product, it was adorable and looked great in my candy bouquets. I'll attach a picture of that too.

***UPDATE*** After I emailed Amazon as part of a survey they sent, I received the following response:
Hello,

I understand you're concerned about $15 credit for purchasing $50 gift cards on our website.

I can completely understand how frustrating and annoying the situation can be when something like this happens but believe us it was never our intention.

After reviewing your previous correspondences with our customer service, I can confirm the information provided to you was correct. As my colleague mentioned, the promotional credit will be provided only one per customer.

You can view the restrictions for promotion code here:

http://www.amazon.com/promos

I realize this is frustrating; however we aren't able to offer any additional insight or action on this matter and any further inquiries regarding this issue won't receive a response.

We appreciate your understanding.

Best regards,
Madhan P.
Amazon.com

I did go to that link, and there is nothing that addresses my concern or complaint. I wrote back asking them to highlight the part that actually has to do with my complaint. Don't worry, I'll update this if they ever respond.

rating 2.0. Showed up bent and had to throw the card away. Mine showed up bent up even though it was well packaged in the box.  So, someone must of saw it bent and decided to mail it off anyways.  Not happy with the lack of quality control.

rating 1.0. These cards are blank!! Don’t gift.. You’ll look like a fool! I ordered six of these cards for my daughters teachers for teachers appreciation week. They arrived on time but apparently they were not activated by Amazon!
Just imagine you gift this card to somebody and when they try to redeem it states the balance on this is zero dollars! How embarrassing!!
I’m glad I checked with one of the teachers to see if it worked because it was a large amount $250 contributed by all the students’ parents and she said that the card was invalid!! The other teachers might have even found it awkward to tell us that the card was blank. It was a gift after all !
I called amazon customer service and ask them to activate these cards. They told me that they need to have the teachers call them directly with claim codes so they can apply the balance to their individual accounts.
Amazon was not able to activate them on their end without each person I gifted it to calling them. It was really a bad experience - first by giving somebody a gift card that was blank and then having them deal with the customer service themselves to get the money they were gifted. I am really really embarrassed and extremely unhappy with the experience. please please please do not make a fool of yourself by getting these!!

rating 2.0. A big bust The drama of not finding the "gift" in the mailbox of the recipient was not fun. Somehow the card was lost in the mailbox. Too much drama. Not even sure if a note was part of the package or not - so they didn't even know what the gift was when asked about it.

rating 1.0. Be Careful! Card was already used! Purchased this gift card as a gift, but the card was already redeemed. Many issues with amazon, as the order details reveal the serial # for the card, but customer service vouches that it's not able to show serial numbers.

rating 5.0. Cute to Present a Gift Card Love that Amazon has different kinds of gift cards that come within a cute card, gift box, etc.  It always makes the presentation special.
rating 1.0. Ehh Flower card came torn.  Didnt have time to replace before gift had to be given

rating 1.0. Cheap This arrived damaged. It was in a thin plastic bag in a bubble envelope but the whole back that held the card in was ripped off. Thankfully i glued it back together and it wasn't noticeable. It came with instructions on how to use the gift card which was odd because its not the same as when you purchase one from a store and  you have to include it in  the reveal card. I freaked out at first because the reveal set up did not contain the actual card but I later found it loose in the bubble envelope and it again is not at all like the gift cards you get at the store. This was just a paper card  that looked like a business card. The reveal card is nice but was damaged but the card makes it look so cheap.

rating 2.0. Do not purchase multiples at the same time If you are only buying one (or several of the same quantity), you can ignore this low review.

I purchased several of these gift cards as gifts for my son's teachers and therapists for end of the school year gifts. I purchased six cards in three different amounts ($15,$20,$40). When the cards arrived, there was no way to tell which amount was on which card. There was a note that said that I could line up the card number with the order number. Unfortunately as they were all connected to same order number, it was not possible to tell. I contacted customer service for help, however they were very reluctant to assist. I'm not sure if it was just the agent, or an issue with gift cards in general, but it was not good customer service, that's for sure.

Anyhow, if you are purchasing multiples in different amounts, purchase each card separately to avoid such issues.

The card itself is cute.

rating 1.0. Don't you think we should separate the price? I bought three $30 and one $50. It was packed together without distinction, so it was ruined when I gave it. I just gave him anything. And the cards are made of paper. Don't you think we should separate the price?
rating 5.0. So Cute This gift card was the perfect addition to the gift baskets we gave for Administrative Assistant's Day.  Tucked notes back it was bright and perfect.

current_product_reviews:
rating 4.0. Got you cardboard flowers... [[VIDEOID:c113e753e31ece0da8873aa8805634a7]] Got you cardboard flowers...and an Amazon gift card!!

First thing to note is that a little setup is required. Card was placed on back for some reason and must move to the card slot up front. Something to keep in mind when gifting.

Material for card stock and print is of very high quality.

Slider can be a bit loose. Not a nice smooth transition every time depending on how you slide it, but does give the card reveal nevertheless.

Amazon gift card itself is very basic. Just Amazon logos on a white backdrop.

Redeeming card is easy. There's a peel off sticker (instead of scratcher) on back of the card, no need to worry about scratching off the text of the code. If you use the amazon app you can redeem the amount in seconds: click the camera option and align the boxes with the text code.
other_product_reviews:
rating 5.0. Teaser of What's To Come. Basically a gift card in mini amazon box.

Cardboard quality is very nice.

Opening flaps held together by magnets

Card holder secures card in place

Back of the box has an open window for UPC and Serial No. barcode.

Card itself has various amazon device and accessories on it. When redeemed, can purchase anything off amazon and not just items displayed. Amount in card is added to your total gift card balance

Redeeming card is easy. There's a peel off sticker (instead of scratcher) on back of the card, no need to worry about scratching off the text of the code. If you use the amazon app you can redeem the amount in seconds: click the camera option and align the boxes with the text code.

rating 5.0. Slide up to reveal card [[VIDEOID:2ef078ae9120aa06532c2d30d4018141]] Very inviting design. Slide up to reveal card.

Card is in it's own insert slot so won't get stuck while sliding

Card design is very generic, just the Amazon logo (probably because you can select your own amount)

On back there's a cutout for UPC, Serial number scanning code

Redeeming card is easy. There's a peel off sticker (instead of scratcher) on back of the card, no need to worry about scratching off the text of the code. If you use the amazon app you can redeem the amount in seconds: click the camera option and align the boxes with the text code.

Shipping is ridiculously fast. Can setup for next day delivery for no additional charge.

rating 5.0. Graduation cap box + gift card Just a tassel attached to a black box resembling a graduation cap.

Shipping is ridiculously fast. Can setup for next day delivery for no additional charge.
