Redeeming card is easy. There's a peel off sticker (instead of scratcher), so no need to worry about scratching off the text of the code. If you use the amazon app you can redeem the amount in seconds: click the camera option and align the boxes with the text code.

Shipping is ridiculously fast. Can setup for next day delivery for no additional charge

rating 5.0. Teaser of What's to Come It's just a mini shipping box containing a gift card inside.

High quality cardboard. The front two flaps is held down via small magnets so it doesn't flap around or stay loose

Redeeming card is easy. There's a peel off sticker (instead of scratcher), so no need to worry about scratching off the text of the code. If you use the amazon app you can redeem the amount in seconds: click the camera option and align the boxes with the text code.

*Unless you get a yellow mailing bag instead lol

rating 5.0. Fun Gift Card Slider [[VIDEOID:362fcac7daee16967f9e95cee3ffa16c]] Great gift card slider for those who like to switch things up form other physical gift card distribution methods like boxes, bi-fold cards and/or envelopes

Middle section gives off a penguin stare, but changes into a smile-like eye appearance when tab is pulled.

When fully open; left side displays over a dozen penguins also giving a smile-like gester...with two balls and a dot (from original penguin eye stare).

Right side also have dozen plus penguins along with a gift card attached via non-residue like glue.

Back of the slider when fully expanded shows back of the penguins on the right and left side minus the eyes.

Sliding is very fluid. Can see a toddler having tons of fun sliding the card in and out

Redeeming card is easy. There's a peel off sticker (instead of scratcher), so no need to worry about scratching off the text of the code. If you use the amazon app you can redeem the amount in seconds: click the camera option and align the boxes with the text code.

Overall, very unique designed card.

rating 5.0. Got this for Lunar New Year Instead of handing out traditional red envelopes with cash for Lunar New Year, decided to hand out $10 amazon gift cards instead.

Doesn't have much to do with Lunar New Year themes with exception of the red coloring on the envelopes.

Can also be used for distributing in bulk at parties or other special occasions; giving every one the same gift so no one feels left out or start comparing which person got the better gift card design. Seeing the golden snow flake imprints, more catered towards the winter season.

Paper/cardboard for the envelope is of good quality. Has slight texture to it.

Card is held onto the envelope via light glue, which can be removed without leaving residue.

There is a cutout on back displaying UPC code or serial number.

Not really re-usable unless you have glue or double sided tape. But it does give off a premium first impression.

Gift card design itself is very basic; just white with red amazon logos on it. Has the amount specified on the corner which is a nice touch and don't keep the receiver guessing how much is inside

Redeeming card is easy. There's a peel off sticker (instead of scratcher), so no need to worry about scratching off the text of the code. If you use the amazon app you can redeem the amount in seconds: click the camera option and align the boxes with the text code.

Overall, great gift card set to distribute in bulk

rating 3.0. Not What I Expected Did not receive bookmark sleeve or the ribbon in stock photos

Code is on the back of the bookmark. From the photos thought it was a separate card or voucher. Fell odd when in use; when you flip it over, you see the gift card amount and bar codes. Guess it can be a motivator to finish a book, rewarding yourself after it's done, but only can be used once.

Code redemption is a scratch off which is disappointing. Thought they went pass that, and gone with a peel off design. Must have a coin or key handy.

If you use the amazon app you can redeem the amount in seconds: click the camera option and align the boxes with the text code.

Shows the amount on the back of the bookmark, so no surprises.

Quality of the bookmark is good. Sturdy plastic. Slightly thinner than regular Amazon Gift Card. Is tear-proof, but can be cut with scissors.

Essentially just an Amazon Gift Card but in a different form factor: in a shape that can be used as a bookmark

rating 5.0. Free Box with Gift Card Purchase Instead of being in a envelope and/or attached to a thank you, congratulations card; it's a card within premium looking gift box.

Box itself is very sturdy, has a premium look and feel to it.

The cloth and card holder inside; not so much. Cloth is wrapped around a plastic card holder that's glued to the box.

Cloth is very thin, but functional for what's it's intended for. If you swap in-and-out cards multiple times, the cloth may rip; but for initial first impression it won't.

Box has a slide out design to it. Has a stop gap so doesn't slide all the way out unless you intentionally use more force than you have to.

Very nice finger ribbon to pull out the contents like a drawer

Back of the box has a space for name of person of sender and receiver.

Includes UPC and Serial No. number and bar code if you find an error with the order.

Gift card design is very basic; just red with amazon logos on it. Redeeming card is easy. There's a peel off sticker (instead of scratcher), so no need to worry about scratching off the text of the code. If you use the amazon app you can redeem the amount in seconds: click the camera option and align the boxes with the text code.

Doesn't show the amount on the card seeing it's customizable on the website. But it does give a pack in slip of paper to verify amount.

Aside from plastic card holder and cloth fabric being low quality; it still gives off a premium first impression

rating 5.0. Free Socks (size 10-13) With Gift Card Purchase Update: They now added sock size in description

First thing to note is that the sock size is 10-13 US. Both my friend and I received the same size.
Description on product page states "Gift Card is affixed inside a gift box with a pair of Happy Socks (one size)" so I assume everyone will receive the same. If not, or want to confirm (if purchased) please put a comment to let others know.

Quality of the socks are standard; not too thin and not too sweat absorbent (like sport socks). Great for formal wear and those who want to draw attention to their feet, ankles, and/or shoes with the bright colors/patterns.

Happy socks usually go for around $16 a pair, so that's how much you are potentially saving

Gift card design is very basic; just red with amazon logos on it. Redeeming card is easy. There's a peel off sticker (instead of scratcher), so no need to worry about scratching off the text of the code. If you use the amazon app you can redeem the amount in seconds: click the camera option next to search bar, swipe up for gift card redeem photo option and align the boxes with the text code.

Box is very presentable; very good gift idea for a family or friends

Overall if you're going to spend money on Amazon throughout the year, or generous enough to give someone a pair of socks + $100+ gift card; then this is a excellent purchase; just keep in mind the sock size

rating 5.0. Teaser of What's to Come It's just a mini shipping box containing a gift card inside.

High quality cardboard. The front two flaps is held down via small magnets so it doesn't flap around/stay loose

Redeeming card is easy. There's a peel of sticker (instead of scratcher), so no need to worry about scratching off the text of the code. If you use the amazon app you can redeem the amount in seconds: click the camera option next to search bar, swipe up for gift card redeem photo option and align the boxes with the text code.

Very good hint of what's to come once the card is redeemed

rating 5.0. Layers of suprise Love the design of the pop-up box as it has a lot of layers to it.

Outward appearance as a nice triangle pattern design tied with a bow

Upon opening the receiver is surprised with balloons and a birthday greeting.

The balloon and happy birthday pop-up won't stand on it's own (unless you force it back later on*), but good as a surprise upon initial opening nevertheless

*For later display?

The card it self has a nice design to it also as it's not just a blank background with amazon logo. it has several objects displayed on the card like a bike, shoes, guitar. Doesn't have a happy birthday greeting which makes sense as you don't want to be repetitive with your presentation

Redeeming card is easy. There's a peel of sticker (instead of scratcher), so no need to worry about scratching off the text of the code. If you use the amazon app you can redeem the amount in seconds: click the camera option

Overall, will make an amazing gift for someone's birthday (especially when you don't really know what to get them)

rating 5.0. Brighten Up Someone's Day [[VIDEOID:eadb31d3ed793581d0a7c14cce88309]] Bunny Slider is very cute; change from expressionless/slight frown to overjoyed/happy after pulling down the tab.

Slider movement is very fluid and will not snag when pulling down. Pushing it back up on the other hand may cause some friction at times, but not to the point that it'll get permanently stuck

Card itself is plain and simple in the front; being all white with amazon logos

Redeeming card is easy. There's a peel of sticker (instead of scratcher), so no need to worry about scratching off the text of the code. If you use the amazon app you can redeem the amount in seconds: click the camera option next to search bar, swipe up for gift card redeem photo option and align the boxes with the text code.

Very good gift to brighten someone's day

rating 5.0. Free Bear With Gift Card Purchase You get a free holiday teddy bear with a gift card purchase. Great incentive if you purchase items off Amazon regularly, or plan to give a $100+ gift card to a friend or family member.

Supposedly this is the 10th edition of the Holiday Teddy Bear. I started collecting these 2 years ago. Last year was a polar bear with a snow flake santa hat and gloves as opposed to grizzly bear with a Santa hat got this year. Year before that was a darker grizzly bear with a beanie.

Hat is not removable (unless you mess with the stitching). There is an ear under there for those wondering. The bear is super soft and huggable. Ideal for toddlers who get attached to soft objects during cold weather. There's also a amazon logo tattoo on it's left foot

Gift card is just a neckless attached to the bear. This time, the gift code is a peel off instead of a scratch off in back. Code is very easy to apply with Amazon app, just use the camera feature instead of the traditional input method.

Minimum gift card amount is $100. From previous years track record, best to get it early or the lower demonization may sell out

Overall if you're going to spend money on Amazon throughout the year or generous enough to give someone a bear + $100+ gift card; then this is a excellent purchase

rating 5.0. Gift Card With Style Just your standard Amazon Giftcard inside a box that has the word 'Amazon' embossed on the front cover with an orange bow.

There are finger grooves for the front cover and inside the box for easy access.

Claiming the gift card once opened is also very easy. There's a peel off sticker so you don't need to bother with getting a coin or sharp metal object. Also you don't need to worry about scratching off the text for the gift card code.

If the person you gift it to have the Amazon app; they can click on the camera icon (after logging in) next to the search bar, and scan the code instead of typing each letter/number individually to claim the total amount.

Overall very simplistic design and a has great presentation. Great if you're tired of gifting gift cards in a standard envelope format.

rating 5.0. Free Bear With Gift Card Purchase You get a free holiday teddy bear with a giftcard purchase. Great incentive if you purchase items off Amazon regularly or plan to give a $100+ giftcard to a friend or family member.

Supposedly this is the 9th edition of the Holiday Teddy Bear. I started collecting these last year. Last year was a grizzly bear with the beanie as opposed to the polar bear with a Santa hat and gloves we got this year. Another difference is that the 2015 model had the Amazon (dot) com logo on the left foot and the 2016 bear has just the amazon arrow logo.

Gloves and hat are not removable (unless you mess with the stitching). The bear is super soft and huggable. Ideal for toddlers who get attached to soft objects during cold weather.

Gift card is just a neckless attached to the bear. This time it's in the shape of a gift box which is nice. Gift code is a scratch off in back.

Overall if you're going to spend money on Amazon throughout the year or generous enough to give someone a bear + $100 to someone then this is a excellent purchase

** Note: I white out the UPC and Serial Number in the photos just in case i need to use it as reference later

rating 5.0. Season's Greetings Get a free Limited Edition Gund Teddy Bear with gift card purchase.

Gift card is per usual, scratch off the foil cover from the back with your keys, coin etc and reveal the gift card code. The bar codes in front serve no purpose to the end customer.

The teddy bear is very high quality, from the fur to the hat. The bear is very fun to hug especially if you have small children.

The only downside I'd say is that the stomach area is a bit bear (pun intended) if you remove the gift card neckless attachment and wish they included some sort of holiday shirt

Overall great gift for a child and/or loved one for the holidays.

input: rating 1.0. So much for free next day delivery! I ordered this online because they said it would ship next day for free.  I ordered it on Dec. 20th, and now it's saying it's not going to be here until the 24th!  The other annoying thing is that it was a $50 gift card and yet they wouldn't count it towards my minimum $25 purchase for an add on item.  SO much for Amazon Prime ...

rating 1.0. Amazon didn't deliver as promised! Amazon promised to deliver the 2 gift cards I ordered next day!  It didn't show up next day so I waited... 2nd day goes by and still no delivery? Amazon dropped the ball on this one and didn't even try to make it right.  Very disappointed!!

rating 1.0. Terrible service - Not printing out the gift message and did not indicate who sent the gift to the receiver Socially it is rude that a gift receiver does not acknowledge to the sender after receiving the gift. rating 1.0. One-Day Delivery Didn't Happen This card was billed as one-day delivery.  It arrived in our city within the specified time, but was delivered at least 22 hours after that.  I normally don't choose rush delivery, but we wanted to include the gift card in a package.  Because we tried to wait for the card, we had to pay very pricey delivery charges on the main package -- and we still have to mail the gift card.  I know we could have done it direct but we were trying to surprise someone.  Lesson learned.

input: rating 1.0. Amazon Gift Card Purchase Leaves A Lot to be Desired I read several reviews regarding Amazon Gift Cards, but decided to purchase one anyway.  I wanted to make sure the gift card had a balance before I gave it as a gift, but obtaining balance information was like pulling teeth.  Amazon Chat was absolutely worthless and I only received canned responses to my questions, so I decided to call Amazon Customer Service.  The customer service representative was only a tad bit more helpful than Amazon Chat, and it took the representative over ten minutes to verify that the gift card I purchased had the correct amount loaded onto it.  Listen up Amazon:  There should be a way for customers to confirm that the gift card purchased actually has the correct amount loaded onto it without calling customer service.

rating 2.0. Don't order multiple card amounts - doesn't tell you which one it is While the cards were delivered with no dents like others stated, however, they don't tell you what the card value is and you have to sort through it yourself.
rating 5.0. It's a gift card,  it works.  :) What can I say?  It's a GC,  arrived on time, not broken :)  and the kids love it.

rating 3.0. Come on, ship it better Okay, amazon... I appreciate that I can buy these at low denominations and without additional fees. Therefore, I get that these are flimsy, easy to make cards subject to bending. What I don't get is why you would send a bunch of flimsy cardboard cards without a backer board in the shipping envelope? I ordered four of these gift cards as Christmas presents, and every single one of them has a visible (ugly) crease in them. I am glad they are only going to close family who I know will be able to laugh at amazon's failures because this would be embarrassing otherwise.

I also am very glad that I ordered different designs for each person, and the same dollar amount for the one duplicate I had....otherwise, it would have been a nightmare trying to figure out which card had which dollar value. I did read in the description that there is no dollar amount on the card itself, and can understand this being a possible good thing when gifting, but there must be something you can do for gifter so they can tell which is which!

I can only hope that they are redeemable with no problems once the recipients get them. (I will update my review accordingly.)

But for now, if nothing else, definitely, DEFINITELY send them with a backer board!! (or in a cardboard envelope)

rating 1.0. Don't get Amazon gift cards!! We got a bunch of cards in various monetary value for Christmas gifts. Never ever ever ever again. They all came and none were labelled with the value, so we didn't know which was which. We loved it up on Amazon and it said to look under "orders" to find the serial number for each card and then match it up with the cars to figure it the value. Annoying, but ok, we could do that. Except there were no serial numbers on the website. Or on the app. Or on the receipt from the box. Or anywhere else we looked. We even tried scanning the cards with the app - nope. So I had to call Amazon and wait on hold to talk with someone. That person then couldn't figure out the issue no matter how many ways I explained it, so after quite a while I agreed to talk with a supervisor. On hold again... Finally I got the value of the cards. I had to read off the entire 16 digit serial number of each card to the supervisor in order to get it. She confirmed that there was no other way to have done it.
I hope the people on our list enjoy their Amazon cards this year because they will never be getting another one!

There's no way to pre-load these gift cards to your account, so we had to make sure the gift cards were always with us. We usually buy our tickets on Fandango through our phone while sitting at dinner, or on the way to the theater, but if we had left the cards at home...too bad.

There's no way to scan the cards with your phone either. That means having to type in each card number (19 digits) then scratching to reveal the pin number to type in another 4. It's a pain when purchasing multiple tickets that cost more than a single $15 dollar card so you're there typing, scratching, typing, typing, scratching, typing from several cards like a weirdo.

rating 5.0. Purchased 20 of these in last 6 months..ZERO problems I’ve read a lot of 1 Star Reviews on this saying they couldn’t activate that card. I’ve ordered approximately 20 of these in the last six months and I never once had a problem. They’re always delivered within two days and I’ve had no issues at all. To use it as a debit card- the pin number is the last four digits of the card number.
Otherwise it functions exactly like a credit card. And the phone number to check your balance is simple, quick and very accurate.
The only thing I wish is that there were more styles of card or they came and gift boxes like the Amazon and Whole Foods ones do. It would be great if there were Trader Joe’s gift cards available on Amazon… But, until then I’ll just keep using these excellent Visa cards.

rating 3.0. Handed to resident??!! I don't think so! Three Amazon gift cards that are to be given to my employees as Christmas gifts. No big deal. Except that I had them shipped to my house on a Saturday knowing that someone would be at home when the package would arrive. I did not see anything show up that day, I had been checking the front door step and mailbox until 6pm (after dark, of course). This morning (the next day) I decided to check to see the status of the package, thinking that it was going to be a late arrival. Order status says that it was delivered on Saturday, and then goes on to say that it was handed to resident. That is just a flat out lie! it was lying on the porch in front of the door (fortunately!). But the doorbell had not been rung, and it certainly wasn't handed to anyone in my house. If you made the delivery and left it on the doorstep, fine, just say that. But don't lie about handing it to someone in the house. That is really irritating. I'm thankful it wasn't stolen. It was small enough that it would not have been noticed from the road, especially after dark.

input: rating 5.0. AVOID FRUSTRATION AND READ THIS I buy these cards all the time. They help me with spending issues I've had in the past.  They work wonderfully. However, based on a lot of the reviews I've read, it's important you read the info pamphlet so you clearly understand how to go about using it.  This will avoid many of the frustrations I read about in other reviews.  First, this card can not be used to retrieve cash.  Second, if you plan on using it for online purchases you need to register it in your name which will link it to your zip code. When merchants run CC #'s, the 4 main things that need to match with the card are name, card #, zip code and most of the time the CVV # on the back of the card. As long as you registered it correctly any online purchase will always go through assuming there's enough $ on the card.  Third, every retailers CC machines operate differently. So, it's important to ask the cashier if their machine automatically subtracts the amount available on the card from your purchase balance or if you need to tell them the exact amount that's on the card so they can manually punch that in before you swipe the card.  Asking this will avoid the card from getting declined. If your purchase balance is less then what's available on the card, then you don't need to ask. Also, there's been a handful of times where mine got declined even though I knew I had plenty of funds. I just re-ran the card as debit instead of credit and it works.  If you haven't registered your card and need to run it as debit, the pin is the last 4 digits on the card.  Fourth, if you purchase items that may hv a chance of being returned, try very hard to keep that card on hand even if it's all used up.  Most retailers insist they reimburse your $ bk onto its original form of payment or you'll end up with store credit. There's been many times I went to return and threw out my card already and had to basically make a scene to get cash back.  Lastly, never ever ever pay at the pump when getting gas w these types of prepaid cards.  There's extra security set up on the CC machine at gas pumps. It knows its a prepaid card, so it will hold all the funds available on your card for probably 7 days. Any retailer that accepts CC has to batch out their machine before any of those funds are deposited into their bank account. This is why they hold the funds.  And honestly for whatever reason that I don't understand it seems that these cards can't be used to pay bills online or over the phone. But, that's no fault of the card. That will happen no matter what prepaid card you use.  I hope this helps as you scroll along other reviews regarding their troubles they experienced. I believe if you are aware of this then it will help avoid any frustrations and help you determine if this is the right type of card depending on your needs.

rating 1.0. Cash stolen moments after registering Received this $200 gift card and followed the instructions to register before using. Help phone line is a robo-call center that will not let you talk with a person.

If you happen to receive one of these cards, spend it immediately (within minutes). Criminals within the business will have your card information too.

rating 1.0. Terrible product, difficult to use, no customer support You're probably thinking the prepaid Mastercard (MetaBank) is the same as the prepaid Visa (Sunrise), right?  Nope.  The prepaid MC (MetaBank) is barely supported by MasterCard.  My card would not work, so I called the number on the back on the card.  They have no live support.  Then I called MasterCard directly and was disconnected multiple times.  After 3-4 attempts I was able to speak to a live rep who transferred me to multiple outside lines.  It was a nightmare.  Finally I got a young, unprofessional person, presumably working out of a basement in Utah, who informed me that I couldn't send money across PayPal using this card.  Why on Earth not?!?  It's my money and my card.  Why can't I send money over PayPal?  I had to request an invoice just to transfer funds with the card.  I had previously used this card and the Visa sold on Amazon several times.  I will never, ever use this card again though.  It took several phone calls and hours of my time to finally use this card.  They're supposed to be activated at the time of purchase, yet this card was blocked and flagged, before I could finally use it.  Don't even think about trying to use it at a gas station or store.  Avoid MetaBank and the Blackhawk Network if possible.

rating 5.0. So cute! This is a great gift with a reusable little tin as a bonus.
The gift card is standard credit card size.
The colors of the tin and card are true to the product photos, and I was happy with them.
The bottom of the tin has a rectangular hole (so the barcode shows) covered in permanent plastic.
The tin only has a tiny bit of wording on it, (Amazon gift cards Made In China) which you can see in the photo.
The faux-velvet plastic card holder inside has semi-circle notches on each side of the card, so it's simple to remove the card. This plastic part is also easy so remove from the tin--and there's no adhesive holding it in place, so the tin is clean.
And, of course, the gift card recipient can buy almost anything with the gift card--since Amazon sells almost everything!

I immediately jumped online to look at reviews for help....If I hadn't read the reviews I would not have known you have to go online and register each card (Include your name, email and address ~ creating a pin is optional). I gave 4 stars because Amazon should note in their description that card can only be activated for use by registering it online.

current_product_reviews: rating 5.0. well received by coworkers because there's a Wendy's close to the office Wendy's $25. Gift Card:  It was a great gift for coworkers because there's a Wendy's close to the office. Recipients reported no problems using the cards. As with any gift cards, I recommended placing a discrete identifying mark on them, so that a cashier can't (accidentally) claim that you have a $0 balance and then hand you back a different card that REALLY is empty. This is especially true for drive throughs when the card you present is sometimes out of your sight.

rating 4.0. Sometimes order works, sometimes it doesn't... I ordered this gift card once, successfully verified the balance online, registered it online, was able to use it with no problems for my online shopping and at local swipe payments.  I thought "great!  So easy!  Let's do that again."  So next time I ordered two gift cards.  This time--after they shipped but before they had arrived, I got an email from Amazon warning that there was a problem activating the card, and that they were sending out a replacement.  When I got the original two, I checked the online balance, and sure enough neither of them were valid.  So I waited for the replacement order.  When it arrived--there was only one replacement card, not two!  The one worked.  But then I had to go through customer service chat at Amazon (took awhile) and get it sorted out for them to give me a credit for the second card.  That was a bit of a hassle.

rating 5.0. All good in this end I don't always like using my own CC when I road trip for the weekend, which I do with some frequency. I'll use GCs so I don't have to carry cash, as a way to maintain a budget and as a means of decreasing any opportunities of having my info compromised. I had absolutely no issue activating or using, just have to take the time to activate and set up a PIN which was all too easy a process. I already know gift cards tend not to to work at the pump at most gas stations so no prob going inside so no issues there. I used this particular card to pick up groceries and to pay for my camping spot.

I do think the activation is a bit higher than necessary, not like it involves anything tangible. I guess they have to make their money some way and it's an upfront detail.

Overall my experiences have been good ones. I won't dismiss those who've had nightmare experiences because I'm sure stuff like that happens. I've been fortunate in that respect, knock on wood. Cheers.

After going back and forth Visa said that Amazon can only activate the card and then Amazon's customer service claimed they cannot activate the cards. After multiple hours on the phone, Amazon finally canceled both cards and refunded me through with an "Amazon gift card" linked to my account instead of refunding me through my original payment method (credit card) which was better than nothing at the time. Two weeks later Amazon re-ordered the Visa Gift cards and charged my credit card without my approval!! I had to call and tell them I didn't know why or how they re-ordered these Visa Gift cards for me and I demanded that they refund my money onto my credit card that they used without my consent and they finally did. Amazon and Visa made it very clear that their customer services don't work together to solve basic issues, it's disappointing as there was no direction and not even managers knew how to deal with their product or services and were not willing to contact each other. This was one of the worst experiences I have ever had with Visa and Amazons customer service. They need to figure out how to reactivate Visa Gift cards and how to work together as two businesses :(!!

From my understanding, the instances where they are unactivated are simply if the person packaging and shipping them is unaware of such or does not scan all of them. If possible, I would recommend purchasing in store instead.

rating 1.0. DO *NOT* BUY THIS BRAND, read why. I bought this as a gift (duh) for a friend. He opened and could not use it, so he contacted me. I investigated and found this damn card needs to be activated online by registering at a website. That is absurd! The card should be ready to go immediately after Amazon activates it.

All right so I went ahead and showed him how to activate it and he tried to purchase an item on Amazon and they flagged the purchase as "suspect" and froze his account. It took him almost a week to get this issue resolved. I felt absolutely horrible and the lesson learned, never to buy this brand again.

rating 1.0. Got completely scammed Quite embarrassing to gift one of these cards to someone who goes on to get it declined by the cashier. You look up the card # online and "We are unable to process your request at this time. If you continue to receive this error, please contact Cardholder services by calling the number on the back of your card". OK! Did just that. Called the number. You enter all the information.. did this four times, only for them to drop the call after three minutes flat. :(

Ordered a same day rush delivery from a VISA Giftcard to make it up to him - and there were 0 problems. Shameless business tactics. Apparently MC wants you to "activate" these, yet intentionally makes it so you cannot do so - by internet nor phone. So, essentially a scam from a major credit card company where you have no recourse, waste $25-50 (depending on your option) then embarrass the recipient. Do yourself a favor and either give good ol' CA$H or a VISA gift card.

Hope others heed this warning this coming Christmas season!! :D

rating 5.0. Again...seriously??? I feel like Amazon should limit what can have a product review. I mean seriously, it's a gift card. I guess if you received it and got a paper cut, you may want to warn people that there is that potential hazard. Or maybe you didn't get the correct design, to which I say again, it's a gift card. You're not getting married to it. You'll most likely toss it when you're done with it. I guess there is the chance that the card is not active when you receive it, but even still, that's not the cards fault, it is the card activator person's fault..we should be able to review them and their performance...'my card's activation seemed to be a little slow, even faulty at times. Maybe the card activator person needs more training.' For the record, my card worked, no paper cut, and the design was perfect!

rating 5.0. Great stocking stuffers! These Fandango Gift Cards make the perfect gift for friends and family.  The 3 pack is very convenient if you need a gift for multiple people.  We used ours as stocking stuffers.  My kids were really happy to receive them.  Please note that you have to use these online at Fandango and you cannot just go into the theater with them and expect to use them at the box office.  They are Fandango gift cards after all.  There is a service fee at Fandango, but if you or your giftee use Fandango already you wouldn't be deterred by that.  The cards came in the mail really fast, faster than if you were to order them from Fandango's website.

rating 3.0. Okay These are okay, you get $45 for Fandango, which I bought as a Deal of the Day so it was a little less expensive, but as far as ease of use they are not good.

There's no way to pre-load these gift cards to your account, so we had to make sure the gift cards were always with us. We usually buy our tickets on Fandango through our phone while sitting at dinner, or on the way to the theater, but if we had left the cards at home...too bad.

There's no way to scan the cards with your phone either. That means having to type in each card number (19 digits) then scratching to reveal the pin number to type in another 4. It's a pain when purchasing multiple tickets that cost more than a single $15 dollar card so you're there typing, scratching, typing, typing, scratching, typing from several cards like a weirdo.

rating 3.0. Handed to resident??!! I don't think so! Three Amazon gift cards that are to be given to my employees as Christmas gifts. No big deal. Except that I had them shipped to my house on a Saturday knowing that someone would be at home when the package would arrive. I did not see anything show up that day, I had been checking the front door step and mailbox until 6pm (after dark, of course). This morning (the next day) I decided to check to see the status of the package, thinking that it was going to be a late arrival. Order status says that it was delivered on Saturday, and then goes on to say that it was handed to resident. That is just a flat out lie! it was lying on the porch in front of the door (fortunately!). But the doorbell had not been rung, and it certainly wasn't handed to anyone in my house. If you made the delivery and left it on the doorstep, fine, just say that. But don't lie about handing it to someone in the house. That is really irritating. I'm thankful it wasn't stolen. It was small enough that it would not have been noticed from the road, especially after dark.

rating 3.0. I never thought I'd write a review of a gift card I never thought I'd write a review of a gift card but I was very disappointed by this. I ordered two for $25 each. The tin is just as the photos show. The gift card itself is just a flimsy little piece of paper and the edges of it were peeling up. It's situated in a little fuzzy insert in the tin so it doesn't move around, but it's too tight and hard to pull out. It had no amount written on it, but since it is just a piece of thick paper I did write the amount on it as well as a "to" and "from" right on the paper card. There is a little square of paper in with the tin that has the instructions on how to use it, but that little square of paper also does not fit into the tin (it's not like it's complicated instructions, so it's not even necessary to include that, but why not just make it the proper size to fit in the tin, guys? It's fine enough, and the most important part of course is that it works (I hope it works) but next time I'll just go to any store that sells Amazon gift cards on a rack and buy one that is actually a plastic card.

rating 5.0. Taco Bell Gift Cards make a satisfying gift! What can I say, you can't go wrong with gift cards for occasions and this gift card is a favorite of college kids!

I gave this gift card as a gift and was really pleased with it. It is just a plain gift card but trust me, your recipient will love it! When I gave this card to my son, he was delighted. You know how many meals he can get especially if he eats off the dollar menu?! Taco Bell has a really nice dollar menu and the food is filling. Two burritos and a drink will cost you three dollars and will make a pretty filling and satisfying lunch!

All in all, he LOVED this gift card. There was no guessing the right size, getting the wrong item and even if he has a double of these, he'll still be happy! I highly recommend a Taco Bell card for anyone! Male, Female, old or young...it makes the perfect gift!

Disclaimer - I paid full price for this item and this is my honest and unbiased review. All opinions are my own based on personal experience.

rating 5.0. Won me over OK, this is less a review about the gift card (it worked!) and more about the store itself. I bought a couple of these as gifts, based on recommendations from some women who said that Ulta is more a store for, well, regular people. I was debating a gift card to Sephora but that was nixed since the price points on Sephora's products tend to be really quite high. Ulta, on the other hand, provided more approachable goods and thus the dollar will go further there. One woman told me that while the Sephora stuff was better, it didn't justify the price difference.

Of course, pure opinion and reasonable people can disagree. That said, I gave these out and was greeted with enthusiasm. For that reason I made the switch and will be getting these regularly as gifts. Thanks!

I registered the card as soon as we got it. It tells how in the pamphlet, it let me check the balance on the same website and it was$200 just like I had ordered. It also let me set up a 4 digit pin on the website. The card works fine but it does tell you where and how to use it. If you're using it to get gas pay inside not at the pump. You can't pay over the phone using this card, and it also states you can't use it for online orders, because it doesn't have your name on the front so it's not verifiable. Otherwise, we got gas today and paid inside it worked fine, used at a drive thru and worked fine so I have no complaints. It's good for things like that, as far as paying bills I don't think it will work. Also if you go to check out and you're wanting to use you're remaining balance along with another form of payment to cover the difference you must tell the cashier the exact amount you want to take off the card, or it will decline it. Overall, it's just what I needed and so far I've had no issues with it.

rating 5.0. AVOID FRUSTRATION AND READ THIS I buy these cards all the time. They help me with spending issues I've had in the past.  They work wonderfully. However, based on a lot of the reviews I've read, it's important you read the info pamphlet so you clearly understand how to go about using it.  This will avoid many of the frustrations I read about in other reviews.  First, this card can not be used to retrieve cash.  Second, if you plan on using it for online purchases you need to register it in your name which will link it to your zip code. When merchants run CC #'s, the 4 main things that need to match with the card are name, card #, zip code and most of the time the CVV # on the back of the card. As long as you registered it correctly any online purchase will always go through assuming there's enough $ on the card.  Third, every retailers CC machines operate differently. So, it's important to ask the cashier if their machine automatically subtracts the amount available on the card from your purchase balance or if you need to tell them the exact amount that's on the card so they can manually punch that in before you swipe the card.  Asking this will avoid the card from getting declined. If your purchase balance is less then what's available on the card, then you don't need to ask. Also, there's been a handful of times where mine got declined even though I knew I had plenty of funds. I just re-ran the card as debit instead of credit and it works.  If you haven't registered your card and need to run it as debit, the pin is the last 4 digits on the card.  Fourth, if you purchase items that may hv a chance of being returned, try very hard to keep that card on hand even if it's all used up.  Most retailers insist they reimburse your $ bk onto its original form of payment or you'll end up with store credit. There's been many times I went to return and threw out my card already and had to basically make a scene to get cash back.  Lastly, never ever ever pay at the pump when getting gas w these types of prepaid cards.  There's extra security set up on the CC machine at gas pumps. It knows its a prepaid card, so it will hold all the funds available on your card for probably 7 days. Any retailer that accepts CC has to batch out their machine before any of those funds are deposited into their bank account. This is why they hold the funds.  And honestly for whatever reason that I don't understand it seems that these cards can't be used to pay bills online or over the phone. But, that's no fault of the card. That will happen no matter what prepaid card you use.  I hope this helps as you scroll along other reviews regarding their troubles they experienced. I believe if you are aware of this then it will help avoid any frustrations and help you determine if this is the right type of card depending on your needs.

rating 1.0. Avoid Buying Gift Card -- Activation and use problems, plus difficulty getting refund. My dad activated the card, obtained PIN, and attempted to use it at gas station pump. Unable to use it - "not valid" message.

August 13 Contacted gift card company about problem and they advised card can only be used at attendant terminal.

On August 14 attempted to use card at CVS cashier for $8.36 purchase. Advised only $0.01 available.

Contacted gift card company and they sent email showing fraudulent transactions. My dad completed forms and sent back. Dad just contacted me asking for purchase receipt.

All of this was a major hassle and turned out to be a terrible gift.

Bottom line. Gift card company sells $100 card. Waits for activation. Then after complaint, they provide this type email showing multiple transactions that are not realistic but amounts to $99.99. They then require extensive paperwork and phone information (name, address, phone) and indicate it will be 60-90 days for them to investigate with retail merchants on the transactions. After they complete actions they will then either provide new card or re-activate existing.

rating 3.0. LATE DELIVERY! Hope you are not on a deadline when they say free overnight shipping included with this $100.00 gift card purchase because I do suspect it will be late like mine was! Ordered on Friday with Monday (notice I said free overnight shipping.) delivery date and on Tuesday when I decided to see why I had not received on Monday, I found out it was routed to the wrong delivery area (it went to New York) and there was no updated estimate delivery date available. If I had known the shipping would be an issue, I would have had it sent directly to the recipient of this gift as they live out of state. They will be visiting later this week and I really wanted to present it to them in person. I don't really think the "product" purchased was bad...can't hardly go wrong with a gift card. But, the advertised shipping was one of the main reasons I chose this particular gift. BIG disappointment right about now. :(

So today is Wednesday and as I write this it is 9:00 p.m. Still no gift card! Oh, and my bank account has already been drafted (on Monday - the original expected date of delivery) for the amount of the card I purchased! ($100.00) VERY BIG disappointment right about now!!! :( :(

Thursday and still waiting....

Okay, UPS delivered the item late Thursday evening - around 7:00 p.m. It was raining outside and the delivery man barely tapped twice on my door and dropped the package. He practically ran back to his truck. If I had not been there to receive it,or hear him even attempt to knock, who knows where it would have ended up at. (Currently my state is under a tropical storm warning with wind gusts of up to 65 mph, but no rain at the time of delivery.) All in all, I must really say that my experience is not so much with the Amazon gift card as it is with the delivery. UPS totally failed on this one and, the saddest part is that it reflects on Amazon. USPS would have been faster in this instance. They would have delivered in five days- not seven! Oh, one more thing, the tin was slightly bent but really barely noticeable. Besides, my son won't be spending the tin,

rating 1.0. GIFT CARD PROGRAM NEEDS A CHANGE TO GET MORE THAN ONE STAR FROM ME Last April TurboTax offered me the option of sending my $4500 refund to Amazon and I accepted the offer, not only because of the nice bonus, but it would give me a nice pot of money to place many orders with Amazon.  I have placed many orders but I have been extremely disappointed in your policy of not letting me purchase gift cards with my tax refund.  The main reason I sent you my tax refund was so I could purchase many gift cards for my relatives, friends, and neighbors.  I can understand not letting someone use a gift card that someone has given them to purchase a gift card for someone else but my gift card is not something someone has given me; IT IS MY OWN MONEY which I authorized the IRS to send to Amazon.  I think you should have a  different category for peoples own money and allow them to buy gift cards.  If I had been allowed to buy gift cards with my tax refund money, I would have introduced Amazon to at least 20 new customers this year; I know that is not many but I bet I am not the only one who sent you their tax refund.  I cannot give gift cards more than one star until you allow your customers to buy gift cards with their own money which they have already sent to Amazon.

input: rating 1.0. the promotional deal was impossible to apply to my Amazon account. When I received this deal to order a gift card,  with the promise of getting $10 credit to use on my account for every $50 purchased, I thought it was great.  First I did not get all the promotional credit that I was promised, or at least I don't know where Amazon placed them.  I received an email for part of the promotional deal to use, which I tried with no success.  When it came to applying the promotional credit onto my Amazon account, it was not possible.  I tried contacting customer service many times.  This was not successful. So essentially, the promotion does not even exist for me.  I am quite disappointed about this. rating 1.0. Serious issues with redemption!!! Ordered this as a gift to my brother in law in the Army stationed in another state. It arrived on time, but when he tried to redeem the card it wouldn't work - it just kept saying something was wrong with the card. I called Amazon, waited on hold, talked to someone who said he saw no issues with the card or my order, and that I needed to have my brother in law call them. So, he called them, only to be treated as if I, or he, stole the card!!!! The card was never even physically in my presence. I ordered directly from Amazon and had it shipped directly to him. After several minutes of being treated like a lying thief, and being placed on hold several times, the woman on the line finally told him they would email him in 2 or 3 days and that she hoped all would turn out well for him. Um, what? I call again, wait on hold, they want the claim code. I call him, get the claim code, call Amazon again, wait on hold, and finally give them the claim code....and when they enter it, it says something is wrong with the card. Really? In the end, they applied it to their account directly anyway, so all is well, but I will never buy a gift card again! Extremely disappointed because this is my absolute favorite shopping site. I'm hoping this is a rare occurrence as I've never, ever had problems like this in all the years I've been using this site. Anyway, happy birthday guy - hope you had fun sitting on hold and being treated like a criminal!

rating 1.0. Do not trust "Free 1-Day Shipping" All the Amazon Gift Cards noted that they included Free 1-day shipping. I ordered two of them. rating 1.0. Buyer beware I buy from Amazon for the convenience, the good prices, and mostly the peace of mind offered should there be a problem with the order. This however does not extend to Gift Cards. I purchased many of these over time, and most times it went without any problem. However, when there is a problem, Amazon's care-free customer-centric approach does not apply. Here is a specific example: I received an envelop with just the purchase receipt and no gift card. I called immediately Customer Service and they told me they couldn't resolve this issue since the Gift Card was already used. After a very long and frustrating hour-long conversation I was offered the option to contact my bank and dispute the charge. I will try this and follow up on how this issue is resolved. At any rate, I will stop purchasing these Gift Cards from Amazon as I don't want to go through this again. I will instead, buy them from the supermarket (same price, same product, minus the convenience of free delivery)

So the payment fails, and Amazon does not let you cancel the order. All you can do is add a different payment method so then you get charged the full amount on your normal credit card and still have an unused gift card.

rating 1.0. I tried to use a visa gift card to reload ... I tried to use a visa gift card to reload my Amazon.com gift balance. The visa gift card had a balance of exactly $100. If you ordered a $100 amazon gift card you got an extra 5-10 dollars. Plan was to use the visa card to buy an amazon card and get the extra money.

1st try the card was rejected. No reason. I thought it was because it was a visa gift card so I spent a considerable amount of time trying to figure out if there was something wrong with my card. Tried again.

2nd try was also rejected. These aren't instant rejections.... they are card denied rejections that happen 5-10 minutes after the order happens. Again no reason as to why this happened.

Called customer service. The advice there wasn't helpful and they didn't really know what was going on. What I eventually figured out was that each time I put an order in, it was charging a $1 authorisation fee to the card. Then the card has a $99 balance instead of $100 and the transaction is rejected. Probably not news to most people but its not common knowledge, after all Amazon Customer Service didn't know what was up...

rating 5.0. Solution Haha, this is funny that I get to review my Amazon Gift Card. So this is my re-loadable gift card for all my Amazon purchases. I don't know about anyone else but I hate all the little charges that come up on my account for Amazon. It is nice that they don't charge till it ships but each product coming out one by one by one! Sometimes it is confusing, because I remember the full purchase of everything in my order and not the individual prices. So I have to go back and look and see, I have to be sure someone didn't get a hold of my card again, it has happened in the past!

So here is a solution I found. Just put one charge on my card for a gift card. It gets loaded right to my account and I can reload it when I need to. So no more little charges here and there adding up on my statement. Easy. So if that part of shopping with Amazon annoys you like it did me, here is a solution that will keep you shopping happy. I am just surprised that it is on my list of items to review!!! It tickles me to see it here, but hey maybe this will help someone else. :)

current_product_reviews:
rating 5.0. Great Feature!! I used this feature because I was offered a $5 bonus with my first $100 upload & it worked perfectly!! Within minutes, I had $105 in my account...Thanks amazon!!
If you're unsure what this feature is, It's basically buying an amazon gift card....After you add money to your account, when you go to pay for items in your cart, you'll be prompted to either use your gift card balance, or pay using another method...which is nice because you're not forced to use this first, or as a payment method every time...(great for accounts with multiple people)!! I think this is also a great feature because, at least for me, it helps me to control & easily keep track of what I spend so I don't go too crazy, especially around the holidays!! Would recommend trying this!!

rating 5.0. Great gifting flexibility with an included free birthday tin card holder These gift card tins are great. The birthday cupcake tin with a padded interior for the card is a nice little extra to affix to a birthday card and does not increase the cost of the gift card. Also, there is flexibility in gifting denomination as the giver can choose whatever amount they wish to gift; the only downside is that the actual physical gift card will not display the amount if you choose something other than the standard $10, $25, $50 denominations.
Couple the one-day shipping with the fact that these gift cards can be put toward just about anything the recipient could possibly want and these are a great option for a last minute gift or a gift for someone who is difficult to shop for.

current_product_reviews: rating 5.0. Wonderful Gift Card Tin and Card This is my second order of an Amazon gift card that came inside this lovely birthday cupcake tin.  In person it looks even more adorable than the photo on Amazon!  In my view this is for all age groups.  It is so sweet.  When the tin is opened, there sits the gift card which is in the same white and blue colors as the tin!  And the gift card card is pressed nicely inside on the bottom that has a black faux velvet-like lining. In other words, the card doesn't move around--it is custom-fitted inside the tin but easy to remove.  So that is another attractive feature.  The back of the gift card itself has the serial number on it.  And when the gift card is in the tin,  you can turn the tin over and there's a "see-through" area that shows the card's serial number. You do need to take the card out of the tin to peel off the claim code area.  What is so nice is that the recipient of this pretty gift card can use the tin again for whatever gift cards they might want to put in it.  Amazon gift cards are always a perfect gift, and when they are inside this lovely tin, the gift just becomes extra special.

rating 5.0. Free Tin Box with Gift card purchase Standard Amazon gift card in a metal tin with textured diamond plate pattern

Tin is reusable, but is not solid on both sizes as there is an rectangular window on back

Material is thin and easily bendable if you intentionally use force or throw it hard against the wall or ground

Redeeming card is easy. There's a peel off sticker (instead of scratcher), so no need to worry about scratching off the text of the code. If you use the amazon app you can redeem the amount in seconds: click the camera option and align the boxes with the text code.

Shipping is ridiculously fast. Can setup for next day delivery for no additional charge

You cannot add to the card, once it's applied to the account, the card is useless as a form of tender.  I personally have a tool to make guitar picks from cards like these, so I'll make a couple of guitar picks from this one and give them to the kids.  One of the kids loves to play his guitar.  The tin in which it arrives has a cutout with a plastic insert so that the warehouse workers know which card to select to fulfill the order, and that does not hinder my using the tin for other things.  I actually like to see what's inside the box, just like the Amazon staff I guess.

rating 1.0. Can't tell how much is on each identical looking card?? I am so upset with the Amazon gift cards. I had bought these before and they were nice. rating 5.0. The diamond plate tin is a nice, manly way to present the Amazon gift card We all know the Amazon gift card is a crowd pleasing gift. Truth be told though, I have always hoped that Amazon would somehow sweeten the pot to make their gift cards a better buy to purchase. This gift tin is just a way that has been done. The diamond plate design is cool and macho, like a workshop and it makes giving a gift card a bit nicer to present. I have bought a bunch of these. The minor downside is that only even denominations come with a labeled card inside (with the denomination), whereas the other choices for card value are not printed on the card.  Overall, these tins are nice additions to the buy-whatever-you-want Amazon gift card.

rating 5.0. Excellent card to use for online US-based subscriptions (Amazon Music, etc) even from abroad! I was worried about the negative reviews for this card where people say that the balance on the card was zero when they received it, but in my case no such issues.

What's great about this card is that you can use it to subscribe to US online services which require US card even when you're abroad. So for instance if you're travelling outside of the US and forgot all your US cards at home, you can order this card (via freight forwarder, as Amazon does not ship these cards abroad), and once you receive it use a VPN when registering it with Amazon Music etc. Worked for me.

rating 1.0. Would use this but NOT with CHASE Bank. I have had a Amazon/Chase Credit Card for years. The Chase Credit Card people are great! So to take advantage of this offer I opened a Checking Account at my Local Chase Branch and deposited 4 checks totaling about $8000. I even had one of my auto-deposits for $1800 switched to them. The manager seemed very nice until I called him a week later when my ATM card didn't work. He instantly said thee appears to be fraudulent activity regarding the checks, everyone I spoke with on the phone lied. The check is in the Mail, we will send you another check tomorrow, blah blah blah! So in may 2018, 8 months later and because I use to work for a bank which is covered under Federal Jurisdiction, I finally called the FBI. I said I'm sorry to bother you with this issue, but the FBI considered this a White Collar Crime and sent an Agent to my house and I had that check for $8000 in a week. And a week later $14,400 in auto deposits that were due me. The Chase Credit Card Division and the Banking Division  are two separate entities. The credit card people have been great, however I am starting not to use that card whenever possible as a matter of principle and there is NO REASON why I have to have a CHASE checking account to get an Amazon DEBIT Card.

rating 5.0. Teaser of What's to Come It's just a mini shipping box containing a gift card inside.

High quality cardboard. The front two flaps is held down via small magnets so it doesn't flap around or stay loose

Redeeming card is easy. There's a peel off sticker (instead of scratcher), so no need to worry about scratching off the text of the code. If you use the amazon app you can redeem the amount in seconds: click the camera option and align the boxes with the text code.

Very good hint of what's to come* once the card is redeemed.

*Unless you get a yellow mailing bag instead lol

Only drawback--you're locking the recipient into one ecosystem. But let's be honest, an Amazon gift card is almost like cash.

rating 5.0. THE CUTEST GIFT BOX EVER! I absolutely love this gift card/holder.  Everyone I've given one to thinks it is so unique and adorable.  You CAN see how much is on it without scratching off any numbers.  There is a clear window on the back of the box to see the numbers of the card.  The last digits will match up to your order and show you how much is on which card.  I thought the same as others at first thinking well this sucks, but then if you put your brain to it you can figure it out.  It is listed clearly in your order history.  I will buy this a million times over and wish I could order just the boxes to use for other gift cards!

rating 5.0. Great gift in a pinch!! Visa gift cards make great gifts because they are widely accepted. Just keep in mind that they are not designed to be converted into cash. I buy them all the time mostly $50 and $100.

current_product_reviews: rating 4.0. Easy to purchase on Amazon site Easy to buy and beautiful xmas box selected to come in. The only thing I did not like was that when I purchase something from Amazon account,  this is the first form of payment it wants to use. You have to be careful and edit your payment and switch to another if you do not want to use it. This was a gift for my niece so I have to be careful every time to switch to my other form of payment since it's linked to my account. Also beware if you rent or buy a movie from Prime or any other subscription linked to your account it will also take  from the gc first. My niece hadn't used the card yet so it deducted from her gift card. Which I had to reload it today. But the GC process was very easy to buy from my account and loved that I could easily get any denomination.

rating 5.0. Won me over OK, this is less a review about the gift card (it worked!) and more about the store itself. I bought a couple of these as gifts, based on recommendations from some women who said that Ulta is more a store for, well, regular people. I was debating a gift card to Sephora but that was nixed since the price points on Sephora's products tend to be really quite high. Ulta, on the other hand, provided more approachable goods and thus the dollar will go further there. One woman told me that while the Sephora stuff was better, it didn't justify the price difference.

Of course, pure opinion and reasonable people can disagree. That said, I gave these out and was greeted with enthusiasm. For that reason I made the switch and will be getting these regularly as gifts. Thanks!

rating 5.0. Excellent card to use for online US-based subscriptions (Amazon Music, etc) even from abroad! I was worried about the negative reviews for this card where people say that the balance on the card was zero when they received it, but in my case no such issues.

What's great about this card is that you can use it to subscribe to US online services which require US card even when you're abroad. So for instance if you're travelling outside of the US and forgot all your US cards at home, you can order this card (via freight forwarder, as Amazon does not ship these cards abroad), and once you receive it use a VPN when registering it with Amazon Music etc. Worked for me.

rating 5.0. Slide up to reveal card [[VIDEOID:2ef078ae9120aa06532c2d30d4018141]] Very inviting design. Slide up to reveal card.

Card is in it's own insert slot so won't get stuck while sliding

Card design is very generic, just the Amazon logo (probably because you can select your own amount)

On back there's a cutout for UPC, Serial number scanning code

Redeeming card is easy. There's a peel off sticker (instead of scratcher) on back of the card, no need to worry about scratching off the text of the code. If you use the amazon app you can redeem the amount in seconds: click the camera option and align the boxes with the text code.

Shipping is ridiculously fast. Can setup for next day delivery for no additional charge.

rating 5.0. Paying Amazon $100 at a time using the gift card balance; makes my checkbook more sane. what's not to love about reloading a gift card balance on my account? I can't even believe they put this on my 'to review' list.

But I will share my strategy... I load $100+ at a time on here because it keeps my checkbook balancing sane. I'm a Prime member, who lives in a rural area. I USE MY DELIVERY option, at least one order a week. Even if I order $60-100 at a time, they hit my account for each 'shipment' they process, so sometimes there can be 4-5 small charges for one larger order. It's crazy... load your own account with a larger amount at time to make your checkbook balancing a bit more sane. Dave Ramsey would approve. ;-)

rating 5.0. Thank You Amazon! UPDATE TO THIS REVIEW: I am raising my rating from 4 stars to 5 because I have found a way to not use my gift card balance when I don't want to use it. Yay! For those of you who feel the same about this as I do, you can save your gift card balance up and make a purchase using your other payment methods. Click on the blue link in checkout to change your payment method. When you scroll all the way down the page you will find a small sentence saying to use the gift card balance for this purchase. There is a check box next to it and all you have to do is uncheck that box. You can then save your gift card balance for a different purchase you may be planning and the full price can be charged to whichever payment method you choose.

I love the fact that I am able to reload my gift card balance. It's a good feature. However, it's kind of awkward to do it. And it's not instantaneous. It can take up to 15 minutes.

My son wanted me to buy him a headset on Amazon. He handed me cash. I logged in to place an order for him. But I didn't want to spend my own gift card balance. But Amazon would have used that first on my purchase. I wanted to keep that on my account. So, instead of just buying his headset, I went in and loaded the amount I would need to the balance. Great. Except there was a delay. So I had to wait and reload the page. Then wait some more and reload the page again until finally the balance was available and I ordered his item. I'm no tech expert, but shouldn't there be an easier way to simply say that I don't want to use my gift card balance on a purchase?

input: rating 1.0. Scam. Do not purchase Scam. Do not purchase. Everything came. Looked fine. Open package and registered it per instructions in booklet. Online after registering it, said it had the hundred dollar balance. Called the number on back of card just to double check everything worked, and it told me my balance was zero dollars. Went to listen to the transaction history and it told me that the card was used for exactly 100 dollars. Ok so I call to talk to the card customer service who told me the card was in fact used for exactly 100 dollars, to the cent. From a company in Florida. Mind you, I'm in north carolina and the card was only activated for 10 minutes. They could do nothing for me. Neither could amazon assocites, even though it was in fact a prime item. rating 5.0. this card will be just fine for you Not sure how so many people don’t know how a gift card works, but it’s activated upon delivery. To make payments to something online, you have to register it TO YOU. Any online purchase has to have a “bill-to” address, hence registering it under your name. Also, if you eat at a restaurant or for gas, or anything for an authorization, every single card will be ran for about 15%-20% of the card value. It’s not that hard. If your IQ is above 13, this card will be just fine for you.

I had to call and tell them I didn't know why or how they re-ordered these Visa Gift cards for me and I demanded that they refund my money onto my credit card that they used without my consent and they finally did. rating 5.0. A way to manage those pesky rebate cards we get in the mail This feature is one of my favorite things on Amazon. At first I had no clue why I would purchase a gift card on Amazon in my own account. Then I got one of those "rebate gift cards" from an item I purchased and had to figure out what to do with a Visa or MC that had only $20, $30, $40 on it. I certainly didn't want to carry it around or track how much I had spent and how much was left.

That is where the gift card balance reload feature on Amazon came to the rescue. I was able to register my rebate card with Amazon, purchase a gift card for the exact balance left on the card, wait for confirmation, and then toss the card, never to worry about it again. This is too easy!

Overall, anything that makes life just a bit simpler or more convenient is a plus. Amazon hit the nail on the head by allowing us to use gift cards, rebate cards and more to purchase Amazon "bucks" that we can use to actually get something we want. Thank you Amazon!

rating 5.0. Attractive and Thoughtful. I bought this for my brother and he WhatsApped me the photos as he went through the steps opening it. It was exactly as advertised, with the orange-ish bow and nestled quite lovely inside of the specialty gift box. If you are purchasing this gift card, do remember that they advertise there are no returns or refunds on gift cards.

Do enjoy your shopping and ability to choose from the millions of items available storewide at amazon.com. It is easier than ever now because we can scan to redeem the gift card with a mobile or tablet device using the Amazon App. I was also able to access the free one-day shipping as advertised, but they also specify that there are certain locations where residents would be able to take advantage of this.

I have read some reviews where customers said that they were not able to redeem the gift card, and believe me, I feel for them. When I made this purchase, I was silently praying that my code was not invalid or stolen as some of the customers indicated.

This is not only a thoughtful last minute gift, but the presentation is next to perfect because I do believe that the ribbon colour could have been a bit more festive. Nevertheless, thank you amazon.com.

rating 4.0. This does not act as a hotel key for some reason At first I assumed this was a a multifunction card used for credit as well as other typical card functions. I must have inserted the card into the face of my door half a dozen times before realizing Amazon had ripped me off.  I went to the hotel’s front desk, borderline hysterical, and said “There must have been some kind of mistake!”  My handler found me and told me everything was alright and it turns out that these actually aren’t used for opening hotel room doors.  It turns out the hotel gives you a free card every time you stay with them, but when you check out, it doesn’t work anymore.  However, I was able to buy products from Amazon using the $25 I had already spent on the card.  That made me feel better.  Thanks Amazon!

which was $26.50.

rating 5.0. All good in this end I don't always like using my own CC when I road trip for the weekend, which I do with some frequency. I'll use GCs so I don't have to carry cash, as a way to maintain a budget and as a means of decreasing any opportunities of having my info compromised. I had absolutely no issue activating or using, just have to take the time to activate and set up a PIN which was all too easy a process. I already know gift cards tend not to to work at the pump at most gas stations so no prob going inside so no issues there. I used this particular card to pick up groceries and to pay for my camping spot.

I do think the activation is a bit higher than necessary, not like it involves anything tangible. I guess they have to make their money some way and it's an upfront detail.

Overall my experiences have been good ones. I won't dismiss those who've had nightmare experiences because I'm sure stuff like that happens. I've been fortunate in that respect, knock on wood. Cheers.

"Your card is valid in the U.S. only...it cannot be used at merchants outside the United States, including internet and mail/telephone order merchants outside the United States..."

input: rating 1.0. Misleading offer! This offer did not have any clear instructions. I mistakenly believed that I had to accept this offer in order to keep my current gift card level active. After submitting to this agreement, I discovered that I was being charged the exact value of my current giftcards in order to double the amount. Fortunately I realized my error upon receiving an email confirmation. I immediately called Amazon customer service to resolve this issue and get that amount credited back to my credit card. The agent that I spoke to was wonderful and credited that amount back to me. However, I feel as though this was a misleading offer and I cannot help but to wonder how many other unsuspecting current gift card holders made the initial error that I did.

rating 4.0. Happy to have extra $5.00 in my gift card balance. As I was making a purchase thru Amazon, I was offered additional $5.00 to my gift card if I placed an order for $100.00 to my gift card balance and my remaining balance owed would be $107.00 on an item costing $112.00.  I had never done this and was not aware of how it worked.  I followed the blue links to read about it because I wanted to use all of my remaining gift card balance to make my purchase, not just the $5.00 being offered.  I read the reviews.  One review stated you can only use your gift card balance to make purchases.  Another stated they never received their additional $5.00 credit after reloading their gift card.  I went ahead and reloaded $100.00 on my gift card, and immediately received email that $100.00 was added to my gift card balance.  I was expecting it to say $105.00 was being added.  I went ahead and purchased my $112.00 item in full with my gift card balance and immediately received email notice that my additional $5.00 credit had been applied to my gift card balance.  I made another purchase today for another item on Amazon with the intention to use reward points on one of my credit cards already on file as one of my payment methods.  When I placed the order, the payment method had defaulted to use the remaining balance on my gift card balance.  I selected the blue link to change payment method to use my reward points on my credit card with any remaining balance to be paid with my credit card and removed the black dot on the radial button for my gift card balance by clicking on it and was able to save the remaining of my gift card balance with this purchase.  I reviewed everything in my order to make sure it was correct before submitting it.  I am giving a 4 star review because the system had defaulted to encourage me to use my gift card balance using $5.00 with my first order and I wanted to pay in full with my gift card balance since I had just made purchase of $100.00 to reload it and also because the second purchase I made defaulted to pay with my remaining gift card balance.  I had to make changes to suit me each time I placed my order.  Otherwise, I am grateful to have the extra $5.00 in my gift card balance.

current_product_reviews:
rating 5.0. Great gifting flexibility with an included free birthday tin card holder These gift card tins are great. The birthday cupcake tin with a padded interior for the card is a nice little extra to affix to a birthday card and does not increase the cost of the gift card. Also, there is flexibility in gifting denomination as the giver can choose whatever amount they wish to gift; the only downside is that the actual physical gift card will not display the amount if you choose something other than the standard $10, $25, $50 denominations.
Couple the one-day shipping with the fact that these gift cards can be put toward just about anything the recipient could possibly want and these are a great option for a last minute gift or a gift for someone who is difficult to shop for.
other_product_reviews: rating 5.0. Great to keep around for a variety of purposes This three pack of $25 gift cards is great. The pack comes really quickly and can be used on anything Amazon. We keep a few of these on hand for last minute gift add-ons (the $25 denomination allows for lots of gift giving flexibility). I even took one of the three and bought something for myself...you know, just to make sure it worked...It did ;)

After spending too much time trying to find the serial numbers listed through the app on my phone as well as the site through my phone’s browser, I finally pulled out my laptop, logged into the site, and found the info that way.
Hopefully this info won’t get lost in the other reviews. I get the sense that the Q and A section isn’t open for additions.

rating 4.0. If I hadn't read the reviews..... I ordered 2 of these $100 Mastercard Gift cards, I ordered last night and received them today. I realized there was nothing stating the cards were activated.  They were purchased as Christmas gifts and I wanted to make sure they were usable; as well as, ensure there were no issues once the recipients went to use.  I immediately jumped online to look at reviews for help....If I hadn't read the reviews I would not have known you have to go online and register each card (Include your name, email and address ~ creating a pin is optional). The card is activated once you register it and the available balance shows up. LOVE IT!!!! I thought I was going to have to argue with Amazon for a refund. I gave 4 stars because Amazon should note in their description that card can only be activated for use by registering it online.

rating 1.0. Do not buy it So here it is Christmas morning and I’ve been on the phone for an hour trying to get this card to work. Even going to the website stated on the back of the card doesn’t recognize the card. For all of the rude people assuming that those of us who have not had luck with the card working, have not followed directions... there aren’t any real directions to follow outside the normal “activating” process. When you can’t even register the card on the website stated on the back of the card, clearly there is an issue with these cards. I’ve purchased these gifts cards before and they are hit or miss. This is ridiculous that Amazon continues to sell these on their site knowing there are so many issues with it. I could have walked to the store 5 houses down and purchased a gift card, but instead purchased thru Amazon and now my son is sitting here on Christmas bummed out because he can’t buy a game for his new oculus quest 2. Shame on Amazon.
