rating 4.0. Bent and included extra card the gift card arrived bent... not too badly, but still not ideal for presentation. ALSO- I opened the shrink wrap around the card and found TWO gift cards enclosed. I didn't know if perhaps they split the balance among 2 cards, or if one was invalid. (Or perhaps BOTH were invalid). So I had to remove the security strip to get the claim code... to then check the balance of the cards. And indeed, one included the full balance, the other was blank. This was a problem I was able to solve. But had I not opened the shrink wrap, the gift recipient would have been confused, and would have had to do that legwork on her own… Again, it's not the end of the world. I did receive the product I ordered.
I have ordered many Amazon gift cards in the past, and will continue to do so. But this experience was not perfect.

rating 5.0. Excellent way to give a gift card! Absolutely perfect gift for Mothers Day, or any occasion where the recipient loves flowers. My mother is a HUGE flower nut (who's isn't?) so to go along with the theme of other flower related gifts, I had to get her an Amazon gift card as well so she could pick out something she's really wanted. This flower pot reveal is simply perfect, and she loved it!!

The only qualm I may see that others have noted is that there really isn't much writing space, so you might not want to plan on using this to present your message. It is a truly awesome way to give someone a gift card.

rating 1.0. Don't believe Amazon promotional ads in the emails I purchased these gift cards as part of a promotion Amazon was running. I will upload a photo of the email I received. In the email it stated "Get $15.00 Amazon credit with a $50 Amazon gift card purchase". It's the end of the school year, so I purchased 3 $50.00 gift card for my kids teachers. I figured that was a great deal for both of us, I spend $35.00 and they get $50.00 for their classroom.

Four days later I attempt to purchase a birthday present and was reminded to check on my $45.00 credit I thought I had coming. I couldn't find it, used all their "self help" links, and finally found a phone number to speak to a human and get help. First lady was wonderful, told me the reason I didn't get it was because I had purchased all three in one transaction, instead of 3. I thought that was silly so I asked for a supervisor.

The supervisor, Deo located in the Philippines, was the supervisor I was connected to. He informed me what the email really meant was ONE $15.00 credit for purchasing OVER $50.00 in gift cards. Clearly that email is open to interpretation. He said it was written in the terms and conditions...however when I tried to read them for myself I was sent to a different screen (picture attached). I asked to speak with his supervisor, to which he informed me he is the very top of the customer service center. I asked for his boss. He said he was only authorized to give me a mailing address. Dumbfounded I asked him if Amazon corporate office in Washington does not have phones or email. He said they are only allowed to contact them via their computer system.

Most of my conversation with Deo was in silence. I'm assuming he was typing up everything I was saying for amazon's records. 54 minutes into my conversation the line disconnected and they never called back. I called back to get amazon's address and was surprisingly connected to a customer service rep who worked in the same department as Deo. He spoke with Deo (who was unable to speak with me since he was busy with another customer). The new customer service rep, Ian, could not have been kinder and more professional. I felt like he was actually listening to my complaint verse Deo who just wanted to record every little thing I said.

Ian helped me get the contact information I needed, and also gave me the email address of where I could forward a copy of the email in question. Ian also provided me with the PO Box for Amazon Corporate Office in Seattle. Since I can not voice my compliant to Amazon corporate office directly, and I must write a letter, I figured I would share it with all who would listen. I hope your Amazon experience is better than mine. And Deo, you might want to pay attention to Ian and Mercy - they are customer service pros.

Long story short, Amazon - who is worth $430 BILLION - would rather lose a long time customer over $30. But as for the product, it was adorable and looked great in my candy bouquets. I'll attach a picture of that too.

***UPDATE*** After I emailed Amazon as part of a survey they sent, I received the following response:
Hello,

I understand you're concerned about $15 credit for purchasing $50 gift cards on our website.

I can completely understand how frustrating and annoying the situation can be when something like this happens but believe us it was never our intention.

After reviewing your previous correspondences with our customer service, I can confirm the information provided to you was correct. As my colleague mentioned, the promotional credit will be provided only one per customer.

You can view the restrictions for promotion code here:

http://www.amazon.com/promos

I realize this is frustrating; however we aren't able to offer any additional insight or action on this matter and any further inquiries regarding this issue won't receive a response.

We appreciate your understanding.

Best regards,
Madhan P.
Amazon.com

I did go to that link, and there is nothing that addresses my concern or complaint. I wrote back asking them to highlight the part that actually has to do with my complaint. Don't worry, I'll update this if they ever respond.

rating 1.0. These cards are blank!! Don’t gift.. You’ll look like a fool! I ordered six of these cards for my daughters teachers for teachers appreciation week. They arrived on time but apparently they were not activated by Amazon!
Just imagine you gift this card to somebody and when they try to redeem it states the balance on this is zero dollars! How embarrassing!!
I’m glad I checked with one of the teachers to see if it worked because it was a large amount $250 contributed by all the students’ parents and she said that the card was invalid!! The other teachers might have even found it awkward to tell us that the card was blank. It was a gift after all !
I called amazon customer service and ask them to activate these cards. They told me that they need to have the teachers call them directly with claim codes so they can apply the balance to their individual accounts.
Amazon was not able to activate them on their end without each person I gifted it to calling them. It was really a bad experience - first by giving somebody a gift card that was blank and then having them deal with the customer service themselves to get the money they were gifted. I am really really embarrassed and extremely unhappy with the experience. please please please do not make a fool of yourself by getting these!!

rating 5.0. Easy, convenient, and a great way to budget your purchases! I love Amazon! I tend to always look here first before I purchase anything. Not just because Amazon has just about anything, but they have all the colors and the options that I could ever possibly want, including ones that I didn't even know I wanted until I saw them. In addition, I love being able to read through the customer reviews. I have been swayed away from items that I thought I wanted because of the reviews, and have instead purchased a similar item based on good reviews. I have had very good experiences by following those guidelines. I never have to fear when ordering through Amazon. As a prime member, I know when my items will arrive, I know that they will arrive on time, I know that they will be packaged well, and based on my reading through reviews? I pretty much know that I will enjoy the item that I receive.

There are so many reasons to purchase an Amazon gift card and to keep the balance loaded. It is so much easier to budget for your purchases, transfer gift card balances, or simply keep track of your Amazon spending.

In addition, they almost always have specials that they run if you load a certain amount. Since I frequently make purchases (including monthly subscriptions), this is the best way to keep my statements from being cluttered with several small Amazon purchases each week. Instead, there is a withdrawal for the gift card purchase, and then all the smaller purchases just come out of that.

Amazon also makes it so easy to do!

current_product_reviews: rating 4.0. Pay attention if you purchase with an incentive I like having a gift card balance from which payment is made for small ticket items and tend to uncheck the box for applying a gift card balance for purchases where a credit card payment is more beneficial, as when an extended warranty is offered.  Since this has no expiration date, I find that it allows me to save time when balancing my credit card statements at the end of the month, particularly since I can check my gift card balance and see exactly when and for what amount the payment was made.  When Amazon offers an incentive to reload is when I typically make my purchases, and although it has not applied the additional amount instantaneously, I have found that after a phone call, it miraculously tends to appear.  For this reason, I have to deduct a star from my review.

rating 1.0. Absolutely disgusted with Amazon gift card service Absolutely disgusted with Amazon gift card service. I advise everyone to NEVER buy any gift card here!

We had one typo trying to redeem the gift card and now the money is apparently gone forever. Have spent days with customer service being promised by multiple representatives that they will help, but none will. Have received several emails all saying the issue has been resolved, it has not been resolved. Received an email saying my account has been refunded, it hasn't. After untold hours trying to get a simple balance transfer, I gave up and tried to purchase a replacement e-card. Found out that I can't purchase an e-card from my bank account - that's against Amazon policy too. E-cards must be purchased from a credit card even though the original gift card was purchased from my bank account.

At this point I am convinced that the only relevant policy Amazon has is that they will never forgive anyone for a typo and will make things as difficult as possible until I just give up and go away.

rating 1.0. Terminate the packer !!! I love Amazon goods & services...however, the packaging was the worst I have experienced for any on-line retailer and I purchase a lot on line. Worse yet, Amazon shipped via USPS and I got the lazy driver who simply dropped the cardboard package at the curb. The cardboard was NOT sealed at either end and the contents were exposed, hanging out of the package.
I now went to the original site and read the numerous reviews. All customers complained about the terrible packaging and many experienced more severe problems with the contents being crushed, etc. Amazon apparently does not read customer reviews because they continue to use an inappropriate package. All Amazon has to do to correct the poor packaging is to add package tape to both ends of the package. Next year I will buy the gift cards at my local church...no packaging required.
The rating reflects the packaging, not the item.

rating 4.0. Elegant (albeit generic) winter holiday gift card tin Even though this gift-card-in-a-tin is designed to be a generic holiday gift, I purchased it as a birthday gift for a friend with a winter birthday because none of the Amazon birthday designs I saw appealed to me and because I liked the idea of a reusable gift card container. (I may be wrong, but I don't believe any of Amazon's birthday gift cards are packaged in tins.) The design of this one is obviously intended to be suitable for any winter holiday -- Christmas, New Year, Hanukkah (note that this snowflake has six points, like the Star of David, and is blue, the official Hanukkah color), Kwanzaa, Festivus, etc. This generic design worked in my favor because I intended to use it for a birthday gift, but if I had been shopping for a holiday gift, I would have preferred a design specific to that particular holiday.

I generally avoid giving people gift cards because I find them impersonal (as well as for other practical reasons that I won't go into here), but when I do, I prefer to present them in gift containers instead of just slipping them into cards for a couple of reasons: a package make the gift card a bit less guessable and reduces the chance that the gift card will be overlooked or discarded by mistake, as can happen when it's enclosed in a card. However, I also try to be environmentally responsible and choose packaging, like this tin, that can be re-used over and over, in place of wrapping paper that is often thrown out after a single use.

One selling feature for this Amazon card was that at the time I bought it, it came with free one-day shipping. I'm a Prime member, so I would have gotten "free" 2-day shipping anyway, but as a chronic procrastinator, every little bit helps.

Overall, I was pleased with this gift card/gift tin and thought it looked rather classy, as far as gift card boxes go, and it's likely that I'll purchase another (or similarly packaged gift card) next year.

If I got the $10 I was never notified that it was applied to my account and I was never able to use the code Amazon supplied me. Amazon gets a lot of my money but they are very difficult to get in touch with. I don't call because I don't hear well and otis very frustrating to me so I click on "email" and that won't even load. This thing expires in 2 more days, although my money they get never expires, go figure. The following is an excerpt from their email when I made the purchase.
"Your recent order 102-2334848-2538632 entitles you to a promotional credit which we have added to your account. This credit can be applied to your next qualifying purchase.".
This says it CAN be applied, NOT WILL be applied, therefore if it was applied, I don't know about it or remember it and there is no way to research that.

2) They come in a plastic covering with some instructions on how to redeem, but why would you give someone this cute gift card in this ugly cellophane bag instead of presenting it in the cute box the card is in? rating 5.0. 360° PERFECTION ® Great for buying exactly what you want. I use these when purchasing online. Security issues are a thing of the past and you can keep your personal accounts away from theives and it's just as simple to use as your debit or credit card. You can register your card online and it will give you a list of your transaction history along with your available balance after each purchase. I've bought several of these off Amazon and have never had any issues or time consuming complications. Don't want to register your card? That is ok too! Run it as credit instead and of debit, no pin required. It's literally ready to use as soon as it arrives and Amazon always does a great job delivering on time sometimes even earlier than expected which is a plus. You will get notified the day before a time window to expect your package along with live detailed tracking  information and will send you a picture of your package excatly where you specify Amazon to leave it. Amazon is the best.

rating 4.0. Sometimes order works, sometimes it doesn't... I ordered this gift card once, successfully verified the balance online, registered it online, was able to use it with no problems for my online shopping and at local swipe payments.  I thought "great!  So easy!  Let's do that again."  So next time I ordered two gift cards.  This time--after they shipped but before they had arrived, I got an email from Amazon warning that there was a problem activating the card, and that they were sending out a replacement.  When I got the original two, I checked the online balance, and sure enough neither of them were valid.  So I waited for the replacement order.  When it arrived--there was only one replacement card, not two!  The one worked.  But then I had to go through customer service chat at Amazon (took awhile) and get it sorted out for them to give me a credit for the second card.  That was a bit of a hassle.

current_product_reviews: rating 5.0. well received & put to good use Kohl's $50. Gift Card:  It was a great gift for a coworker because there are multiple Kohl's nearby. Recipient reported no problems using the card. As with any gift cards, I recommended placing a discrete identifying mark on them, so that a cashier can't (accidentally) claim that you have a $0 balance and then hand you back a different card that REALLY is empty. Keep them in sight for the entire transaction if at all possible!

Perfect -- or so I thought.  When I got it, my heart sunk.  The Gift Card had no denomination printed on it.  There was no envelope enclosed and worst of all, there was very little room to write any message.  (It has the same design both front and back.) rating 5.0. Very cute way to package a gift card! These cards were very cute! I bought three for my sons' teachers for Teacher Appreciation Week and they loved them! rating 4.0. Bent and included extra card the gift card arrived bent... not too badly, but still not ideal for presentation. ALSO- I opened the shrink wrap around the card and found TWO gift cards enclosed. I didn't know if perhaps they split the balance among 2 cards, or if one was invalid. (Or perhaps BOTH were invalid). So I had to remove the security strip to get the claim code... to then check the balance of the cards. And indeed, one included the full balance, the other was blank. This was a problem I was able to solve. But had I not opened the shrink wrap, the gift recipient would have been confused, and would have had to do that legwork on her own… Again, it's not the end of the world. I did receive the product I ordered.
I have ordered many Amazon gift cards in the past, and will continue to do so. But this experience was not perfect.
rating 1.0. DISAPPOINTED Very annoyed that the gift card wasn't included in the package.  I finally had to call the recipient to make sure she received it.? Very sloppy work indeed!!!

rating 5.0. Excellent way to give a gift card! Absolutely perfect gift for Mothers Day, or any occasion where the recipient loves flowers. My mother is a HUGE flower nut (who's isn't?) so to go along with the theme of other flower related gifts, I had to get her an Amazon gift card as well so she could pick out something she's really wanted. This flower pot reveal is simply perfect, and she loved it!!

The only qualm I may see that others have noted is that there really isn't much writing space, so you might not want to plan on using this to present your message. It is a truly awesome way to give someone a gift card.

rating 1.0. Don't believe Amazon promotional ads in the emails I purchased these gift cards as part of a promotion Amazon was running. I will upload a photo of the email I received. In the email it stated "Get $15.00 Amazon credit with a $50 Amazon gift card purchase". It's the end of the school year, so I purchased 3 $50.00 gift card for my kids teachers. I figured that was a great deal for both of us, I spend $35.00 and they get $50.00 for their classroom.

Four days later I attempt to purchase a birthday present and was reminded to check on my $45.00 credit I thought I had coming. I couldn't find it, used all their "self help" links, and finally found a phone number to speak to a human and get help. First lady was wonderful, told me the reason I didn't get it was because I had purchased all three in one transaction, instead of 3. I thought that was silly so I asked for a supervisor.

The supervisor, Deo located in the Philippines, was the supervisor I was connected to. He informed me what the email really meant was ONE $15.00 credit for purchasing OVER $50.00 in gift cards. Clearly that email is open to interpretation. He said it was written in the terms and conditions...however when I tried to read them for myself I was sent to a different screen (picture attached). I asked to speak with his supervisor, to which he informed me he is the very top of the customer service center. I asked for his boss. He said he was only authorized to give me a mailing address. Dumbfounded I asked him if Amazon corporate office in Washington does not have phones or email. He said they are only allowed to contact them via their computer system.

Most of my conversation with Deo was in silence. I'm assuming he was typing up everything I was saying for amazon's records. 54 minutes into my conversation the line disconnected and they never called back. I called back to get amazon's address and was surprisingly connected to a customer service rep who worked in the same department as Deo. He spoke with Deo (who was unable to speak with me since he was busy with another customer). The new customer service rep, Ian, could not have been kinder and more professional. I felt like he was actually listening to my complaint verse Deo who just wanted to record every little thing I said.

Ian helped me get the contact information I needed, and also gave me the email address of where I could forward a copy of the email in question. Ian also provided me with the PO Box for Amazon Corporate Office in Seattle. Since I can not voice my compliant to Amazon corporate office directly, and I must write a letter, I figured I would share it with all who would listen. I hope your Amazon experience is better than mine. And Deo, you might want to pay attention to Ian and Mercy - they are customer service pros.

Long story short, Amazon - who is worth $430 BILLION - would rather lose a long time customer over $30. But as for the product, it was adorable and looked great in my candy bouquets. I'll attach a picture of that too.

***UPDATE*** After I emailed Amazon as part of a survey they sent, I received the following response:
Hello,

I understand you're concerned about $15 credit for purchasing $50 gift cards on our website.

I can completely understand how frustrating and annoying the situation can be when something like this happens but believe us it was never our intention.

After reviewing your previous correspondences with our customer service, I can confirm the information provided to you was correct. As my colleague mentioned, the promotional credit will be provided only one per customer.

You can view the restrictions for promotion code here:

http://www.amazon.com/promos

I realize this is frustrating; however we aren't able to offer any additional insight or action on this matter and any further inquiries regarding this issue won't receive a response.

We appreciate your understanding.

Best regards,
Madhan P.
Amazon.com

I did go to that link, and there is nothing that addresses my concern or complaint. I wrote back asking them to highlight the part that actually has to do with my complaint. Don't worry, I'll update this if they ever respond.

rating 1.0. These cards are blank!! Don’t gift.. You’ll look like a fool! I ordered six of these cards for my daughters teachers for teachers appreciation week. They arrived on time but apparently they were not activated by Amazon!
Just imagine you gift this card to somebody and when they try to redeem it states the balance on this is zero dollars! How embarrassing!!
I’m glad I checked with one of the teachers to see if it worked because it was a large amount $250 contributed by all the students’ parents and she said that the card was invalid!! The other teachers might have even found it awkward to tell us that the card was blank. It was a gift after all !
I called amazon customer service and ask them to activate these cards. They told me that they need to have the teachers call them directly with claim codes so they can apply the balance to their individual accounts.
Amazon was not able to activate them on their end without each person I gifted it to calling them. It was really a bad experience - first by giving somebody a gift card that was blank and then having them deal with the customer service themselves to get the money they were gifted. I am really really embarrassed and extremely unhappy with the experience. please please please do not make a fool of yourself by getting these!!

rating 2.0. Do not purchase multiples at the same time If you are only buying one (or several of the same quantity), you can ignore this low review.

I purchased several of these gift cards as gifts for my son's teachers and therapists for end of the school year gifts. I purchased six cards in three different amounts ($15,$20,$40). When the cards arrived, there was no way to tell which amount was on which card. There was a note that said that I could line up the card number with the order number. Unfortunately as they were all connected to same order number, it was not possible to tell. I contacted customer service for help, however they were very reluctant to assist. I'm not sure if it was just the agent, or an issue with gift cards in general, but it was not good customer service, that's for sure.

Anyhow, if you are purchasing multiples in different amounts, purchase each card separately to avoid such issues.

The card itself is cute.

rating 4.0. Got you cardboard flowers... [[VIDEOID:c113e753e31ece0da8873aa8805634a7]] Got you cardboard flowers...and an Amazon gift card!!

First thing to note is that a little setup is required. Card was placed on back for some reason and must move to the card slot up front. Something to keep in mind when gifting.

Material for card stock and print is of very high quality.

Slider can be a bit loose. Not a nice smooth transition every time depending on how you slide it, but does give the card reveal nevertheless.

Amazon gift card itself is very basic. Just Amazon logos on a white backdrop.

Redeeming card is easy. There's a peel off sticker (instead of scratcher) on back of the card, no need to worry about scratching off the text of the code. If you use the amazon app you can redeem the amount in seconds: click the camera option and align the boxes with the text code.

rating 1.0. I was misled I had an issue with one of my orders: for some reason the payment didn't went thru and the message
"Revise you payment method" has appeared for this order. So I added a different credit card to my account and tried to use it as the payment method for this order. However, I couldn't do that because it was erroring out when I tried. So I contacted Amazon support asking how can I fix this order. Amazon representative also couldn't change the credit card as he was getting the same error message. So he suggested a workaround: I should add money to my amazon gift card balance using my credit card and he will apply this gift card balance as a payment for this order. After I followed rep instructions and reloaded my gift card balance, I was assures that he applied it to pay for this order. A couple days later I found that I was lied to: the order was cancelled anyway ( and the price went up like 50% since then, so I will have to pay more if reorder), and now I stuck with several hundreds gift card balance which I have nothing to spend for.

rating 1.0. Beware of loading gift card. Do not use this service. So apparently there is a list of guidelines while purchasing a gift card online through Amazon, that if you unknowingly violate, they will freeze your purchases and force you to reset your account, but they won't let you verify the purchase and they won't tell you exactly why they accused you of fraud.  All while telling you there are no limits or guidelines. This happened before and I was told it won't be an issue again as they will put a notice on my account not to flag gift cards.  Even though they say there is no purchase limit on gift cards, there absolutely is, but again they won't tell you what that cutoff is.  It makes absolutely no since why you can select any amount, but then you get flagged for buying it.  The canned response if you contact customer service is "just wait 24 hours for processing."  Which is completely false and there is a department for verifying pending gift card transactions, but they will tell you there isn't and make you deal with them for multiple hours without helping you, and even their own customer service employees are not allowed to directly contact the department to verify your purchases.  And, if you miss any phone calls you are out of luck because there are no return calls received by these Amazon departments.  So my advice is to buy gift cards in person and then pay with it in checkout, but do not load it on your amazon account.  They also apparently have the authority to remove your gift card balance without any verification and you will not get the money back. Do not use this service.

input: rating 5.0. Very convenient, saves a bit of money Wish I had known about this feature sooner, as it probably would've saved me nearly $100 in overall spent money with Amazon. Super convenient, though if you are looking for an instantaneous deposit into your Amazon account, you won't get it. This took roughly 30-40 minutes to hit my account before and I was able to use it, so if you're looking to do this and purchase a deal before time runs out, you may not make it in time. rating 4.0. Dude, where's my PIN? Exactly what I needed. But knocking a star off for vulnerability to theft. Card ships activated, ready-to-spend, no secret PIN or password required. And since it's a gift card, there's no name printed on it -- which by itself is totally fine, but all these things taken together makes it a thief's delight. Like sending cash in the mail. Maybe all of this wouldn't have occurred to me, except the delivery person left the envelope on my front porch in full view of the public. Thanks be praised it wasn't stolen!

rating 1.0. Don't buy these!!! I ordered 2 $100 Visa gift cards and I'm beyond disappointed. When I received my cards I tried to use them and they both were declined. I also tried to check my balance through Visa and was told that Amazon canceled my cards so they were no longer activated (there was no answer to why they were deactivated.) After going back and forth Visa said that Amazon can only activate the card and then Amazon's customer service claimed they cannot activate the cards. After multiple hours on the phone, Amazon finally canceled both cards and refunded me through with an "Amazon gift card" linked to my account instead of refunding me through my original payment method (credit card) which was better than nothing at the time. Two weeks later Amazon re-ordered the Visa Gift cards and charged my credit card without my approval!! I had to call and tell them I didn't know why or how they re-ordered these Visa Gift cards for me and I demanded that they refund my money onto my credit card that they used without my consent and they finally did. Amazon and Visa made it very clear that their customer services don't work together to solve basic issues, it's disappointing as there was no direction and not even managers knew how to deal with their product or services and were not willing to contact each other. This was one of the worst experiences I have ever had with Visa and Amazons customer service. They need to figure out how to reactivate Visa Gift cards and how to work together as two businesses :(!!

rating 1.0. DO NOT BUY GIFT CARD DIRECTLY FROM AMAZON I purchased this gift card as a gift and the recipient used a small portion of it. Someone hacked into the remaining balance and used it to buy items and have them shipped to a different state. This is fraud and Amazon has been very in helpful in giving us our money back. We have called many times and have not gotten our money back. DO NOT BUY A GIFT CARD DIRECTLY FROM AMAZON! Reviews show this has happened many times to customers and occurs frequently.  I have purchased Amazon cards from local stores and have NEVER had someone steal my money. It has only happened when I bought directly from Amazon.  BUYER BEWARE. If I could have given 0 stars I would have.

rating 5.0. Works fine if you just read things. Worked perfectly for me. Was able to use online, as credit and as debit. First thing when it arrived I made sure there was a slip included showing Amazon had activated it (PEOPLE: If this is not included it is a red flag and you must contact Amazon immediately... If it wasn't activated they just need to send you a new replacement card). I then went online immediately to the website on the card and the balance showed immediately and said the card was active. Then I set up my address and pin number on the same page and that was it... Less than 5 minutes. Many people leaving negative reviews must not understand how to register the card online. If you don't go online and add your address, you can't use online. If you don't go in and add a pin number, you can't use it as a debit. You also can't use it to get cash back (which the seller discloses), and I bet some people are just mad that's not an option. Just read the fine print, read the very simple instructions on the card, and you're good to go.

rating 5.0. this card will be just fine for you Not sure how so many people don’t know how a gift card works, but it’s activated upon delivery. To make payments to something online, you have to register it TO YOU. Any online purchase has to have a “bill-to” address, hence registering it under your name. Also, if you eat at a restaurant or for gas, or anything for an authorization, every single card will be ran for about 15%-20% of the card value. It’s not that hard. If your IQ is above 13, this card will be just fine for you.

rating 1.0. These cards are blank!! Don’t gift.. You’ll look like a fool! I ordered six of these cards for my daughters teachers for teachers appreciation week. They arrived on time but apparently they were not activated by Amazon!
Just imagine you gift this card to somebody and when they try to redeem it states the balance on this is zero dollars! How embarrassing!!
I’m glad I checked with one of the teachers to see if it worked because it was a large amount $250 contributed by all the students’ parents and she said that the card was invalid!! The other teachers might have even found it awkward to tell us that the card was blank. It was a gift after all !
I called amazon customer service and ask them to activate these cards. They told me that they need to have the teachers call them directly with claim codes so they can apply the balance to their individual accounts.
Amazon was not able to activate them on their end without each person I gifted it to calling them. It was really a bad experience - first by giving somebody a gift card that was blank and then having them deal with the customer service themselves to get the money they were gifted. I am really really embarrassed and extremely unhappy with the experience. please please please do not make a fool of yourself by getting these!!

rating 2.0. Do not purchase multiples at the same time If you are only buying one (or several of the same quantity), you can ignore this low review.

I purchased several of these gift cards as gifts for my son's teachers and therapists for end of the school year gifts. I purchased six cards in three different amounts ($15,$20,$40). When the cards arrived, there was no way to tell which amount was on which card. There was a note that said that I could line up the card number with the order number. Unfortunately as they were all connected to same order number, it was not possible to tell. I contacted customer service for help, however they were very reluctant to assist. I'm not sure if it was just the agent, or an issue with gift cards in general, but it was not good customer service, that's for sure.

Anyhow, if you are purchasing multiples in different amounts, purchase each card separately to avoid such issues.

The card itself is cute.

rating 4.0. Got you cardboard flowers... [[VIDEOID:c113e753e31ece0da8873aa8805634a7]] Got you cardboard flowers...and an Amazon gift card!!

First thing to note is that a little setup is required. Card was placed on back for some reason and must move to the card slot up front. Something to keep in mind when gifting.

Material for card stock and print is of very high quality.

Slider can be a bit loose. Not a nice smooth transition every time depending on how you slide it, but does give the card reveal nevertheless.

Amazon gift card itself is very basic. Just Amazon logos on a white backdrop.

Redeeming card is easy. There's a peel off sticker (instead of scratcher) on back of the card, no need to worry about scratching off the text of the code. If you use the amazon app you can redeem the amount in seconds: click the camera option and align the boxes with the text code.

rating 1.0. Don't believe Amazon promotional ads in the emails I purchased these gift cards as part of a promotion Amazon was running. I will upload a photo of the email I received. In the email it stated "Get $15.00 Amazon credit with a $50 Amazon gift card purchase". It's the end of the school year, so I purchased 3 $50.00 gift card for my kids teachers. I figured that was a great deal for both of us, I spend $35.00 and they get $50.00 for their classroom.

Four days later I attempt to purchase a birthday present and was reminded to check on my $45.00 credit I thought I had coming. I couldn't find it, used all their "self help" links, and finally found a phone number to speak to a human and get help. First lady was wonderful, told me the reason I didn't get it was because I had purchased all three in one transaction, instead of 3. I thought that was silly so I asked for a supervisor.

The supervisor, Deo located in the Philippines, was the supervisor I was connected to. He informed me what the email really meant was ONE $15.00 credit for purchasing OVER $50.00 in gift cards. Clearly that email is open to interpretation. He said it was written in the terms and conditions...however when I tried to read them for myself I was sent to a different screen (picture attached). I asked to speak with his supervisor, to which he informed me he is the very top of the customer service center. I asked for his boss. He said he was only authorized to give me a mailing address. Dumbfounded I asked him if Amazon corporate office in Washington does not have phones or email. He said they are only allowed to contact them via their computer system.

Most of my conversation with Deo was in silence. I'm assuming he was typing up everything I was saying for amazon's records. 54 minutes into my conversation the line disconnected and they never called back. I called back to get amazon's address and was surprisingly connected to a customer service rep who worked in the same department as Deo. He spoke with Deo (who was unable to speak with me since he was busy with another customer). The new customer service rep, Ian, could not have been kinder and more professional. I felt like he was actually listening to my complaint verse Deo who just wanted to record every little thing I said.

Ian helped me get the contact information I needed, and also gave me the email address of where I could forward a copy of the email in question. Ian also provided me with the PO Box for Amazon Corporate Office in Seattle. Since I can not voice my compliant to Amazon corporate office directly, and I must write a letter, I figured I would share it with all who would listen. I hope your Amazon experience is better than mine. And Deo, you might want to pay attention to Ian and Mercy - they are customer service pros.

Long story short, Amazon - who is worth $430 BILLION - would rather lose a long time customer over $30. But as for the product, it was adorable and looked great in my candy bouquets. I'll attach a picture of that too.

***UPDATE*** After I emailed Amazon as part of a survey they sent, I received the following response:
Hello,

I understand you're concerned about $15 credit for purchasing $50 gift cards on our website.

I can completely understand how frustrating and annoying the situation can be when something like this happens but believe us it was never our intention.

After reviewing your previous correspondences with our customer service, I can confirm the information provided to you was correct. As my colleague mentioned, the promotional credit will be provided only one per customer.

You can view the restrictions for promotion code here:

http://www.amazon.com/promos

I realize this is frustrating; however we aren't able to offer any additional insight or action on this matter and any further inquiries regarding this issue won't receive a response.

We appreciate your understanding.

Best regards,
Madhan P.
Amazon.com

I did go to that link, and there is nothing that addresses my concern or complaint. I wrote back asking them to highlight the part that actually has to do with my complaint. Don't worry, I'll update this if they ever respond.

current_product_reviews:
rating 5.0. No tamper proof! Comes sealed in plastic &envelope glued closed. I got this $10 giftcard as part of a bundle promotion during Cyber Monday. It came sealed in a plastic bag so you know it hasn't been tampered with. The black envelope comes glued closed, as well, which makes it great for gifting. This does have $10 on it, and was a nice incentive. If you're purchasing this outright to gift, I hope these pictures also help give you an idea of what to expect in person. I know there are many amazon gift card options to choose from, and I've bought many, so I just wanted to show what this particular gift card looks like in person. FYI...This is the hard cardboard style giftcard, which I've noticed is what seems to come in most options. Even the one's that come in the fancier box options on here. This is shinier. Some are matte. Most people probably don't care, but just thought I'd mention it.

*Tips if looking because of a bundle promo: Don't forget to add this in your cart along with the accompanying item if this is offered as a promotion. But...You must get to this page by clicking on the promotional add or it will not count. I originally tried just searching for a $10 gift card and adding it, but it would not deduct the discount when doing it that way. The link should be on the product page. Just click the words giftcard, and it should bring you here. I personally got mine as part of the Ring doorbell pro &Chime pro bundle on Cyber Monday. I actually thought it was going to be emailed for some reason, but it arrived in the box with the Chime Pro. I hope this review helps someone in some way.
other_product_reviews:
rating 5.0. A classy box, especially nice if you're not putting it in a card. I bought two of these from my daughter's volleyball team to their coaches for our volleyball banquet. In past years they've always received some type of volleyball item, and me and the other mom's thought we'd get them $50 gift cards instead so they can buy themselves whatever they want. I chose the gift box option, because it looks very classy and comes ready to gift if you want. I personally chose to put these in a little gift bag for a little mystery, and the fact they came with boxes really made this idea work.

They quality of the packaging was beautiful. The box has a beveled Amazon printed allover the entire top lid, including the sides. I do wish that the actual gift cards themselves were plastic like a credit card or how most other gift cards usually are. These are a thick stock paper/cardboard material. It still works, and for our purpose, I was happy. I would suggest getting a plastic option that doesn't come with a box if you're going to just unbox it and put this in an actual card. I almost bought these to do that, and figured at least I'd have a little gift box if needed. I now would recommend not doing that unless you are gifting in the box. I mean, you still could. It's just a gift card after all. The one meant for placing in a card will look higher quality for that purpose. But for handing someone a card with this gift box accompanying it would be a great idea, too.

This came in a sealed plastic bag to protect it, and so you can tell it hasn't been tampered with. It has an instruction card, which I placed in the lid, as well as a receipt incase there's any issues, which I folded and put under the gift cards. This is my experience, and I hope this helps with your gifting ideas and needs. I hope my pictures help give you an idea of to expect in person.

rating 5.0. I love being able to add any amount to my balance, and not just a whole number. I love being able to add any amount to my gift card balance. Definitely, the way to go in this kind of situation.

rating 4.0. The product itself is great and worked perfectly, but Amazon falsified the shipping time. To those who are having trouble with their cards make sure you receive an activation slip in your package so you know that it was scanned and will work. Next, you want to make sure you go to the website on the back of the card, enter your card information and register the card and setup your pin. Also make sure you check the card balance afterwards to make sure your card was activated and has the amount it's supposed to have.

I received my card yesterday morning, I followed the instructions on the website on the back of the card to register. It was very easy. No problems at the store using the card. However, when ordering my card Amazon said Guaranteed delivery by June 4th so I bought. Upon reviewing my order after purchase Amazon said Guaranteed delivery June 5th by 5pm. I attempted to contact Amazon via live chat but he was just making excuses.
The next day I reviewed my order yet again and it said Guaranteed delivery June 5th 9pm. Also, I'm a prime customer so I should have received it in a days time (I ordered it on the 3rd) but didn't receive it until the 5th. So basically it took over 2 days to get the package.

rating 1.0. Buyer beware I buy from Amazon for the convenience, the good prices, and mostly the peace of mind offered should there be a problem with the order. This however does not extend to Gift Cards. I purchased many of these over time, and most times it went without any problem. However, when there is a problem, Amazon's care-free customer-centric approach does not apply. Here is a specific example: I received an envelop with just the purchase receipt and no gift card. I called immediately Customer Service and they told me they couldn't resolve this issue since the Gift Card was already used. After a very long and frustrating hour-long conversation I was offered the option to contact my bank and dispute the charge. I will try this and follow up on how this issue is resolved. At any rate, I will stop purchasing these Gift Cards from Amazon as I don't want to go through this again. I will instead, buy them from the supermarket (same price, same product, minus the convenience of free delivery)

rating 5.0. Solution Haha, this is funny that I get to review my Amazon Gift Card. So this is my re-loadable gift card for all my Amazon purchases. I don't know about anyone else but I hate all the little charges that come up on my account for Amazon. It is nice that they don't charge till it ships but each product coming out one by one by one! Sometimes it is confusing, because I remember the full purchase of everything in my order and not the individual prices. So I have to go back and look and see, I have to be sure someone didn't get a hold of my card again, it has happened in the past!

So here is a solution I found. Just put one charge on my card for a gift card. It gets loaded right to my account and I can reload it when I need to. So no more little charges here and there adding up on my statement. Easy. So if that part of shopping with Amazon annoys you like it did me, here is a solution that will keep you shopping happy. I am just surprised that it is on my list of items to review!!! It tickles me to see it here, but hey maybe this will help someone else. :)

rating 5.0. I have found lots of uses for purchasing an Amazon gift card! Here are a couple benefits to buying an Amazon gift card I don't think everybody thinks of. I read it before on some article about uses for Amazon gift cards and I think they are brilliant ideas.

Before I share my first one, let me start by saying that I don't remember how I lived to before Amazon and I don't want to try to remember it. It's kind of a similar feeling to when I think about how people must've lived before the Internet and how hard it must have been to have to go look things up at the library instead of googling them. So my first use for an Amazon gift card is actually just to build up a gift card amount to use to purchase my many many products that I am constantly making orders for on Amazon instead of using a credit card or debit card for each of those orders that early. The reason for that being that I ended up with line after line and page after page of Amazon orders on my credit card which made it really difficult to find any other purchases on there, most importantly possibly purchases that I did not make. My credit card has been stolen several times and the only way I found out was because I happened to see a purchase on The statement that was not familiar. Virtually impossible to do that now one almost everything says Amazon on my credit card bill. So instead I use an Amazon gift card to buy everything on Amazon and that leaves my credit card clear of Amazon plastered all over the statement.

The second thing that I use it for is actually just to get rid of other odds and ends of gift cards such as other visa gift cards and things like that that you get as a gift. I do that because usually End up with two dollars and change or so on on each of my leftover Visa gift cards and I have nothing to do with it because you can't spend it without knowing the exact balance and you have to remember that Number as you go to buy something andmake sure you're not applying to something that's more than what you have left on your card and it just turns into a huge pain. So instead of trying to use it from the visa gift card itself, I just buy an Amazon gift card with that which you can buy in any dollar amount and you can use a Visa gift card to purchase it, therefore getting rid of your visa gift card while using every single penny on it.

Those are my two biggest uses for Amazon gift cards and this hundred dollar gift card I got because Amazon was offering five dollars to you if you purchased $100 or more and Amazon gift cards, so why not? I know I probably spend that with in a month, if not a week :)

rating 1.0. Do not buy it So here it is Christmas morning and I’ve been on the phone for an hour trying to get this card to work. We activated the card on the phone, it said it was active... go online, and no matter what website, it does not accept the card. It says invalid. Even going to the website stated on the back of the card doesn’t recognize the card. For all of the rude people assuming that those of us who have not had luck with the card working, have not followed directions... there aren’t any real directions to follow outside the normal “activating” process. When you can’t even register the card on the website stated on the back of the card, clearly there is an issue with these cards. I’ve purchased these gifts cards before and they are hit or miss. This is ridiculous that Amazon continues to sell these on their site knowing there are so many issues with it. I could have walked to the store 5 houses down and purchased a gift card, but instead purchased thru Amazon and now my son is sitting here on Christmas bummed out because he can’t buy a game for his new oculus quest 2. Shame on Amazon.

rating 4.0. If I hadn't read the reviews..... I ordered 2 of these $100 Mastercard Gift cards, I ordered last night and received them today. I immediately jumped online to look at reviews for help....If I hadn't read the reviews I would not have known you have to go online and register each card (Include your name, email and address ~ creating a pin is optional). rating 5.0. Pretty fast, worked like a charm I was making a large purchase with Amazon but didn't have enough funds on just 1 debit card. I called Customer Service to see if they could split payment between 2 cards over the phone, since I knew it wasn't an option online. The representative told me they couldn't do that, but suggested I use 1 card to buy an electronic gift card, and then use the code to apply to my large order, using my 2nd card to pay the remaining balance. It worked out perfectly. The electronic gift card came relatively fast (about 15 minutes) and I was able to complete my order. Thank you Customer Service and thank you Amazon for the speedy delivery of the electronic gift card.

rating 5.0. Always be cautious and AWARE.. I had limited options and really needed to purchase this despite the problems I read here. My purchase was problem free but I also took the time to call the toll free number listed in information packet to check on the card balance. In my case the card was valid with funds. For this reason ONLY I gave it 5 stars. I however give VISA the brand “1” star because they have the POWER to implement changes that can eradicate all the issues around their pre-paid cards. It’s criminal and greedy to take advantage of consumers all because they have BRAND MONOPLY. Which basically means their name/brand carry enormous weight and clout that has afforded them a nice cushy spot as the leading source in pre-paid cards. Brand monoply allows for VISA something they otherwise could not afford to do because there are no other alternatives for us the consumer. There mindset is, “We don’t have to do anything but supply a card upon purchase...it doesn’t need to be dependable or reliable because we don’t have competiting brands that can match our years of branding and global recognition so we don’t really need to work too hard or break a sweat in providing quality service for our pre-paid cards...our integrity is competition based which means the less competition, less focus on ethics and integrity and more capitalization on being the monopoly!”

Unfortunately folks it’s a win win for VISA and crap shoot for us....so be cautious and aware of the game!...

rating 2.0. Think twice when purchasing Amazon Gift Cards I usually LOVE amazon and visit/purchase frequently, however my past experience with their gift cards have been a dissapointment. I received a Amazon gift card for Christmas that I tried to use, but was told it was not valid. I contacted the giver (my neice who assured me this card was VALID!) and I also contacted Amazon. The Amazon rep told me that it would be investigaged and someone would get back to me in two days - that was TWO WEEKS AGO and I still have not received ANYTHING! Oh I also emailed them and still did not hear back!!!

My sister just received a card from my mother - just tried to use it - GUESS WHAT - invalid code!!! SAME issue we had. I am the one who purchased the cards for my sister on my mothers behalf and they were sent directly from AMAZON!

Would probably NOT purchase any more amazon gift cards - NOT WORTH THE HASSEL. Customer service has not been great either! Dissappointed and not what I would expect from a company who usually does everything so well!

rating 1.0. Serious issues with redemption!!! Ordered this as a gift to my brother in law in the Army stationed in another state. It arrived on time, but when he tried to redeem the card it wouldn't work - it just kept saying something was wrong with the card. I called Amazon, waited on hold, talked to someone who said he saw no issues with the card or my order, and that I needed to have my brother in law call them. So, he called them, only to be treated as if I, or he, stole the card!!!! The card was never even physically in my presence. I ordered directly from Amazon and had it shipped directly to him. After several minutes of being treated like a lying thief, and being placed on hold several times, the woman on the line finally told him they would email him in 2 or 3 days and that she hoped all would turn out well for him. Um, what? I call again, wait on hold, they want the claim code. I call him, get the claim code, call Amazon again, wait on hold, and finally give them the claim code....and when they enter it, it says something is wrong with the card. Really? In the end, they applied it to their account directly anyway, so all is well, but I will never buy a gift card again! Extremely disappointed because this is my absolute favorite shopping site. I'm hoping this is a rare occurrence as I've never, ever had problems like this in all the years I've been using this site. Anyway, happy birthday guy - hope you had fun sitting on hold and being treated like a criminal!

rating 1.0. Do not trust "Free 1-Day Shipping" All the Amazon Gift Cards noted that they included Free 1-day shipping. I ordered two of them. The one sent to my house was delivered the next day, but the one sent to my relatives house (only a few towns over) wasn't delivered until the third day. Please don't advertise free one day shipping...especially on something like a gift card which is most likely a gift to someone....unless it WILL be delivered when you say it will. This is not the first time something like this has happened. It was just the first time it mattered that it was as late as it was!

rating 1.0. Terrible service - Not printing out the gift message and did not indicate who sent the gift to the receiver Socially it is rude that a gift receiver does not acknowledge to the sender after receiving the gift. After not receiving any word from the receiver, I waited very long time before making a step to check with the receiver about the gift. It turned out that my written message for good wishes was not delivered by Amazon.com. Socially it could be very damage to the future relationship between a gift giver and its receiver, had it not been handled correctly. It could defeat the good intention of sending a gift.

rating 1.0. One-Day Delivery Didn't Happen This card was billed as one-day delivery.  It arrived in our city within the specified time, but was delivered at least 22 hours after that.  I normally don't choose rush delivery, but we wanted to include the gift card in a package.  Because we tried to wait for the card, we had to pay very pricey delivery charges on the main package -- and we still have to mail the gift card.  I know we could have done it direct but we were trying to surprise someone.  Lesson learned.

current_product_reviews: rating 5.0. good gift for coworkers who like the Panda Panda Express $25. Gift Card:  I was a great gift for coworkers who like the Panda. Recipients reported no problems using the cards. As with any gift cards, I recommended placing a discrete identifying mark on them, so that a cashier can't (accidentally) claim that you have a $0 balance and then hand you back a different card that REALLY is empty. This is especially true for drive throughs when the card you present is sometimes out of your sight.
other_product_reviews: rating 5.0. great card for great food El Pollo Loco Gift Card:  The card itself was great. But twice, different drive throughs told me that the balance was $0 when I KNEW it wasn't. Both times I had them re-check. And both times the correct balance was then noted. I don't know if it was a card reader issue or something else. Regardless, I suggest marking  your card to know immediately if a different, truly $0. balance, card is handed back to you.

Even if they are only a dollar each, I would rather have the same amount of money on an Amazon gift card.

rating 5.0. Free Reindeer Ornament (and Box) with Gift Card Purchase [[VIDEOID:6b54148bd4781e6be110f9cfafe8b370]] First thing to note is that this is one of Amazon's Premium gift cards. $50 minimum on gift card for purchase. Gift cards easy to slide out and replace with another one if you think $50 is too much to give

Has a nice detachable reindeer ornament (via Velcro) to a box with ribbon design

Inside there's a gift card with checkered red design background with Amazon logos

No blank space for name of person of sender and receiver. Best to give to person directly (like proposing with a wedding ring) or combine it as part of other gift

Redeeming gift card is easy. There's a peel off sticker (instead of scratcher), so no need to worry about scratching off the text of the code. If you use the amazon app you can redeem the amount in seconds: click the camera option and align the boxes with the text code.

Doesn't show the amount on the card seeing it's customizable on the website. But it does give a pack in slip of paper to verify amount.

Totally ridiculous for a company the size of Amazon. I buy a huge amt of products from Amazon and never really wrote reviews but this was so annoying I had to. rating 1.0. Terrible product, difficult to use, no customer support You're probably thinking the prepaid Mastercard (MetaBank) is the same as the prepaid Visa (Sunrise), right?  Nope.  The prepaid MC (MetaBank) is barely supported by MasterCard.  My card would not work, so I called the number on the back on the card.  They have no live support.  Then I called MasterCard directly and was disconnected multiple times.  After 3-4 attempts I was able to speak to a live rep who transferred me to multiple outside lines.  It was a nightmare.  Finally I got a young, unprofessional person, presumably working out of a basement in Utah, who informed me that I couldn't send money across PayPal using this card.  Why on Earth not?!?  It's my money and my card.  Why can't I send money over PayPal?  I had to request an invoice just to transfer funds with the card.  I had previously used this card and the Visa sold on Amazon several times.  I will never, ever use this card again though.  It took several phone calls and hours of my time to finally use this card.  They're supposed to be activated at the time of purchase, yet this card was blocked and flagged, before I could finally use it.  Don't even think about trying to use it at a gas station or store.  Avoid MetaBank and the Blackhawk Network if possible.

rating 5.0. AVOID FRUSTRATION AND READ THIS I buy these cards all the time. They help me with spending issues I've had in the past.  They work wonderfully. However, based on a lot of the reviews I've read, it's important you read the info pamphlet so you clearly understand how to go about using it.  This will avoid many of the frustrations I read about in other reviews.  First, this card can not be used to retrieve cash.  Second, if you plan on using it for online purchases you need to register it in your name which will link it to your zip code. When merchants run CC #'s, the 4 main things that need to match with the card are name, card #, zip code and most of the time the CVV # on the back of the card. As long as you registered it correctly any online purchase will always go through assuming there's enough $ on the card.  Third, every retailers CC machines operate differently. So, it's important to ask the cashier if their machine automatically subtracts the amount available on the card from your purchase balance or if you need to tell them the exact amount that's on the card so they can manually punch that in before you swipe the card.  Asking this will avoid the card from getting declined. If your purchase balance is less then what's available on the card, then you don't need to ask. Also, there's been a handful of times where mine got declined even though I knew I had plenty of funds. I just re-ran the card as debit instead of credit and it works.  If you haven't registered your card and need to run it as debit, the pin is the last 4 digits on the card.  Fourth, if you purchase items that may hv a chance of being returned, try very hard to keep that card on hand even if it's all used up.  Most retailers insist they reimburse your $ bk onto its original form of payment or you'll end up with store credit. There's been many times I went to return and threw out my card already and had to basically make a scene to get cash back.  Lastly, never ever ever pay at the pump when getting gas w these types of prepaid cards.  There's extra security set up on the CC machine at gas pumps. It knows its a prepaid card, so it will hold all the funds available on your card for probably 7 days. Any retailer that accepts CC has to batch out their machine before any of those funds are deposited into their bank account. This is why they hold the funds.  And honestly for whatever reason that I don't understand it seems that these cards can't be used to pay bills online or over the phone. But, that's no fault of the card. That will happen no matter what prepaid card you use.  I hope this helps as you scroll along other reviews regarding their troubles they experienced. I believe if you are aware of this then it will help avoid any frustrations and help you determine if this is the right type of card depending on your needs.

Then, just to make my gifts a little more tacky, I had to put a sticky note on each container with the denomination of the enclosed card. Reading the reviews I see I was not the only one who was caught blind sighted by Amazon's Christmas "surprise". Last year this was not an issue. I'm sure Amazon saved a lot of money printing blank, generic gift cards. Next year I'll order my gift cards elsewhere.

rating 5.0. You get exactly what you pay for! 80k+ reviews on Amazon gift cards already... Not feeling too confident that this will get many views... Sorry, I don't have social media, so sometimes I tend to update my status subliminally within the reviews that I write. Anyhow, what a lifesaver, am I right? This is probably the only thing that you can buy for anyone. For instance, you can't buy a Walmart gift card for just anyone; you can only buy one of those for the people in your life that are actually willing to step inside of one. And be honest; have you ever heard someone say, "I don't shop at Amazon, too far of a drive for me."? Of course you haven't! Everyone you know shops at Amazon, probably multiple times a day.

Also, it's a life saver for people like me who are awful at remembering important dates (e.g., wife's birthday, wedding anniversary, mother's day, day you first met, anniversary of your first date, first time you held hands, kissed, proposal date, et cetera). So, do what I do, buy a few to have on hand incase you forget to get your wife a gift for one of the aforementioned 'important dates'. It doesn't matter that the two of you share your Amazon account. When she asks, "Why did you buy five $10 Amazon gift cards while you were in the bathroom?" Either respond by winking, or telling her that you have a potluck at working coming up.

Free Reindeer Ornament (and Box) with Gift Card Purchase [[VIDEOID:6b54148bd4781e6be110f9cfafe8b370]] First thing to note is that this is one of Amazon's Premium gift cards. $50 minimum on gift card for purchase. Gift cards easy to slide out and replace with another one if you think $50 is too much to give

Has a nice detachable reindeer ornament (via Velcro) to a box with ribbon design

Inside there's a gift card with checkered red design background with Amazon logos

No blank space for name of person of sender and receiver. Best to give to person directly (like proposing with a wedding ring) or combine it as part of other gift

Redeeming gift card is easy. There's a peel off sticker (instead of scratcher), so no need to worry about scratching off the text of the code. If you use the amazon app you can redeem the amount in seconds: click the camera option and align the boxes with the text code.

Doesn't show the amount on the card seeing it's customizable on the website. But it does give a pack in slip of paper to verify amount.

rating 1.0. Don't believe the promotion emails - out $45.00 I purchased these gift cards as part of a promotion Amazon was running. I will upload a photo of the email I received. In the email it stated "Get $15.00 Amazon credit with a $50 Amazon gift card purchase". It's the end of the school year, so I purchased 3 $50.00 gift card for my kids teachers. I figured that was a great deal for both of us, I spend $35.00 and they get $50.00 for their classroom.

Four days later I attempt to purchase a birthday present and was reminded to check on my $45.00 credit I thought I had coming. I couldn't find it, used all their "self help" links, and finally found a phone number to speak to a human and get help. First lady was wonderful, told me the reason I didn't get it was because I had purchased all three in one transaction, instead of 3. I thought that was silly so I asked for a supervisor.

The supervisor, Deo located in the Philippines, was the supervisor I was connected to. He informed me what the email really meant was ONE $15.00 credit for purchasing OVER $50.00 in gift cards. Clearly that email is open to interpretation. He said it was written in the terms and conditions...however when I tried to read them for myself I was sent to a different screen (picture attached). I asked to speak with his supervisor, to which he informed me he is the very top of the customer service center. I asked for his boss. He said he was only authorized to give me a mailing address. Dumbfounded I asked him if Amazon corporate office in Washington does not have phones or email. He said they are only allowed to contact them via their computer system.

Most of my conversation with Deo was in silence. I'm assuming he was typing up everything I was saying for amazon's records. 54 minutes into my conversation the line disconnected and they never called back. I called back to get amazon's address and was surprisingly connected to a customer service rep who worked in the same department as Deo. He spoke with Deo (who was unable to speak with me since he was busy with another customer). The new customer service rep, Ian, could not have been kinder and more professional. I felt like he was actually listening to my complaint verse Deo who just wanted to record every little thing I said.

Ian helped me get the contact information I needed, and also gave me the email address of where I could forward a copy of the email in question. Ian also provided me with the PO Box for Amazon Corporate Office in Seattle. Since I can not voice my compliant to Amazon corporate office directly, and I must write a letter, I figured I would share it with all who would listen. I hope your Amazon experience is better than mine. And Deo, you might want to pay attention to Ian and Mercy - they are customer service pros.

Long story short, Amazon - who is worth $430 BILLION - would rather lose a long time customer over $30. But as for the product, it was adorable and looked great in my candy bouquets. I'll attach a picture of that too.

It occurs the Saturday after Thanksgiving when we hide different gift cards in differnet tins, boxes or envelopes around the house. We draw numbers from a hat and go in the order of your number. rating 5.0. AVOID FRUSTRATION AND READ THIS I buy these cards all the time. They help me with spending issues I've had in the past.  They work wonderfully. However, based on a lot of the reviews I've read, it's important you read the info pamphlet so you clearly understand how to go about using it.  This will avoid many of the frustrations I read about in other reviews.  First, this card can not be used to retrieve cash.  Second, if you plan on using it for online purchases you need to register it in your name which will link it to your zip code. When merchants run CC #'s, the 4 main things that need to match with the card are name, card #, zip code and most of the time the CVV # on the back of the card. As long as you registered it correctly any online purchase will always go through assuming there's enough $ on the card.  Third, every retailers CC machines operate differently. So, it's important to ask the cashier if their machine automatically subtracts the amount available on the card from your purchase balance or if you need to tell them the exact amount that's on the card so they can manually punch that in before you swipe the card.  Asking this will avoid the card from getting declined. If your purchase balance is less then what's available on the card, then you don't need to ask. Also, there's been a handful of times where mine got declined even though I knew I had plenty of funds. I just re-ran the card as debit instead of credit and it works.  If you haven't registered your card and need to run it as debit, the pin is the last 4 digits on the card.  Fourth, if you purchase items that may hv a chance of being returned, try very hard to keep that card on hand even if it's all used up.  Most retailers insist they reimburse your $ bk onto its original form of payment or you'll end up with store credit. There's been many times I went to return and threw out my card already and had to basically make a scene to get cash back.  Lastly, never ever ever pay at the pump when getting gas w these types of prepaid cards.  There's extra security set up on the CC machine at gas pumps. It knows its a prepaid card, so it will hold all the funds available on your card for probably 7 days. Any retailer that accepts CC has to batch out their machine before any of those funds are deposited into their bank account. This is why they hold the funds.  And honestly for whatever reason that I don't understand it seems that these cards can't be used to pay bills online or over the phone. But, that's no fault of the card. That will happen no matter what prepaid card you use.  I hope this helps as you scroll along other reviews regarding their troubles they experienced. rating 5.0. So cute! This is a great gift with a reusable little tin as a bonus.
The gift card is standard credit card size.
The colors of the tin and card are true to the product photos, and I was happy with them.
The bottom of the tin has a rectangular hole (so the barcode shows) covered in permanent plastic.
The tin only has a tiny bit of wording on it, (Amazon gift cards Made In China) which you can see in the photo.
The faux-velvet plastic card holder inside has semi-circle notches on each side of the card, so it's simple to remove the card. This plastic part is also easy so remove from the tin--and there's no adhesive holding it in place, so the tin is clean.
And, of course, the gift card recipient can buy almost anything with the gift card--since Amazon sells almost everything!

rating 5.0. Great gifting flexibility with an included free birthday tin card holder These gift card tins are great. The birthday cupcake tin with a padded interior for the card is a nice little extra to affix to a birthday card and does not increase the cost of the gift card. Also, there is flexibility in gifting denomination as the giver can choose whatever amount they wish to gift; the only downside is that the actual physical gift card will not display the amount if you choose something other than the standard $10, $25, $50 denominations.
Couple the one-day shipping with the fact that these gift cards can be put toward just about anything the recipient could possibly want and these are a great option for a last minute gift or a gift for someone who is difficult to shop for.

It's situated in a little fuzzy insert in the tin so it doesn't move around, but it's too tight and hard to pull out. It had no amount written on it, but since it is just a piece of thick paper I did write the amount on it as well as a "to" and "from" right on the paper card. There is a little square of paper in with the tin that has the instructions on how to use it, but that little square of paper also does not fit into the tin (it's not like it's complicated instructions, so it's not even necessary to include that, but why not just make it the proper size to fit in the tin, guys? It's fine enough, and the most important part of course is that it works (I hope it works) but next time I'll just go to any store that sells Amazon gift cards on a rack and buy one that is actually a plastic card.

The tin is just as the photos show. The gift card itself is just a flimsy little piece of paper and the edges of it were peeling up. It's situated in a little fuzzy insert in the tin so it doesn't move around, but it's too tight and hard to pull out. It had no amount written on it, but since it is just a piece of thick paper I did write the amount on it as well as a "to" and "from" right on the paper card. There is a little square of paper in with the tin that has the instructions on how to use it, but that little square of paper also does not fit into the tin (it's not like it's complicated instructions, so it's not even necessary to include that, but why not just make it the proper size to fit in the tin, guys? It's fine enough, and the most important part of course is that it works (I hope it works) but next time I'll just go to any store that sells Amazon gift cards on a rack and buy one that is actually a plastic card.

rating 5.0. Great gifting flexibility with an included free birthday tin card holder These gift card tins are great. The birthday cupcake tin with a padded interior for the card is a nice little extra to affix to a birthday card and does not increase the cost of the gift card. Also, there is flexibility in gifting denomination as the giver can choose whatever amount they wish to gift; the only downside is that the actual physical gift card will not display the amount if you choose something other than the standard $10, $25, $50 denominations.
Couple the one-day shipping with the fact that these gift cards can be put toward just about anything the recipient could possibly want and these are a great option for a last minute gift or a gift for someone who is difficult to shop for.

They assured me that the $25 value was on my new card but when I tried to confirm it was not accepted. Reluctant to give a gift card that might have $0 value to the recipient, I asked for the supervisor. After assuring me that there was $25 in my account, I reiterated that my intent was to give the gift card to someone who would expect that they would have $25 value. rating 5.0. Wonderful Gift Card Tin and Card This is my second order of an Amazon gift card that came inside this lovely birthday cupcake tin.  In person it looks even more adorable than the photo on Amazon!  In my view this is for all age groups.  It is so sweet.  When the tin is opened, there sits the gift card which is in the same white and blue colors as the tin!  And the gift card card is pressed nicely inside on the bottom that has a black faux velvet-like lining. In other words, the card doesn't move around--it is custom-fitted inside the tin but easy to remove.  So that is another attractive feature.  The back of the gift card itself has the serial number on it.  And when the gift card is in the tin,  you can turn the tin over and there's a "see-through" area that shows the card's serial number. You do need to take the card out of the tin to peel off the claim code area.  What is so nice is that the recipient of this pretty gift card can use the tin again for whatever gift cards they might want to put in it.  Amazon gift cards are always a perfect gift, and when they are inside this lovely tin, the gift just becomes extra special.
