rating 5.0. Fast, Easy Setup, No Hassle!!! My husband lost his debit card and needed gas for the week. I was able to pay for this giftcard via bank account on Amazon to solve the issue. The card came quickly and was easy to set up over the phone. The webpage is easy to use too. I recommend registering your card there. You can see a record of your purchases, like you can at a bank. It also can protect you against theft. It’s not a scam. Always look for the Amazon Verified Purchase near headings of reviews, when buying items like these. Amazon doesn’t require any additional information.
Note: Cannot be used at the ATM, no cash back, and gas must be purchased inside, instead of at the pump. Enjoy.

rating 5.0. Slide up to reveal card [[VIDEOID:2ef078ae9120aa06532c2d30d4018141]] Very inviting design. Slide up to reveal card.

Card is in it's own insert slot so won't get stuck while sliding

Card design is very generic, just the Amazon logo (probably because you can select your own amount)

On back there's a cutout for UPC, Serial number scanning code

Redeeming card is easy. There's a peel off sticker (instead of scratcher) on back of the card, no need to worry about scratching off the text of the code. If you use the amazon app you can redeem the amount in seconds: click the camera option and align the boxes with the text code.

Shipping is ridiculously fast. Can setup for next day delivery for no additional charge.

rating 4.0. Seriously low stress method to complete a "wish you well" gift It was the perfect "wish you well as you go" gift. Ordered as part of a group goodbye gift to some of our team moving onto other adventures. Became the perfect gift as there was no worries about what to get, who needed more $$ for an appropriate gift or things not fitting. Amazon gift card allowed them to "store" electronically their treat, add other funds to it and select what they wanted to celebrate their next step in life. Even came wrapped! So easy I will offer to do the "goodbye gift" again.

Delay in delivery is the only reason these are not 5 stars -- as delivery person would not get out of their car to walk through an open gate to deliver.

rating 4.0. Mystery Cards! As other reviewers have mentioned, the product itself is lovely and delivery is fast! However, the cards come in snowflake tins that are SEALED and there is NO indication of denomination anywhere.  Thus, one would either have to a) unwrap the gift card to determine its value or b) call Amazon and seek assistance.  After 20 minutes on the phone I was finally able to locate an Amazon employee who had me read each ( of SEVEN) card serial number to her so she could look up the value of each card. Come on, Amazon....I'm not the first one to complain about this.  I love you guys, but this is sloppy...

rating 1.0. Size Matters BE VERY CAREFUL.  If you order cards of different denominations, as I did. Don't order them all in the snowflake tin because there is no easy way to sort which tin is for which amount. They are individually shrinked wrapped with a covered claim code. The $100 have the denomination labelled on the card but can't be identified without a call to Amazon with the serial number or removing the individual tin shrink wrap. And even then the recipient you won't know the unlabelled card is for $25. Only after calling with the serial number and a long hold could I find out which were which. ARRGGHHH.  This is frustration plus wrapping, especially for someone with arthritis.  I  decided to return all the $25 cards.

rating 3.0. I ordered two gift cards in different amounts. They ... I ordered two gift cards in different amounts.  They were both in snowflake gift tins.  I assumed they would be marked so I would know the amount of each.  That is not the case.  The cards have serial numbers so I looked on the invoice to see the serial number for amount.  Not any help there either.  How do I check the value of each card.  I went to "amazon.com redeem" and it seemed the only thing I could do was attach the card to my account.  Not helpful at all.  If you are going to order gift cards from amazon, make sure you get different gift cards or tins, and write down what amount goes into which card.  Or make sure all the amounts are the same.

rating 5.0. A classy box, especially nice if you're not putting it in a card. I bought two of these from my daughter's volleyball team to their coaches for our volleyball banquet. In past years they've always received some type of volleyball item, and me and the other mom's thought we'd get them $50 gift cards instead so they can buy themselves whatever they want. I chose the gift box option, because it looks very classy and comes ready to gift if you want. I personally chose to put these in a little gift bag for a little mystery, and the fact they came with boxes really made this idea work.

They quality of the packaging was beautiful. The box has a beveled Amazon printed allover the entire top lid, including the sides. I do wish that the actual gift cards themselves were plastic like a credit card or how most other gift cards usually are. These are a thick stock paper/cardboard material. It still works, and for our purpose, I was happy. I would suggest getting a plastic option that doesn't come with a box if you're going to just unbox it and put this in an actual card. I almost bought these to do that, and figured at least I'd have a little gift box if needed. I now would recommend not doing that unless you are gifting in the box. I mean, you still could. It's just a gift card after all. The one meant for placing in a card will look higher quality for that purpose. But for handing someone a card with this gift box accompanying it would be a great idea, too.

This came in a sealed plastic bag to protect it, and so you can tell it hasn't been tampered with. It has an instruction card, which I placed in the lid, as well as a receipt incase there's any issues, which I folded and put under the gift cards. This is my experience, and I hope this helps with your gifting ideas and needs. I hope my pictures help give you an idea of to expect in person.

rating 5.0. Register it online before you can use it online I needed to spend some money on a credit card that has a UK billing address, which can be difficult as a lot of online stores don't allow foreign billing addresses. And most sites that sell Visa gift cards also don't accept foreign billing addresses themselves. But Amazon does, so I bought this Visa gift card on Amazon, paid for it with the UK card, problem solved.

I didn't use this card in any physical stores, so I can't speak to that experience. I received it today, immediately registered it at the URL that's on the back of the card (top right, small text). You need to register it online first to be able to make purchases with it online (this is how you set your name and billing address on it, which you would need for any online order).

I then used it on Etsy.com and Mouth.com for purchases that came close to the $100. I then used it to reload my Amazon gift card balance in the amount that I had left. I then checked my balance again at the same website where I had registered it, and indeed it showed my three transactions and correct zero balance. No problems at any point.

rating 5.0. A GREAT (and SAFER & MORE PRIVATE!) Alternative to Using Your Own Credit Card for Online Shopping! I got this [[ASIN:B01MSBQB1P $100 Visa Gift Card]] because although I have actual credit/debit cards that I use in person at brick & mortar stores and other businesses and online on big-name websites like Amazon, Instacart, Shipt, Target and the like, I am not comfortable giving out my actual card info when shopping on less(er) known and/or potentially risky websites.

So when circumstances necessitate doing business with them (as it did recently, while looking for a replacement battery for my 2007 HP laptop I dug out from a closet after not using it since 2009-2010 [and particularly since switching to Apple/Mac in 2012-2013] in order to play some retro offline Windows games I've not played in years that I missed playing................unfortunately, neither Amazon nor any other big-name stores or websites [like Best Buy, NewEgg, etc.] seem to carry a compatible battery and I had to go with an unknown/lesser known website) I prefer to use non-reloadable prepaid "gift" cards, so that in the worst case scenario, if the site is "fly-by-night" scam business and/or the website gets hacked, they won't have access to my actual credit/debit card info or bank data, and the maximum amount of money I'd potentially lose would limited to whatever the denomination (or remaining balance) on the prepaid card. Granted, depending on the card (AFAIK there cards come in denominations of $25, $50, $100 and $200 cards) it could still be a lot of money to lose, but at least one's entire credit line and private info wouldn't be at risk as it would be using a conventional credit/debit card.

Personally, after several years of using prepaid Visa, Mastercard and other credit cards, like this one, I have to say I think prepaid "gift" cards are a GREAT option, when shopping online, especially when making purchases at certain websites/kinds of websites.

However, previously, Amazon did NOT offer these prepaid Visa, Mastercard and other credit-card "gift" cards and rather, only offered gift cards for specific stores, restaurants or other businesses. This necessitated having to physically go to a brick & mortar store and buy a prepaid "gift" credit card in person anytime I needed a Visa/Mastercard to use online -- and since, due to disabilities, I'm unable to drive, getting these prepaid cards were a huge hassle!

Which is why I am so glad that Amazon has started offering these prepaid Visa/Mastercard gift cards for sale through their site. I don't know if its because of COVID-19, and the lockdowns, or what, but making these prepaid cards available through Amazon, has made it a LOT easier for those of us who are mostly homebound, to get and use these cards as needed.

As mentioned, these cards come in denominations of $25, $50, $100 and $200, and are NOT reloadable (meaning once the balance on the card is used up, you need to buy another card and cannot add more money to that same card). Also they cannot be used to get cash at ATMs or POS (point of sale) purchases. But they can be made for most other purchases where Visa and Mastercard are accepted, which these days, is the majority of places, at least in the US. I have never used these in other countries, not even Canada, so I cannot speak to their functionality abroad. That being said, I recently tried to use one of these cards online on GOG (Good Old Games) and I had difficulty completing the transaction because apparently the site's based in Europe, so the card was declined. But then I just made the payment using the card as a "guest" via Paypal (I no longer trust Paypal enough to maintain an actual account with them anymore) and the transaction went through, immediately.

Other than that, I've had no problems with these cards. The card arrives, open the package and register the card number on their listed website, with your basic info (name, address, phone number, email) which AFAIK is NOT necessary for in-person purchases, but IS (or may be) necessary for online purchases -- and then use the card just like you would any other Visa or Mastercard until you've exhausted the amount on your card. Then, throw it away (or cut it up first if you've registered it with your personal info) and get another one the next time you need it!

Easy-peasy!

rating 2.0. Amazon Reload: Marketing: Meh; Technology: Yeesh! Amazon has changed up its Gift Card Reload Program. The Eye Candy leaves me, a regular Gift card reloader, underwhelmed.

Indeed, it looks and feels more confusing.

The underlying tech seems to have changed.
Previously both the amount of the reload and the 2% cashback showed up quicker.

Now the reload transaction took a little longer to show up. The 2% Cash Back took even longer to show up.

Amazon failed to have announced the changes ahead of time!

Given the changes, the transaction should not slowed down and the 2% Cash Back should have been instanteous as it was previously.

Can't say I can blame other customers more alarming bad reviews. I join those saying, if it wasn't broke, why change it?

The explanation could have been given in the announcement that didn't happen.

I'll continue to use the program and monitor the situation.

rating 4.0. Sometimes order works, sometimes it doesn't... I ordered this gift card once, successfully verified the balance online, registered it online, was able to use it with no problems for my online shopping and at local swipe payments.  I thought "great!  So easy!  Let's do that again."  So next time I ordered two gift cards.  This time--after they shipped but before they had arrived, I got an email from Amazon warning that there was a problem activating the card, and that they were sending out a replacement.  When I got the original two, I checked the online balance, and sure enough neither of them were valid.  So I waited for the replacement order.  When it arrived--there was only one replacement card, not two!  The one worked.  But then I had to go through customer service chat at Amazon (took awhile) and get it sorted out for them to give me a credit for the second card.  That was a bit of a hassle.

rating 5.0. 360° PERFECTION ® Great for buying exactly what you want. I use these when purchasing online. Security issues are a thing of the past and you can keep your personal accounts away from theives and it's just as simple to use as your debit or credit card. You can register your card online and it will give you a list of your transaction history along with your available balance after each purchase. I've bought several of these off Amazon and have never had any issues or time consuming complications. Don't want to register your card? That is ok too! Run it as credit instead and of debit, no pin required. It's literally ready to use as soon as it arrives and Amazon always does a great job delivering on time sometimes even earlier than expected which is a plus. You will get notified the day before a time window to expect your package along with live detailed tracking  information and will send you a picture of your package excatly where you specify Amazon to leave it. Amazon is the best.

rating 5.0. All good in this end I don't always like using my own CC when I road trip for the weekend, which I do with some frequency. I'll use GCs so I don't have to carry cash, as a way to maintain a budget and as a means of decreasing any opportunities of having my info compromised. I had absolutely no issue activating or using, just have to take the time to activate and set up a PIN which was all too easy a process. I already know gift cards tend not to to work at the pump at most gas stations so no prob going inside so no issues there. I used this particular card to pick up groceries and to pay for my camping spot.

I do think the activation is a bit higher than necessary, not like it involves anything tangible. I guess they have to make their money some way and it's an upfront detail.

Overall my experiences have been good ones. I won't dismiss those who've had nightmare experiences because I'm sure stuff like that happens. I've been fortunate in that respect, knock on wood. Cheers.

rating 5.0. Wonderful Gift Card Tin and Card This is my second order of an Amazon gift card that came inside this lovely birthday cupcake tin.  In person it looks even more adorable than the photo on Amazon!  In my view this is for all age groups.  It is so sweet.  When the tin is opened, there sits the gift card which is in the same white and blue colors as the tin!  And the gift card card is pressed nicely inside on the bottom that has a black faux velvet-like lining. In other words, the card doesn't move around--it is custom-fitted inside the tin but easy to remove.  So that is another attractive feature.  The back of the gift card itself has the serial number on it.  And when the gift card is in the tin,  you can turn the tin over and there's a "see-through" area that shows the card's serial number. You do need to take the card out of the tin to peel off the claim code area.  What is so nice is that the recipient of this pretty gift card can use the tin again for whatever gift cards they might want to put in it.  Amazon gift cards are always a perfect gift, and when they are inside this lovely tin, the gift just becomes extra special.

rating 4.0. This does not act as a hotel key for some reason At first I assumed this was a a multifunction card used for credit as well as other typical card functions.  When staying at a Comfort Inn in Pittsburgh, I tried to use this to unlock my hotel room door.  I must have inserted the card into the face of my door half a dozen times before realizing Amazon had ripped me off.  I went to the hotel’s front desk, borderline hysterical, and said “There must have been some kind of mistake!”  My handler found me and told me everything was alright and it turns out that these actually aren’t used for opening hotel room doors.  It turns out the hotel gives you a free card every time you stay with them, but when you check out, it doesn’t work anymore.  However, I was able to buy products from Amazon using the $25 I had already spent on the card.  That made me feel better.  Thanks Amazon!

rating 5.0. Ummmmm.... Well, how exactly do you review a gift card? I paid $25 and received a piece of plastic that was *worth* $25. So....5 stars!!!

In all seriousness though, I buy at least 3-5 of these every year for Christmas presents. Pretty much everyone (including my 74 year old mom, who literally can't even remember what the Start button is on her Windows laptop) shops on Amazon now, so these are just as good as cash in my opinion, without the ridiculous "activation" fees of buying Visa or MC gift cards. I seriously never understood why people fall for that nonsense. Plus, Amazon usually does free next day shipping around Christmas which makes it great for those last minute gifts and you don't feel like fighting with 10 other people for the last couple gift cards at Walgreens or CVS.

rating 4.0. Pretty Enough Box and Bow for Any Occasion It's a $25 gift card. I bought it to take advantage of an Alexa/Echo offer. The box and bow are attractive but they were in the garbage in five minutes and a few minutes after that the credit balance was in my account. Sending the pretty box and bow was nice and only other option was a white gift box with bow. Why I couldn't have been emailed the $25 code I don't know but I actually feel a little guilty for Amazon wrapping this up nicely and shipping it overnight. I'd have been happy with a code to copy, paste and redeem. Now all Amazon had to do, as promised, is email my $5 voucher for buying this $25 card.

rating 5.0. Gift Card With Style Just your standard Amazon Giftcard inside a box that has the word 'Amazon' embossed on the front cover with an orange bow.

There are finger grooves for the front cover and inside the box for easy access.

Claiming the gift card once opened is also very easy. There's a peel off sticker so you don't need to bother with getting a coin or sharp metal object. Also you don't need to worry about scratching off the text for the gift card code.

If the person you gift it to have the Amazon app; they can click on the camera icon (after logging in) next to the search bar, and scan the code instead of typing each letter/number individually to claim the total amount.

Overall very simplistic design and a has great presentation. Great if you're tired of gifting gift cards in a standard envelope format.

rating 5.0. Great packaging Ordered this through Alexa on Echo as part of a promo. I planned on just keeping it for myself, but now I'm going to give it as a gift. Came nicely packaged in a black box with orange bow :)

rating 5.0. Classy, elegant presentation... Silly to say that I was impressed with this gift card but that's exactly what went through my mind when I opened it.
The card itself is shipped inside a small, elegant gift box that is adorned with an orange ribbon. The entire box is shrink wrapped and makes a nice gift presentation so feel confident if you need to ship this directly to the recipient rather than give to someone in person.
The box features the Amazon logo - very classy and suitable for both men and women of all ages.

rating 5.0. Exceptional Gund quality, great gift or collectible.. I've been ordering the Amazon holiday bears since they first came out. These are exceptional quality Gund Bears with an Amazon logo worked into the design. This year's bear - the ninth in the series - has the logo "smile" embroidered on the sole of the left foot. It is very soft and cuddly with red snowflake fleece for mittens and cap.
I spend a lot on Amazon over the holidays, mostly for local charities, so I end up using the gift card myself. The bear becomes a gift, again usually for a local charity. Win-win all around. Thank you Amazon!

Not only does Amazon have the widest selection of household stuff but they also have great boxes for presenting their cards. This one has a satiny pillow and is a perfect wedding gift look. The box slides open like a match box.

rating 1.0. Don’t buy it for a teen on their parents account—it may get revoked I am appalled at Amazon’s process of revoking gift cards for teens on their parent’s account. Obviously no testing done, a system that shuts down accounts for no reason and customer service that hasn’t a clue. I didn’t understand why this gift card has negative reviews and now I know. If I can’t give my grandchildren an Amazon gift card, of what use are they to me? In addition, the packing for this is ridiculous. It’s a beautiful card in beautiful packaging, but the card doesn’t stay in the box, gets stuck on the sliding lid, and makes you think the card wasn’t included when you open it. Figure out a way to get it to stick in place. Incompetence drives me crazy as does lack of testing for new updates to the system.

rating 4.0. Nice box but cheap card The box is elegant but the gift card itself is not!

1) why use a cardboard card and not plastic? When you’re gifting a large amount it just looks cheap! And it’s wedged so tightly inside that you easily risk damaging the card trying to extract it. This problem would be solved with a plastic card.

2) It should come with a printed card/note that indicates the amount of the gift. There’s no way to tell if you received a $10 or $1,000 card unless you went online to redeem it to your account. If I’m gifting a large face amount I want the recipient to know it’s a valuable card, so they don’t treat it casually and put it aside and misplace or lose it, as one does with random gift cards.

But as this is essentially a “free” accoutrement that comes with the purchased gift card, I won’t deduct more than a star.

rating 1.0. HOW TO GET CARD TO WORK!!!! Gave as a GIFT. Was told that she tried to use card, WOULD NOT WORK, kept saying INVALID CODE.  LOTS OF MONEY HERE. HOW DO I GET CARD TO WORK OR GET MY MONEY BACK?

rating 1.0. DO NOT BUY AMAZON GIFT CARDS - NO SOLUTION TO STOLEN CODES AND ZERO BALANCE My Amazon Gift Card arrived with the claim code already revealed. It had no balance on it. Amazon said they can't replace the card or refund my money.

I have been purchasing Amazon gift cards for years. I like the boxes and tins they provide. Sadly, I recently found out from friends that sometimes the gift cards I had been gifting had $0 on them but they felt bad telling me. Being that it was much later, I could no longer do anything about it.

However, it happened again…this December 2022, I purchased a $50 Amazon gift card. The claim code was already revealed (it didn't have the orange strip covering it). I called Amazon and they said the card was not valid and they would refund my money. They didn't refund my money, so I had to call back again. This time they said they couldn't refund my money because the gift card was canceled. They weren’t sure what to do next, so they escalated the issue and told me to wait 24 hours. I received an email from the escalation team that said,

"I've researched your Amazon Gift Card and found it is unable to use. I tried to issue refund however I'm not able to resend the gift card or refund the order. Under these circumstances, if the gift card was purchased using a credit/debit card, we suggest that the purchaser contact their bank to see what options may be available."

Per Amazon’s Customer Service’s instructions, I disputed the charge with my credit card company. A few hours later I received an angry email from Amazon telling me that I disputed a charge and they would still need to receive payment from me for the disputed charge so they will be charging my credit card on file. Now I’m getting charged twice for something I never received!

I can't believe a company like Amazon would do this! It's fraudulent to keep issuing gift cards with no money and then insisting on charging customers for the missing funds. To top it all off, they had the nerve to sign off on their email saying they are building the “Earth's Most Customer-Centric Company.”

NEVER BUY THESE GIFT CARDS! IMPOSSIBLE TO GET YOUR MONEY BACK.

rating 2.0. Buyers Beware - Gift Cards are Blank (denominations are NOT printed on them) Yes, the pop up card is very cute, so is the snowflake, and all the other Amazon gift card containers. The cards came with a "cute" note . . . Don't see an amount on your gift cards? It's a surprise! So, I had to go to my order history to see which cards had $50 and which had $25. Then, just to make my gifts a little more tacky, I had to put a sticky note on each container with the denomination of the enclosed card. Reading the reviews I see I was not the only one who was caught blind sighted by Amazon's Christmas "surprise". Last year this was not an issue. I'm sure Amazon saved a lot of money printing blank, generic gift cards. Next year I'll order my gift cards elsewhere.

rating 1.0. DO NOT BUY GIFT CARD DIRECTLY FROM AMAZON I purchased this gift card as a gift and the recipient used a small portion of it. Someone hacked into the remaining balance and used it to buy items and have them shipped to a different state. This is fraud and Amazon has been very in helpful in giving us our money back. We have called many times and have not gotten our money back. DO NOT BUY A GIFT CARD DIRECTLY FROM AMAZON! Reviews show this has happened many times to customers and occurs frequently.  I have purchased Amazon cards from local stores and have NEVER had someone steal my money. It has only happened when I bought directly from Amazon.  BUYER BEWARE. If I could have given 0 stars I would have.

rating 4.0. Free Tin Box with Gift card purchase Which card design to get is fun but daunting with so many choices. Let’s first break this down into digital versus physical. Simply said, if you are giving a gift in person, don’t settle for the simple card in plain plastic sleeve. For no extra charge, has truly wonderfully designed boxes. Free! That’s why I chose this diamond plate tin. It works for men or women, and for ANY occasion. I buy this design in advance, keep a couple “in stock” at home and ready to give. This design works for any holiday, any occasion, any taste.

When you need a gift in a pinch, or like me, ALMOST forget a birthday: Love the digital version gift cards that get emailed, too. HOWEVER: Be sure to check with your recipient that it was received!! Three times, I have had people either forget about the email containing the gift card, or lose the code. What’s great is that Amazon can re-issue a new digital card and can easily tell the giver if it has been redeemed. So, word of advice, it is NOT tacky to ask if received if you didn’t get the thank you are entitled to in a not so perfect world of imperfect manner.

rating 1.0. Great card until I have bought several of these gift cards for personal use. One of the reasons I buy these is because you can use them at stores that do not accept credit cards. You can assign a pin and use the card as a debit card. There is a grocery store that I like to shop at that will not accept credit cards but does accept visa debit gift cards such as this one. Again I think this is my 3rd or 4th card purchased from Amazon and up until this point I had no issues with this card.
So what happened?
There is a lot of fraud that happens with gift cards. They can be easily compromised. The gift card I purchased prior to this one did have a fraudulent charge on it. I caught it a few hours after it happened and called Paypal. They were able to stop the transaction and get me my money back. Understanding this, when I made any transactions with this card I made sure the card was not handed over to anyone. In early November I decided to check on my balance. The card was compromised. The last transaction was identical to the fraudulent charge mentioned above. A paypal charge leaving a few pennies left in the card. This gift card DOES have a way to dispute fraudulent charges. I downloaded the forms filled them out and e-mailed them to the e-mail address listed. A week later after receiving no response I sent another email inquiring on the status of my dispute. They e-mailed stating I need to send them the dispute form. I replied with the dispute form and explained I had sent it with my first e-mail. A week later no response. I sent it again to both emails asking for a status. Again no response. At this point it feels the ignorance is intentional.
So here's my suggestion.
Unless you use all the funds on this card within a month after purchasing it I don't recommend this card.

rating 5.0. Great gifting flexibility with an included free birthday tin card holder These gift card tins are great. The birthday cupcake tin with a padded interior for the card is a nice little extra to affix to a birthday card and does not increase the cost of the gift card. Also, there is flexibility in gifting denomination as the giver can choose whatever amount they wish to gift; the only downside is that the actual physical gift card will not display the amount if you choose something other than the standard $10, $25, $50 denominations.
Couple the one-day shipping with the fact that these gift cards can be put toward just about anything the recipient could possibly want and these are a great option for a last minute gift or a gift for someone who is difficult to shop for.

input:
rating 2.0. Think twice when purchasing Amazon Gift Cards I usually LOVE amazon and visit/purchase frequently, however my past experience with their gift cards have been a dissapointment. I received a Amazon gift card for Christmas that I tried to use, but was told it was not valid. I contacted the giver (my neice who assured me this card was VALID!) and I also contacted Amazon. The Amazon rep told me that it would be investigaged and someone would get back to me in two days - that was TWO WEEKS AGO and I still have not received ANYTHING! Oh I also emailed them and still did not hear back!!!

My sister just received a card from my mother - just tried to use it - GUESS WHAT - invalid code!!! SAME issue we had. I am the one who purchased the cards for my sister on my mothers behalf and they were sent directly from AMAZON!

Would probably NOT purchase any more amazon gift cards - NOT WORTH THE HASSEL. Customer service has not been great either! Dissappointed and not what I would expect from a company who usually does everything so well!

rating 1.0. Serious issues with redemption!!! Ordered this as a gift to my brother in law in the Army stationed in another state. It arrived on time, but when he tried to redeem the card it wouldn't work - it just kept saying something was wrong with the card. I called Amazon, waited on hold, talked to someone who said he saw no issues with the card or my order, and that I needed to have my brother in law call them. So, he called them, only to be treated as if I, or he, stole the card!!!! The card was never even physically in my presence. I ordered directly from Amazon and had it shipped directly to him. After several minutes of being treated like a lying thief, and being placed on hold several times, the woman on the line finally told him they would email him in 2 or 3 days and that she hoped all would turn out well for him. Um, what? I call again, wait on hold, they want the claim code. I call him, get the claim code, call Amazon again, wait on hold, and finally give them the claim code....and when they enter it, it says something is wrong with the card. Really? In the end, they applied it to their account directly anyway, so all is well, but I will never buy a gift card again! Extremely disappointed because this is my absolute favorite shopping site. I'm hoping this is a rare occurrence as I've never, ever had problems like this in all the years I've been using this site. Anyway, happy birthday guy - hope you had fun sitting on hold and being treated like a criminal!

rating 1.0. Don't believe the promotion emails - out $45.00 I purchased these gift cards as part of a promotion Amazon was running. I will upload a photo of the email I received. In the email it stated "Get $15.00 Amazon credit with a $50 Amazon gift card purchase". It's the end of the school year, so I purchased 3 $50.00 gift card for my kids teachers. I figured that was a great deal for both of us, I spend $35.00 and they get $50.00 for their classroom.

Four days later I attempt to purchase a birthday present and was reminded to check on my $45.00 credit I thought I had coming. I couldn't find it, used all their "self help" links, and finally found a phone number to speak to a human and get help. First lady was wonderful, told me the reason I didn't get it was because I had purchased all three in one transaction, instead of 3. I thought that was silly so I asked for a supervisor.

The supervisor, Deo located in the Philippines, was the supervisor I was connected to. He informed me what the email really meant was ONE $15.00 credit for purchasing OVER $50.00 in gift cards. Clearly that email is open to interpretation. He said it was written in the terms and conditions...however when I tried to read them for myself I was sent to a different screen (picture attached). I asked to speak with his supervisor, to which he informed me he is the very top of the customer service center. I asked for his boss. He said he was only authorized to give me a mailing address. Dumbfounded I asked him if Amazon corporate office in Washington does not have phones or email. He said they are only allowed to contact them via their computer system.

Most of my conversation with Deo was in silence. I'm assuming he was typing up everything I was saying for amazon's records. 54 minutes into my conversation the line disconnected and they never called back. I called back to get amazon's address and was surprisingly connected to a customer service rep who worked in the same department as Deo. He spoke with Deo (who was unable to speak with me since he was busy with another customer). The new customer service rep, Ian, could not have been kinder and more professional. I felt like he was actually listening to my complaint verse Deo who just wanted to record every little thing I said.

Ian helped me get the contact information I needed, and also gave me the email address of where I could forward a copy of the email in question. Ian also provided me with the PO Box for Amazon Corporate Office in Seattle. Since I can not voice my compliant to Amazon corporate office directly, and I must write a letter, I figured I would share it with all who would listen. I hope your Amazon experience is better than mine. And Deo, you might want to pay attention to Ian and Mercy - they are customer service pros.

Long story short, Amazon - who is worth $430 BILLION - would rather lose a long time customer over $30. But as for the product, it was adorable and looked great in my candy bouquets. I'll attach a picture of that too.

rating 5.0. AVOID FRUSTRATION AND READ THIS I buy these cards all the time. They help me with spending issues I've had in the past.  They work wonderfully. However, based on a lot of the reviews I've read, it's important you read the info pamphlet so you clearly understand how to go about using it.  This will avoid many of the frustrations I read about in other reviews.  First, this card can not be used to retrieve cash.  Second, if you plan on using it for online purchases you need to register it in your name which will link it to your zip code. When merchants run CC #'s, the 4 main things that need to match with the card are name, card #, zip code and most of the time the CVV # on the back of the card. As long as you registered it correctly any online purchase will always go through assuming there's enough $ on the card.  Third, every retailers CC machines operate differently. So, it's important to ask the cashier if their machine automatically subtracts the amount available on the card from your purchase balance or if you need to tell them the exact amount that's on the card so they can manually punch that in before you swipe the card.  Asking this will avoid the card from getting declined. If your purchase balance is less then what's available on the card, then you don't need to ask. Also, there's been a handful of times where mine got declined even though I knew I had plenty of funds. I just re-ran the card as debit instead of credit and it works.  If you haven't registered your card and need to run it as debit, the pin is the last 4 digits on the card.  Fourth, if you purchase items that may hv a chance of being returned, try very hard to keep that card on hand even if it's all used up.  Most retailers insist they reimburse your $ bk onto its original form of payment or you'll end up with store credit. There's been many times I went to return and threw out my card already and had to basically make a scene to get cash back.  Lastly, never ever ever pay at the pump when getting gas w these types of prepaid cards.  There's extra security set up on the CC machine at gas pumps. It knows its a prepaid card, so it will hold all the funds available on your card for probably 7 days. Any retailer that accepts CC has to batch out their machine before any of those funds are deposited into their bank account. This is why they hold the funds.  And honestly for whatever reason that I don't understand it seems that these cards can't be used to pay bills online or over the phone. But, that's no fault of the card. That will happen no matter what prepaid card you use.  I hope this helps as you scroll along other reviews regarding their troubles they experienced. I believe if you are aware of this then it will help avoid any frustrations and help you determine if this is the right type of card depending on your needs.

rating 5.0. Sturdy cardboard gift card affixed within an adorable tin! I ordered three of these cute, snowman gift card tins this year. One was sent to someone out of state, and two were sent to my home for local gifts. All of the tins (3 separate orders) arrived with the card well-affixed inside, and all of the tins arrived in perfect condition. The card is made of a heavy card board rather than the plastic ones of the past. This is better for the environment folks!  This particular tin has a ribbon on top so that it can be used as an ornament or easily tied to a wrapped gift. The tin opens well and closes firmly. It has a window in the back which reveals the number, but with the security code hidden. This adorable tin is free from Amazon with the purchase of your gift card, and the card has no expiration date. We all know that you can buy just about anything from Amazon, so these cute gift tins make really good gifts!!!

rating 5.0. Great Chrsitmas Gifts 2 years ago we started a new tradition at our house, a Yankee gift exchange. It occurs the Saturday after Thanksgiving when we hide different gift cards in differnet tins, boxes or envelopes around the house. We draw numbers from a hat and go in the order of your number. The first person picks a package and opens it. The next person has the option to steal that gift card or pick a new one and so on. We go 4 rounds and before each round we pick a new number. The Amazon gift cards are always the most popular and often get stolen by other family members. We have to buy more each year.

Was originally going to buy an e-gift card but there was a lot of reviews mentioning that the card value was zero when they tried to use it as the information was stolen during the email process (which is why you don't send credit card or other sensitive information via email). Apparently Amazon doesn't encrypt their emails. They could have you buy the gift card, then go into your account to share the information with the recipient. They could even send separate emails with the card number in one and the pin in the other. I'm sure that would confuse hackers somewhat. But they aren't doing any of that even though they have been aware of the problem for years.

Since I had to order a physical gift card by mail, I figured I might as well get one that is cute.

rating 5.0. Fun Gift Card Slider [[VIDEOID:362fcac7daee16967f9e95cee3ffa16c]] Great gift card slider for those who like to switch things up form other physical gift card distribution methods like boxes, bi-fold cards and/or envelopes

Middle section gives off a penguin stare, but changes into a smile-like eye appearance when tab is pulled.

When fully open; left side displays over a dozen penguins also giving a smile-like gester...with two balls and a dot (from original penguin eye stare).

Right side also have dozen plus penguins along with a gift card attached via non-residue like glue.

Back of the slider when fully expanded shows back of the penguins on the right and left side minus the eyes.

Sliding is very fluid. Can see a toddler having tons of fun sliding the card in and out

Redeeming card is easy. There's a peel off sticker (instead of scratcher), so no need to worry about scratching off the text of the code. If you use the amazon app you can redeem the amount in seconds: click the camera option and align the boxes with the text code.

Overall, very unique designed card.

rating 5.0. Got this for Lunar New Year Instead of handing out traditional red envelopes with cash for Lunar New Year, decided to hand out $10 amazon gift cards instead.

Doesn't have much to do with Lunar New Year themes with exception of the red coloring on the envelopes.

Can also be used for distributing in bulk at parties or other special occasions; giving every one the same gift so no one feels left out or start comparing which person got the better gift card design. Seeing the golden snow flake imprints, more catered towards the winter season.

Paper/cardboard for the envelope is of good quality. Has slight texture to it.

Card is held onto the envelope via light glue, which can be removed without leaving residue.

There is a cutout on back displaying UPC code or serial number.

Not really re-usable unless you have glue or double sided tape. But it does give off a premium first impression.

Gift card design itself is very basic; just white with red amazon logos on it. Has the amount specified on the corner which is a nice touch and don't keep the receiver guessing how much is inside

Redeeming card is easy. There's a peel off sticker (instead of scratcher), so no need to worry about scratching off the text of the code. If you use the amazon app you can redeem the amount in seconds: click the camera option and align the boxes with the text code.

Overall, great gift card set to distribute in bulk

rating 3.0. Not What I Expected Did not receive bookmark sleeve or the ribbon in stock photos

Code is on the back of the bookmark. From the photos thought it was a separate card or voucher. Fell odd when in use; when you flip it over, you see the gift card amount and bar codes. Guess it can be a motivator to finish a book, rewarding yourself after it's done, but only can be used once.

Code redemption is a scratch off which is disappointing. Thought they went pass that, and gone with a peel off design. Must have a coin or key handy.

If you use the amazon app you can redeem the amount in seconds: click the camera option and align the boxes with the text code.

Shows the amount on the back of the bookmark, so no surprises.

Quality of the bookmark is good. Sturdy plastic. Slightly thinner than regular Amazon Gift Card. Is tear-proof, but can be cut with scissors.

Essentially just an Amazon Gift Card but in a different form factor: in a shape that can be used as a bookmark

rating 5.0. Free Box with Gift Card Purchase Instead of being in a envelope and/or attached to a thank you, congratulations card; it's a card within premium looking gift box.

Box itself is very sturdy, has a premium look and feel to it.

The cloth and card holder inside; not so much. Cloth is wrapped around a plastic card holder that's glued to the box.

Cloth is very thin, but functional for what's it's intended for. If you swap in-and-out cards multiple times, the cloth may rip; but for initial first impression it won't.

Box has a slide out design to it. Has a stop gap so doesn't slide all the way out unless you intentionally use more force than you have to.

Very nice finger ribbon to pull out the contents like a drawer

Back of the box has a space for name of person of sender and receiver.

Includes UPC and Serial No. number and bar code if you find an error with the order.

Gift card design is very basic; just red with amazon logos on it. Redeeming card is easy. There's a peel off sticker (instead of scratcher), so no need to worry about scratching off the text of the code. If you use the amazon app you can redeem the amount in seconds: click the camera option and align the boxes with the text code.

Doesn't show the amount on the card seeing it's customizable on the website. But it does give a pack in slip of paper to verify amount.

Aside from plastic card holder and cloth fabric being low quality; it still gives off a premium first impression

rating 5.0. Free Socks (size 10-13) With Gift Card Purchase Update: They now added sock size in description

First thing to note is that the sock size is 10-13 US. Both my friend and I received the same size.
Description on product page states "Gift Card is affixed inside a gift box with a pair of Happy Socks (one size)" so I assume everyone will receive the same. If not, or want to confirm (if purchased) please put a comment to let others know.

Quality of the socks are standard; not too thin and not too sweat absorbent (like sport socks). Great for formal wear and those who want to draw attention to their feet, ankles, and/or shoes with the bright colors/patterns.

Happy socks usually go for around $16 a pair, so that's how much you are potentially saving

Gift card design is very basic; just red with amazon logos on it. Redeeming card is easy. There's a peel off sticker (instead of scratcher), so no need to worry about scratching off the text of the code. If you use the amazon app you can redeem the amount in seconds: click the camera option next to search bar, swipe up for gift card redeem photo option and align the boxes with the text code.

Box is very presentable; very good gift idea for a family or friends

Overall if you're going to spend money on Amazon throughout the year, or generous enough to give someone a pair of socks + $100+ gift card; then this is a excellent purchase; just keep in mind the sock size

The card it self has a nice design to it also as it's not just a blank background with amazon logo. it has several objects displayed on the card like a bike, shoes, guitar. Doesn't have a happy birthday greeting which makes sense as you don't want to be repetitive with your presentation

Redeeming card is easy. There's a peel of sticker (instead of scratcher), so no need to worry about scratching off the text of the code. If you use the amazon app you can redeem the amount in seconds: click the camera option

Overall, will make an amazing gift for someone's birthday (especially when you don't really know what to get them)

rating 5.0. Brighten Up Someone's Day [[VIDEOID:eadb31d3ed793581d0a7c14cce88309]] Bunny Slider is very cute; change from expressionless/slight frown to overjoyed/happy after pulling down the tab.

Slider movement is very fluid and will not snag when pulling down. Pushing it back up on the other hand may cause some friction at times, but not to the point that it'll get permanently stuck

Card itself is plain and simple in the front; being all white with amazon logos

Redeeming card is easy. There's a peel of sticker (instead of scratcher), so no need to worry about scratching off the text of the code. If you use the amazon app you can redeem the amount in seconds: click the camera option next to search bar, swipe up for gift card redeem photo option and align the boxes with the text code.

Very good gift to brighten someone's day

rating 5.0. Free Bear With Gift Card Purchase You get a free holiday teddy bear with a gift card purchase. Great incentive if you purchase items off Amazon regularly, or plan to give a $100+ gift card to a friend or family member.

Supposedly this is the 10th edition of the Holiday Teddy Bear. I started collecting these 2 years ago. Last year was a polar bear with a snow flake santa hat and gloves as opposed to grizzly bear with a Santa hat got this year. Year before that was a darker grizzly bear with a beanie.

Hat is not removable (unless you mess with the stitching). There is an ear under there for those wondering. The bear is super soft and huggable. Ideal for toddlers who get attached to soft objects during cold weather. There's also a amazon logo tattoo on it's left foot

Gift card is just a neckless attached to the bear. This time, the gift code is a peel off instead of a scratch off in back. Code is very easy to apply with Amazon app, just use the camera feature instead of the traditional input method.

Minimum gift card amount is $100. From previous years track record, best to get it early or the lower demonization may sell out

Overall if you're going to spend money on Amazon throughout the year or generous enough to give someone a bear + $100+ gift card; then this is a excellent purchase

rating 5.0. Gift Card With Style Just your standard Amazon Giftcard inside a box that has the word 'Amazon' embossed on the front cover with an orange bow.

There are finger grooves for the front cover and inside the box for easy access.

Claiming the gift card once opened is also very easy. There's a peel off sticker so you don't need to bother with getting a coin or sharp metal object. Also you don't need to worry about scratching off the text for the gift card code.

If the person you gift it to have the Amazon app; they can click on the camera icon (after logging in) next to the search bar, and scan the code instead of typing each letter/number individually to claim the total amount.

Overall very simplistic design and a has great presentation. Great if you're tired of gifting gift cards in a standard envelope format.

rating 5.0. Free Bear With Gift Card Purchase You get a free holiday teddy bear with a giftcard purchase. Great incentive if you purchase items off Amazon regularly or plan to give a $100+ giftcard to a friend or family member.

Supposedly this is the 9th edition of the Holiday Teddy Bear. I started collecting these last year. Last year was a grizzly bear with the beanie as opposed to the polar bear with a Santa hat and gloves we got this year. Another difference is that the 2015 model had the Amazon (dot) com logo on the left foot and the 2016 bear has just the amazon arrow logo.

Gloves and hat are not removable (unless you mess with the stitching). The bear is super soft and huggable. Ideal for toddlers who get attached to soft objects during cold weather.

Gift card is just a neckless attached to the bear. This time it's in the shape of a gift box which is nice. Gift code is a scratch off in back.

Overall if you're going to spend money on Amazon throughout the year or generous enough to give someone a bear + $100 to someone then this is a excellent purchase

** Note: I white out the UPC and Serial Number in the photos just in case i need to use it as reference later

rating 5.0. Season's Greetings Get a free Limited Edition Gund Teddy Bear with gift card purchase.

Gift card is per usual, scratch off the foil cover from the back with your keys, coin etc and reveal the gift card code. The bar codes in front serve no purpose to the end customer.

The teddy bear is very high quality, from the fur to the hat. The bear is very fun to hug especially if you have small children.

The only downside I'd say is that the stomach area is a bit bear (pun intended) if you remove the gift card neckless attachment and wish they included some sort of holiday shirt

Overall great gift for a child and/or loved one for the holidays.

rating 1.0. Don't buy these!!! I ordered 2 $100 Visa gift cards and I'm beyond disappointed. When I received my cards I tried to use them and they both were declined. I also tried to check my balance through Visa and was told that Amazon canceled my cards so they were no longer activated (there was no answer to why they were deactivated.) After going back and forth Visa said that Amazon can only activate the card and then Amazon's customer service claimed they cannot activate the cards. After multiple hours on the phone, Amazon finally canceled both cards and refunded me through with an "Amazon gift card" linked to my account instead of refunding me through my original payment method (credit card) which was better than nothing at the time. Two weeks later Amazon re-ordered the Visa Gift cards and charged my credit card without my approval!! I had to call and tell them I didn't know why or how they re-ordered these Visa Gift cards for me and I demanded that they refund my money onto my credit card that they used without my consent and they finally did. Amazon and Visa made it very clear that their customer services don't work together to solve basic issues, it's disappointing as there was no direction and not even managers knew how to deal with their product or services and were not willing to contact each other. This was one of the worst experiences I have ever had with Visa and Amazons customer service. They need to figure out how to reactivate Visa Gift cards and how to work together as two businesses :(!!

rating 3.0. Handed to resident??!! I don't think so! Three Amazon gift cards that are to be given to my employees as Christmas gifts. No big deal. Except that I had them shipped to my house on a Saturday knowing that someone would be at home when the package would arrive. I did not see anything show up that day, I had been checking the front door step and mailbox until 6pm (after dark, of course). This morning (the next day) I decided to check to see the status of the package, thinking that it was going to be a late arrival. Order status says that it was delivered on Saturday, and then goes on to say that it was handed to resident. That is just a flat out lie! it was lying on the porch in front of the door (fortunately!). But the doorbell had not been rung, and it certainly wasn't handed to anyone in my house. If you made the delivery and left it on the doorstep, fine, just say that. But don't lie about handing it to someone in the house. That is really irritating. I'm thankful it wasn't stolen. It was small enough that it would not have been noticed from the road, especially after dark.

It was exactly as advertised, with the orange-ish bow and nestled quite lovely inside of the specialty gift box. If you are purchasing this gift card, do remember that they advertise there are no returns or refunds on gift cards.

Do enjoy your shopping and ability to choose from the millions of items available storewide at amazon.com. It is easier than ever now because we can scan to redeem the gift card with a mobile or tablet device using the Amazon App. I was also able to access the free one-day shipping as advertised, but they also specify that there are certain locations where residents would be able to take advantage of this.

I have read some reviews where customers said that they were not able to redeem the gift card, and believe me, I feel for them. When I made this purchase, I was silently praying that my code was not invalid or stolen as some of the customers indicated.

This is not only a thoughtful last minute gift, but the presentation is next to perfect because I do believe that the ribbon colour could have been a bit more festive. Nevertheless, thank you amazon.com.

rating 5.0. Taco Bell Gift Cards make a satisfying gift! What can I say, you can't go wrong with gift cards for occasions and this gift card is a favorite of college kids!

I gave this gift card as a gift and was really pleased with it. It is just a plain gift card but trust me, your recipient will love it! When I gave this card to my son, he was delighted. You know how many meals he can get especially if he eats off the dollar menu?! Taco Bell has a really nice dollar menu and the food is filling. Two burritos and a drink will cost you three dollars and will make a pretty filling and satisfying lunch!

All in all, he LOVED this gift card. There was no guessing the right size, getting the wrong item and even if he has a double of these, he'll still be happy! I highly recommend a Taco Bell card for anyone! Male, Female, old or young...it makes the perfect gift!

Disclaimer - I paid full price for this item and this is my honest and unbiased review. All opinions are my own based on personal experience.

rating 1.0. Gift card reload process is now harder and more confusing than it was before! After redesigning a workflow process, I think everyone will agree that it should be easier and less confusing. Amazon has managed to do the opposite!

- It now takes 5 clicks to add money to an existing gift card! Why?
- It's now more laborious to do multiple reloads in a row. The old process had a link at the end of the process to "reload again". The new process does not have such a link.
- It now appears like you are buying something from another company, which is super confusing and made me wonder if I was even doing the right thing.
- It now asks me to confirm whether I really want to use the debit card that I always use to purchase my gift card reloads. Yes already! Another un-necessary click to confirm every single time.

Usually Amazon has good user experience and workflow. Major fail here, Amazon.

input: rating 1.0. DO NOT BUY I purchased a pre-packaged Amazon gift card directly from Amazon.  The recipient entered the claim code, and received a message that the card has already been redeemed.  I figured that Amazon had accidently issued multiple cards with the same claim code, so I contacted customer service to ask that they replenish the gift card.  They refused to do so, and instead directed me to an FBI link for reporting internet fraud.  I am now out $100.  A did a quick google search of this issue, and I learned that this is a common problem with Amazon gift cards.  You are better off giving cash as gifts.  Never again will I purchase a gift card from Amazon!!!!

rating 1.0. unusable gift card My granddaughter always gets an Amazon gift card for Christmas. This year, she received an email after she submitted her order, telling her that there was a problem with the gift card, her order will not be shipped, and she is locked out of her account. She provided information they asked for, and tried to follow directions to get her account unlocked, but has been unable to even access her account, despite several calls and online chats with customer service.  I have had to intervene and I am still getting the runaround.  I had to get firm twice and insist that all directions were followed. I provided purchase information that I am the one who purchased the card directly from Amazon as I am an Amazon Prime member.  Reports have been submitted to Customer "care". rating 5.0. The Tale of the Gift Where to begin. Upon purchasing this gift card for a Christmas present I was not aware of what it might bring. It brought love, joy, romance, action and adventure!! Like a Dwain Johnson movie, but in card form.  I was whisked away to lands of beauty and lore. It took me to the top of the mountains and to the deeps of the sea.  I laughed till I cried, and cried till I loved. For I no longer knew love. it had been lost to me so very long ago to a Portuguese bride who could no longer be mine. But alas, this card was delivered and I knew in my soul, this would turn the days of dreary dreams into the magical bliss I would be longing for. Amazon, thank you for connecting me those things in life I have yearned for, yet lost in the tide so many years ago. So I say again with a burning in my heart where a cold bitterness once resided, thank you. For a million years throughout the cosmos, thank you and you're welcome.

Accuracy gets 0 stars since I wound up paying less than face value after accounting for credit card rebates,...
(You really should be taking advantage of offers for gift cards and other spending which can get you more balance credit than you pay for.)
Comfort, hmm, I'm perfectly comfortable with companies using their marketing budgets so that I actually pay less for what I choose to purchase. I wonder how the Advertising Value Equivalent for campaigns that offer rebates and discounts directly through credit cards and other channels is measured?
Oh yeah, make that a 5 for Comfort. I'll go so far as offer up a -5 Comfort for the wasted online adds that manage to slip through my defense in depth against such.

rating 5.0. Take care of your old gift cards! I found a surprising use for reloading my Amazon gift card. At one point I received several Visa gift cards worth about $100 each; by now I had, for example, $14 left on one, $9 on another, etc. I had not figured out a way to use them, and then I discovered the Amazon reload capability. If I had $4.57 left on one gift card I could reload the entire amount onto my Amazon gift card and get rid of the Visa gift card. Having combined all my old Visa gift cards into Amazon, I was surprised to be able to buy things "for free" :) on Amazon for almost two weeks, until I spent it all. (Since I am one of the people who gets a huge benefit from Amazon Prime, in that I probably place about 60 orders a year, that was a considerable time!)

rating 4.0. Dude, where's my PIN? Exactly what I needed. But knocking a star off for vulnerability to theft. Card ships activated, ready-to-spend, no secret PIN or password required. And since it's a gift card, there's no name printed on it -- which by itself is totally fine, but all these things taken together makes it a thief's delight. Like sending cash in the mail. Maybe all of this wouldn't have occurred to me, except the delivery person left the envelope on my front porch in full view of the public. Thanks be praised it wasn't stolen!

rating 3.0. May not be activated Honestly hit or miss. I have ordered these at least 10+ times in increments of 1 or 2 and, honestly, although most arrive activated, I have had multiple arrive unactivated, and customer service is not necessarily always the most helpful in these instances because it is already too late. From my understanding, the instances where they are unactivated are simply if the person packaging and shipping them is unaware of such or does not scan all of them. I once ordered 2 at once, and both arrived unactivated, but customer service did not refund me the full amount I had paid for both. However, they have arrived activated more often than not. If possible, I would recommend purchasing in store instead.

rating 5.0. this card will be just fine for you Not sure how so many people don’t know how a gift card works, but it’s activated upon delivery. To make payments to something online, you have to register it TO YOU. Any online purchase has to have a “bill-to” address, hence registering it under your name. Also, if you eat at a restaurant or for gas, or anything for an authorization, every single card will be ran for about 15%-20% of the card value. It’s not that hard. If your IQ is above 13, this card will be just fine for you.

Thus, one would either have to a) unwrap the gift card to determine its value or b) call Amazon and seek assistance.  After 20 minutes on the phone I was finally able to locate an Amazon employee who had me read each ( of SEVEN) card serial number to her so she could look up the value of each card. Come on, Amazon....I'm not the first one to complain about this.  I love you guys, but this is sloppy...

rating 1.0. Size Matters BE VERY CAREFUL.  If you order cards of different denominations, as I did. Don't order them all in the snowflake tin because there is no easy way to sort which tin is for which amount. They are individually shrinked wrapped with a covered claim code. The $100 have the denomination labelled on the card but can't be identified without a call to Amazon with the serial number or removing the individual tin shrink wrap. And even then the recipient you won't know the unlabelled card is for $25. Only after calling with the serial number and a long hold could I find out which were which. ARRGGHHH.  This is frustration plus wrapping, especially for someone with arthritis.  I  decided to return all the $25 cards.
rating 1.0. One Star I ordered gift cards for multiple amounts, and now I don't know which is which!
rating 2.0. Don't fall for this I ordered this gift card believing I would get a $50.00 gift card for $25.00.  NOPE.  Gift cards value was $25.00.  Just wrong to advertise this way.

rating 3.0. I ordered two gift cards in different amounts. They ... I ordered two gift cards in different amounts.  They were both in snowflake gift tins.  I assumed they would be marked so I would know the amount of each.  That is not the case.  The cards have serial numbers so I looked on the invoice to see the serial number for amount.  Not any help there either.  How do I check the value of each card.  I went to "amazon.com redeem" and it seemed the only thing I could do was attach the card to my account.  Not helpful at all.  If you are going to order gift cards from amazon, make sure you get different gift cards or tins, and write down what amount goes into which card.  Or make sure all the amounts are the same.

rating 1.0. Terminate the packer !!! I love Amazon goods & services...however, the packaging was the worst I have experienced for any on-line retailer and I purchase a lot on line. Worse yet, Amazon shipped via USPS and I got the lazy driver who simply dropped the cardboard package at the curb. The cardboard was NOT sealed at either end and the contents were exposed, hanging out of the package.
I now went to the original site and read the numerous reviews. All customers complained about the terrible packaging and many experienced more severe problems with the contents being crushed, etc. Amazon apparently does not read customer reviews because they continue to use an inappropriate package. All Amazon has to do to correct the poor packaging is to add package tape to both ends of the package. Next year I will buy the gift cards at my local church...no packaging required.
The rating reflects the packaging, not the item.

rating 5.0. Sturdy cardboard gift card affixed within an adorable tin! I ordered three of these cute, snowman gift card tins this year. One was sent to someone out of state, and two were sent to my home for local gifts. All of the tins (3 separate orders) arrived with the card well-affixed inside, and all of the tins arrived in perfect condition. The card is made of a heavy card board rather than the plastic ones of the past. This is better for the environment folks!  This particular tin has a ribbon on top so that it can be used as an ornament or easily tied to a wrapped gift. The tin opens well and closes firmly. It has a window in the back which reveals the number, but with the security code hidden. This adorable tin is free from Amazon with the purchase of your gift card, and the card has no expiration date. We all know that you can buy just about anything from Amazon, so these cute gift tins make really good gifts!!!

rating 1.0. Terrible product, difficult to use, no customer support You're probably thinking the prepaid Mastercard (MetaBank) is the same as the prepaid Visa (Sunrise), right?  Nope.  The prepaid MC (MetaBank) is barely supported by MasterCard.  My card would not work, so I called the number on the back on the card.  They have no live support.  Then I called MasterCard directly and was disconnected multiple times.  After 3-4 attempts I was able to speak to a live rep who transferred me to multiple outside lines.  It was a nightmare.  Finally I got a young, unprofessional person, presumably working out of a basement in Utah, who informed me that I couldn't send money across PayPal using this card.  Why on Earth not?!?  It's my money and my card.  Why can't I send money over PayPal?  I had to request an invoice just to transfer funds with the card.  I had previously used this card and the Visa sold on Amazon several times.  I will never, ever use this card again though.  It took several phone calls and hours of my time to finally use this card.  They're supposed to be activated at the time of purchase, yet this card was blocked and flagged, before I could finally use it.  Don't even think about trying to use it at a gas station or store.  Avoid MetaBank and the Blackhawk Network if possible.

rating 5.0. Money was on card Update! The card still works but some locations it just declines it no matter if it's ran as debit or credit..(dollar store). Otherwise, we got gas today and paid inside it worked fine, used at a drive thru and worked fine so I have no complaints. It's good for things like that, as far as paying bills I don't think it will work. Also if you go to check out and you're wanting to use you're remaining balance along with another form of payment to cover the difference you must tell the cashier the exact amount you want to take off the card, or it will decline it. Overall, it's just what I needed and so far I've had no issues with it.

rating 5.0. AVOID FRUSTRATION AND READ THIS I buy these cards all the time. They help me with spending issues I've had in the past.  They work wonderfully. However, based on a lot of the reviews I've read, it's important you read the info pamphlet so you clearly understand how to go about using it.  This will avoid many of the frustrations I read about in other reviews.  First, this card can not be used to retrieve cash.  Second, if you plan on using it for online purchases you need to register it in your name which will link it to your zip code. When merchants run CC #'s, the 4 main things that need to match with the card are name, card #, zip code and most of the time the CVV # on the back of the card. As long as you registered it correctly any online purchase will always go through assuming there's enough $ on the card.  Third, every retailers CC machines operate differently. So, it's important to ask the cashier if their machine automatically subtracts the amount available on the card from your purchase balance or if you need to tell them the exact amount that's on the card so they can manually punch that in before you swipe the card.  Asking this will avoid the card from getting declined. If your purchase balance is less then what's available on the card, then you don't need to ask. Also, there's been a handful of times where mine got declined even though I knew I had plenty of funds. I just re-ran the card as debit instead of credit and it works.  If you haven't registered your card and need to run it as debit, the pin is the last 4 digits on the card.  Fourth, if you purchase items that may hv a chance of being returned, try very hard to keep that card on hand even if it's all used up.  Most retailers insist they reimburse your $ bk onto its original form of payment or you'll end up with store credit. There's been many times I went to return and threw out my card already and had to basically make a scene to get cash back.  Lastly, never ever ever pay at the pump when getting gas w these types of prepaid cards.  There's extra security set up on the CC machine at gas pumps. It knows its a prepaid card, so it will hold all the funds available on your card for probably 7 days. Any retailer that accepts CC has to batch out their machine before any of those funds are deposited into their bank account. This is why they hold the funds.  And honestly for whatever reason that I don't understand it seems that these cards can't be used to pay bills online or over the phone. But, that's no fault of the card. That will happen no matter what prepaid card you use.  I hope this helps as you scroll along other reviews regarding their troubles they experienced. I believe if you are aware of this then it will help avoid any frustrations and help you determine if this is the right type of card depending on your needs.

rating 1.0. Avoid Buying Gift Card -- Activation and use problems, plus difficulty getting refund. My dad activated the card, obtained PIN, and attempted to use it at gas station pump. Unable to use it - "not valid" message.

August 13 Contacted gift card company about problem and they advised card can only be used at attendant terminal.

On August 14 attempted to use card at CVS cashier for $8.36 purchase. Advised only $0.01 available.

Contacted gift card company and they sent email showing fraudulent transactions. My dad completed forms and sent back. Dad just contacted me asking for purchase receipt.

All of this was a major hassle and turned out to be a terrible gift.

Bottom line. Gift card company sells $100 card. Waits for activation. Then after complaint, they provide this type email showing multiple transactions that are not realistic but amounts to $99.99. They then require extensive paperwork and phone information (name, address, phone) and indicate it will be 60-90 days for them to investigate with retail merchants on the transactions. After they complete actions they will then either provide new card or re-activate existing.

rating 5.0. Purchased 20 of these in last 6 months..ZERO problems I’ve read a lot of 1 Star Reviews on this saying they couldn’t activate that card. I’ve ordered approximately 20 of these in the last six months and I never once had a problem. They’re always delivered within two days and I’ve had no issues at all. To use it as a debit card- the pin number is the last four digits of the card number.
Otherwise it functions exactly like a credit card. And the phone number to check your balance is simple, quick and very accurate.
The only thing I wish is that there were more styles of card or they came and gift boxes like the Amazon and Whole Foods ones do. It would be great if there were Trader Joe’s gift cards available on Amazon… But, until then I’ll just keep using these excellent Visa cards.

rating 4.0. Bent and included extra card the gift card arrived bent... not too badly, but still not ideal for presentation. ALSO- I opened the shrink wrap around the card and found TWO gift cards enclosed. I didn't know if perhaps they split the balance among 2 cards, or if one was invalid. (Or perhaps BOTH were invalid). So I had to remove the security strip to get the claim code... to then check the balance of the cards. And indeed, one included the full balance, the other was blank. This was a problem I was able to solve. But had I not opened the shrink wrap, the gift recipient would have been confused, and would have had to do that legwork on her own… Again, it's not the end of the world. I did receive the product I ordered.
I have ordered many Amazon gift cards in the past, and will continue to do so. But this experience was not perfect.
