rating 5.0. Excellent way to give a gift card! Absolutely perfect gift for Mothers Day, or any occasion where the recipient loves flowers. My mother is a HUGE flower nut (who's isn't?) so to go along with the theme of other flower related gifts, I had to get her an Amazon gift card as well so she could pick out something she's really wanted. This flower pot reveal is simply perfect, and she loved it!!

The only qualm I may see that others have noted is that there really isn't much writing space, so you might not want to plan on using this to present your message. It is a truly awesome way to give someone a gift card.

rating 1.0. Don't believe Amazon promotional ads in the emails I purchased these gift cards as part of a promotion Amazon was running. I will upload a photo of the email I received. In the email it stated "Get $15.00 Amazon credit with a $50 Amazon gift card purchase". It's the end of the school year, so I purchased 3 $50.00 gift card for my kids teachers. I figured that was a great deal for both of us, I spend $35.00 and they get $50.00 for their classroom.

Four days later I attempt to purchase a birthday present and was reminded to check on my $45.00 credit I thought I had coming. I couldn't find it, used all their "self help" links, and finally found a phone number to speak to a human and get help. First lady was wonderful, told me the reason I didn't get it was because I had purchased all three in one transaction, instead of 3. I thought that was silly so I asked for a supervisor.

The supervisor, Deo located in the Philippines, was the supervisor I was connected to. He informed me what the email really meant was ONE $15.00 credit for purchasing OVER $50.00 in gift cards. Clearly that email is open to interpretation. He said it was written in the terms and conditions...however when I tried to read them for myself I was sent to a different screen (picture attached). I asked to speak with his supervisor, to which he informed me he is the very top of the customer service center. I asked for his boss. He said he was only authorized to give me a mailing address. Dumbfounded I asked him if Amazon corporate office in Washington does not have phones or email. He said they are only allowed to contact them via their computer system.

Most of my conversation with Deo was in silence. I'm assuming he was typing up everything I was saying for amazon's records. 54 minutes into my conversation the line disconnected and they never called back. I called back to get amazon's address and was surprisingly connected to a customer service rep who worked in the same department as Deo. He spoke with Deo (who was unable to speak with me since he was busy with another customer). The new customer service rep, Ian, could not have been kinder and more professional. I felt like he was actually listening to my complaint verse Deo who just wanted to record every little thing I said.

Ian helped me get the contact information I needed, and also gave me the email address of where I could forward a copy of the email in question. Ian also provided me with the PO Box for Amazon Corporate Office in Seattle. Since I can not voice my compliant to Amazon corporate office directly, and I must write a letter, I figured I would share it with all who would listen. I hope your Amazon experience is better than mine. And Deo, you might want to pay attention to Ian and Mercy - they are customer service pros.

Long story short, Amazon - who is worth $430 BILLION - would rather lose a long time customer over $30. But as for the product, it was adorable and looked great in my candy bouquets. I'll attach a picture of that too.

***UPDATE*** After I emailed Amazon as part of a survey they sent, I received the following response:
Hello,

I understand you're concerned about $15 credit for purchasing $50 gift cards on our website.

I can completely understand how frustrating and annoying the situation can be when something like this happens but believe us it was never our intention.

After reviewing your previous correspondences with our customer service, I can confirm the information provided to you was correct. As my colleague mentioned, the promotional credit will be provided only one per customer.

You can view the restrictions for promotion code here:

http://www.amazon.com/promos

I realize this is frustrating; however we aren't able to offer any additional insight or action on this matter and any further inquiries regarding this issue won't receive a response.

We appreciate your understanding.

Best regards,
Madhan P.
Amazon.com

I did go to that link, and there is nothing that addresses my concern or complaint. I wrote back asking them to highlight the part that actually has to do with my complaint. Don't worry, I'll update this if they ever respond.

rating 1.0. These cards are blank!! Don’t gift.. You’ll look like a fool! I ordered six of these cards for my daughters teachers for teachers appreciation week. They arrived on time but apparently they were not activated by Amazon!
Just imagine you gift this card to somebody and when they try to redeem it states the balance on this is zero dollars! How embarrassing!!
I’m glad I checked with one of the teachers to see if it worked because it was a large amount $250 contributed by all the students’ parents and she said that the card was invalid!! The other teachers might have even found it awkward to tell us that the card was blank. It was a gift after all !
I called amazon customer service and ask them to activate these cards. They told me that they need to have the teachers call them directly with claim codes so they can apply the balance to their individual accounts.
Amazon was not able to activate them on their end without each person I gifted it to calling them. It was really a bad experience - first by giving somebody a gift card that was blank and then having them deal with the customer service themselves to get the money they were gifted. I am really really embarrassed and extremely unhappy with the experience. please please please do not make a fool of yourself by getting these!!

rating 2.0. Do not purchase multiples at the same time If you are only buying one (or several of the same quantity), you can ignore this low review.

I purchased several of these gift cards as gifts for my son's teachers and therapists for end of the school year gifts. I purchased six cards in three different amounts ($15,$20,$40). When the cards arrived, there was no way to tell which amount was on which card. There was a note that said that I could line up the card number with the order number. Unfortunately as they were all connected to same order number, it was not possible to tell. I contacted customer service for help, however they were very reluctant to assist. I'm not sure if it was just the agent, or an issue with gift cards in general, but it was not good customer service, that's for sure.

Anyhow, if you are purchasing multiples in different amounts, purchase each card separately to avoid such issues.

The card itself is cute.

rating 4.0. Got you cardboard flowers... [[VIDEOID:c113e753e31ece0da8873aa8805634a7]] Got you cardboard flowers...and an Amazon gift card!!

First thing to note is that a little setup is required. Card was placed on back for some reason and must move to the card slot up front. Something to keep in mind when gifting.

Material for card stock and print is of very high quality.

Slider can be a bit loose. Not a nice smooth transition every time depending on how you slide it, but does give the card reveal nevertheless.

Amazon gift card itself is very basic. Just Amazon logos on a white backdrop.

Redeeming card is easy. There's a peel off sticker (instead of scratcher) on back of the card, no need to worry about scratching off the text of the code. If you use the amazon app you can redeem the amount in seconds: click the camera option and align the boxes with the text code.

rating 1.0. Don't believe Amazon promotional ads in the emails I purchased these gift cards as part of a promotion Amazon was running. I will upload a photo of the email I received. In the email it stated "Get $15.00 Amazon credit with a $50 Amazon gift card purchase". It's the end of the school year, so I purchased 3 $50.00 gift card for my kids teachers. I figured that was a great deal for both of us, I spend $35.00 and they get $50.00 for their classroom.

Four days later I attempt to purchase a birthday present and was reminded to check on my $45.00 credit I thought I had coming. I couldn't find it, used all their "self help" links, and finally found a phone number to speak to a human and get help. First lady was wonderful, told me the reason I didn't get it was because I had purchased all three in one transaction, instead of 3. I thought that was silly so I asked for a supervisor.

The supervisor, Deo located in the Philippines, was the supervisor I was connected to. He informed me what the email really meant was ONE $15.00 credit for purchasing OVER $50.00 in gift cards. Clearly that email is open to interpretation. He said it was written in the terms and conditions...however when I tried to read them for myself I was sent to a different screen (picture attached). I asked to speak with his supervisor, to which he informed me he is the very top of the customer service center. I asked for his boss. He said he was only authorized to give me a mailing address. Dumbfounded I asked him if Amazon corporate office in Washington does not have phones or email. He said they are only allowed to contact them via their computer system.

Most of my conversation with Deo was in silence. I'm assuming he was typing up everything I was saying for amazon's records. 54 minutes into my conversation the line disconnected and they never called back. I called back to get amazon's address and was surprisingly connected to a customer service rep who worked in the same department as Deo. He spoke with Deo (who was unable to speak with me since he was busy with another customer). The new customer service rep, Ian, could not have been kinder and more professional. I felt like he was actually listening to my complaint verse Deo who just wanted to record every little thing I said.

Ian helped me get the contact information I needed, and also gave me the email address of where I could forward a copy of the email in question. Ian also provided me with the PO Box for Amazon Corporate Office in Seattle. Since I can not voice my compliant to Amazon corporate office directly, and I must write a letter, I figured I would share it with all who would listen. I hope your Amazon experience is better than mine. And Deo, you might want to pay attention to Ian and Mercy - they are customer service pros.

Long story short, Amazon - who is worth $430 BILLION - would rather lose a long time customer over $30. But as for the product, it was adorable and looked great in my candy bouquets. I'll attach a picture of that too.

***UPDATE*** After I emailed Amazon as part of a survey they sent, I received the following response:
Hello,

I understand you're concerned about $15 credit for purchasing $50 gift cards on our website.

I can completely understand how frustrating and annoying the situation can be when something like this happens but believe us it was never our intention.

After reviewing your previous correspondences with our customer service, I can confirm the information provided to you was correct. As my colleague mentioned, the promotional credit will be provided only one per customer.

You can view the restrictions for promotion code here:

http://www.amazon.com/promos

I realize this is frustrating; however we aren't able to offer any additional insight or action on this matter and any further inquiries regarding this issue won't receive a response.

We appreciate your understanding.

Best regards,
Madhan P.
Amazon.com

I did go to that link, and there is nothing that addresses my concern or complaint. I wrote back asking them to highlight the part that actually has to do with my complaint. Don't worry, I'll update this if they ever respond.

rating 4.0. Got you cardboard flowers... [[VIDEOID:c113e753e31ece0da8873aa8805634a7]] Got you cardboard flowers...and an Amazon gift card!!

First thing to note is that a little setup is required. Card was placed on back for some reason and must move to the card slot up front. Something to keep in mind when gifting.

Material for card stock and print is of very high quality.

Slider can be a bit loose. Not a nice smooth transition every time depending on how you slide it, but does give the card reveal nevertheless.

Amazon gift card itself is very basic. Just Amazon logos on a white backdrop.

Redeeming card is easy. There's a peel off sticker (instead of scratcher) on back of the card, no need to worry about scratching off the text of the code. If you use the amazon app you can redeem the amount in seconds: click the camera option and align the boxes with the text code.

Pay attention if you purchase with an incentive I like having a gift card balance from which payment is made for small ticket items and tend to uncheck the box for applying a gift card balance for purchases where a credit card payment is more beneficial, as when an extended warranty is offered.  Since this has no expiration date, I find that it allows me to save time when balancing my credit card statements at the end of the month, particularly since I can check my gift card balance and see exactly when and for what amount the payment was made.  When Amazon offers an incentive to reload is when I typically make my purchases, and although it has not applied the additional amount instantaneously, I have found that after a phone call, it miraculously tends to appear.  For this reason, I have to deduct a star from my review.

rating 1.0. How to find out the value of your newly purchased Amazon Gift Cards We ordered multiple values like many others and had a horrible time figuring out the value of each card. The packaging on the plastic cover for each card states "To check the purchase value of the gift card, simply match at the last 4 digits of the serial number on the back of the gift card to those listed on www.amazon.com/orders."  You cannot see the serial numbers using the normal screen on your phone.  You need to look on your computer or select Desktop Site on your cell phone. For android select the 3 little dots in the upper right hand corner in the address bar, then select Desktop Site. For iPhone, select the aA in the address bar, then Request Mobile Website.  You can then select your order and see the serial numbers.

rating 4.0. Bent and included extra card the gift card arrived bent... not too badly, but still not ideal for presentation. So I had to remove the security strip to get the claim code... to then check the balance of the cards. And indeed, one included the full balance, the other was blank. This was a problem I was able to solve. But had I not opened the shrink wrap, the gift recipient would have been confused, and would have had to do that legwork on her own… Again, it's not the end of the world. I did receive the product I ordered.
I have ordered many Amazon gift cards in the past, and will continue to do so. But this experience was not perfect.

rating 5.0. Excellent way to give a gift card! Absolutely perfect gift for Mothers Day, or any occasion where the recipient loves flowers. This flower pot reveal is simply perfect, and she loved it!!

The only qualm I may see that others have noted is that there really isn't much writing space, so you might not want to plan on using this to present your message. It is a truly awesome way to give someone a gift card.

rating 1.0. Don't believe Amazon promotional ads in the emails I purchased these gift cards as part of a promotion Amazon was running. I will upload a photo of the email I received. In the email it stated "Get $15.00 Amazon credit with a $50 Amazon gift card purchase". It's the end of the school year, so I purchased 3 $50.00 gift card for my kids teachers. I figured that was a great deal for both of us, I spend $35.00 and they get $50.00 for their classroom.

Four days later I attempt to purchase a birthday present and was reminded to check on my $45.00 credit I thought I had coming. I couldn't find it, used all their "self help" links, and finally found a phone number to speak to a human and get help. First lady was wonderful, told me the reason I didn't get it was because I had purchased all three in one transaction, instead of 3. I thought that was silly so I asked for a supervisor.

The supervisor, Deo located in the Philippines, was the supervisor I was connected to. He informed me what the email really meant was ONE $15.00 credit for purchasing OVER $50.00 in gift cards. Clearly that email is open to interpretation. He said it was written in the terms and conditions...however when I tried to read them for myself I was sent to a different screen (picture attached). I asked to speak with his supervisor, to which he informed me he is the very top of the customer service center. I asked for his boss. He said he was only authorized to give me a mailing address. Dumbfounded I asked him if Amazon corporate office in Washington does not have phones or email. He said they are only allowed to contact them via their computer system.

Most of my conversation with Deo was in silence. I'm assuming he was typing up everything I was saying for amazon's records. 54 minutes into my conversation the line disconnected and they never called back. I called back to get amazon's address and was surprisingly connected to a customer service rep who worked in the same department as Deo. He spoke with Deo (who was unable to speak with me since he was busy with another customer). The new customer service rep, Ian, could not have been kinder and more professional. I felt like he was actually listening to my complaint verse Deo who just wanted to record every little thing I said.

Ian helped me get the contact information I needed, and also gave me the email address of where I could forward a copy of the email in question. Ian also provided me with the PO Box for Amazon Corporate Office in Seattle. Since I can not voice my compliant to Amazon corporate office directly, and I must write a letter, I figured I would share it with all who would listen. I hope your Amazon experience is better than mine. And Deo, you might want to pay attention to Ian and Mercy - they are customer service pros.

Long story short, Amazon - who is worth $430 BILLION - would rather lose a long time customer over $30. But as for the product, it was adorable and looked great in my candy bouquets. I'll attach a picture of that too.

***UPDATE*** After I emailed Amazon as part of a survey they sent, I received the following response:
Hello,

I understand you're concerned about $15 credit for purchasing $50 gift cards on our website.

I can completely understand how frustrating and annoying the situation can be when something like this happens but believe us it was never our intention.

After reviewing your previous correspondences with our customer service, I can confirm the information provided to you was correct. As my colleague mentioned, the promotional credit will be provided only one per customer.

You can view the restrictions for promotion code here:

http://www.amazon.com/promos

I realize this is frustrating; however we aren't able to offer any additional insight or action on this matter and any further inquiries regarding this issue won't receive a response.

We appreciate your understanding.

Best regards,
Madhan P.
Amazon.com

I did go to that link, and there is nothing that addresses my concern or complaint. I wrote back asking them to highlight the part that actually has to do with my complaint. Don't worry, I'll update this if they ever respond.

rating 1.0. These cards are blank!! Don’t gift.. You’ll look like a fool! I ordered six of these cards for my daughters teachers for teachers appreciation week. They arrived on time but apparently they were not activated by Amazon!
Just imagine you gift this card to somebody and when they try to redeem it states the balance on this is zero dollars! How embarrassing!!
I’m glad I checked with one of the teachers to see if it worked because it was a large amount $250 contributed by all the students’ parents and she said that the card was invalid!! The other teachers might have even found it awkward to tell us that the card was blank. It was a gift after all !
I called amazon customer service and ask them to activate these cards. They told me that they need to have the teachers call them directly with claim codes so they can apply the balance to their individual accounts.
Amazon was not able to activate them on their end without each person I gifted it to calling them. It was really a bad experience - first by giving somebody a gift card that was blank and then having them deal with the customer service themselves to get the money they were gifted. I am really really embarrassed and extremely unhappy with the experience. please please please do not make a fool of yourself by getting these!!

rating 2.0. Do not purchase multiples at the same time If you are only buying one (or several of the same quantity), you can ignore this low review.

I purchased several of these gift cards as gifts for my son's teachers and therapists for end of the school year gifts. I purchased six cards in three different amounts ($15,$20,$40). When the cards arrived, there was no way to tell which amount was on which card. There was a note that said that I could line up the card number with the order number. Unfortunately as they were all connected to same order number, it was not possible to tell. I contacted customer service for help, however they were very reluctant to assist. I'm not sure if it was just the agent, or an issue with gift cards in general, but it was not good customer service, that's for sure.

Anyhow, if you are purchasing multiples in different amounts, purchase each card separately to avoid such issues.

The card itself is cute.

rating 1.0. Don't you think we should separate the price? I bought three $30 and one $50. It was packed together without distinction, so it was ruined when I gave it. I just gave him anything. And the cards are made of paper. Don't you think we should separate the price?

rating 4.0. Got you cardboard flowers... [[VIDEOID:c113e753e31ece0da8873aa8805634a7]] Got you cardboard flowers...and an Amazon gift card!!

First thing to note is that a little setup is required. Card was placed on back for some reason and must move to the card slot up front. Something to keep in mind when gifting.

Material for card stock and print is of very high quality.

Slider can be a bit loose. Not a nice smooth transition every time depending on how you slide it, but does give the card reveal nevertheless.

Amazon gift card itself is very basic. Just Amazon logos on a white backdrop.

Redeeming card is easy. There's a peel off sticker (instead of scratcher) on back of the card, no need to worry about scratching off the text of the code. If you use the amazon app you can redeem the amount in seconds: click the camera option and align the boxes with the text code.

rating 1.0. DO NOT BUY GIFT CARD DIRECTLY FROM AMAZON I purchased this gift card as a gift and the recipient used a small portion of it. We have called many times and have not gotten our money back. DO NOT BUY A GIFT CARD DIRECTLY FROM AMAZON! Reviews show this has happened many times to customers and occurs frequently. rating 1.0. DO NOT BUY I purchased a pre-packaged Amazon gift card directly from Amazon.  The recipient entered the claim code, and received a message that the card has already been redeemed. They refused to do so, and instead directed me to an FBI link for reporting internet fraud.  I am now out $100.  A did a quick google search of this issue, and I learned that this is a common problem with Amazon gift cards.  You are better off giving cash as gifts.  Never again will I purchase a gift card from Amazon!!!!

rating 1.0. unusable gift card My granddaughter always gets an Amazon gift card for Christmas. She provided information they asked for, and tried to follow directions to get her account unlocked, but has been unable to even access her account, despite several calls and online chats with customer service.  I have had to intervene and I am still getting the runaround.  I had to get firm twice and insist that all directions were followed. I provided purchase information that I am the one who purchased the card directly from Amazon as I am an Amazon Prime member.  Reports have been submitted to Customer "care".  I am questioning if they really do "care".  The simplest resolution would be to cancel her order and refund my money.  For some reason, they seem to have difficulty with this concept. I am very disappointed.  I have been a loyal Amazon customer for a long time.

rating 5.0. Attractive and Thoughtful. I bought this for my brother and he WhatsApped me the photos as he went through the steps opening it. It was exactly as advertised, with the orange-ish bow and nestled quite lovely inside of the specialty gift box. If you are purchasing this gift card, do remember that they advertise there are no returns or refunds on gift cards.

Do enjoy your shopping and ability to choose from the millions of items available storewide at amazon.com. It is easier than ever now because we can scan to redeem the gift card with a mobile or tablet device using the Amazon App. I was also able to access the free one-day shipping as advertised, but they also specify that there are certain locations where residents would be able to take advantage of this.

I have read some reviews where customers said that they were not able to redeem the gift card, and believe me, I feel for them. When I made this purchase, I was silently praying that my code was not invalid or stolen as some of the customers indicated.

This is not only a thoughtful last minute gift, but the presentation is next to perfect because I do believe that the ribbon colour could have been a bit more festive. Nevertheless, thank you amazon.com.

rating 2.0. Just learned about the 2% issue, it NEVER worked for me... Guess I can't be too upset at losing something that never worked in the first place, but Hindsight Being 2020, seems there was some sort of 2% Deal from 2017-2021. What I guess I should be doing now is finding out why it worked with my account, how it slipped through the cracks, and what this means going forward - what else isn't working?

Other than that, it is a very convenient feature, and great when one needs to reload their balance at 3 in the morning and doesn't want to go out for a gift card. It seems to have changed (again) in the last few weeks, and am a bit on the nervous side what this may (or may not) mean. Hopefully things will continue to work, even though they don't appear to be working with 100% accuracy. Some times it Takes A While as the Powers That Be need to make sure that the transaction is safe-and-secure, but these days a little extra time is the price we pay.

rating 4.0. Consistent Breakfast; Pancakes This was a holiday lightning deal that I couldn't pass up. I don't know why but I have always liked IHOP a lot. Sometimes you just want breakfast and you can get it there day or night.

Sure, it's not always perfect but it's pretty consistent from one franchise restaurant to the next. Sometimes it's good to know that you are going to find just about the same thing at whichever one you find yourself sitting. Plus they have pancakes just about any way you want.

On the rest of the menu, I can't recall a time I've ever had anything that wouldn't be considered breakfast in the hundreds of times I've eaten in an IHOP. So if you've read this far, take this review with a grain-of-salt (pun intended) knowing that I've only eaten breakfast here.

If you do not really need the box it is worth giving it a try as you may get lucky.  But, do not count on it if you really need it for a gift!

rating 1.0. Terminate the packer !!! I love Amazon goods & services...however, the packaging was the worst I have experienced for any on-line retailer and I purchase a lot on line. Worse yet, Amazon shipped via USPS and I got the lazy driver who simply dropped the cardboard package at the curb. The cardboard was NOT sealed at either end and the contents were exposed, hanging out of the package.
I now went to the original site and read the numerous reviews. All customers complained about the terrible packaging and many experienced more severe problems with the contents being crushed, etc. Amazon apparently does not read customer reviews because they continue to use an inappropriate package. All Amazon has to do to correct the poor packaging is to add package tape to both ends of the package. Next year I will buy the gift cards at my local church...no packaging required.
The rating reflects the packaging, not the item.

rating 5.0. Stars are nice addition to gift card! I turned the card around so the Happy Holiday message would be what shows in little window instead of a bar code and the amount. Nice way to present a gift card. However, of course could be put in a little box and wrapped if you prefer. What I WOULD like to see is more, different messages. Happy Holidays is not my preference, better to say what holiday it is, such as MERRY CHRISTMAS, JOY TO THE WORLD, BLESSINGS, PEACE, that type. A nice extra to the gift card though. Thanks Amazon!

rating 1.0. Absolutely disgusted with Amazon gift card service Absolutely disgusted with Amazon gift card service. I advise everyone to NEVER buy any gift card here!

We had one typo trying to redeem the gift card and now the money is apparently gone forever. Have spent days with customer service being promised by multiple representatives that they will help, but none will. Have received several emails all saying the issue has been resolved, it has not been resolved. Received an email saying my account has been refunded, it hasn't. After untold hours trying to get a simple balance transfer, I gave up and tried to purchase a replacement e-card. Found out that I can't purchase an e-card from my bank account - that's against Amazon policy too. E-cards must be purchased from a credit card even though the original gift card was purchased from my bank account.

At this point I am convinced that the only relevant policy Amazon has is that they will never forgive anyone for a typo and will make things as difficult as possible until I just give up and go away.

rating 4.0. Elegant (albeit generic) winter holiday gift card tin Even though this gift-card-in-a-tin is designed to be a generic holiday gift, I purchased it as a birthday gift for a friend with a winter birthday because none of the Amazon birthday designs I saw appealed to me and because I liked the idea of a reusable gift card container. (I may be wrong, but I don't believe any of Amazon's birthday gift cards are packaged in tins.) The design of this one is obviously intended to be suitable for any winter holiday -- Christmas, New Year, Hanukkah (note that this snowflake has six points, like the Star of David, and is blue, the official Hanukkah color), Kwanzaa, Festivus, etc. This generic design worked in my favor because I intended to use it for a birthday gift, but if I had been shopping for a holiday gift, I would have preferred a design specific to that particular holiday.

I generally avoid giving people gift cards because I find them impersonal (as well as for other practical reasons that I won't go into here), but when I do, I prefer to present them in gift containers instead of just slipping them into cards for a couple of reasons: a package make the gift card a bit less guessable and reduces the chance that the gift card will be overlooked or discarded by mistake, as can happen when it's enclosed in a card. However, I also try to be environmentally responsible and choose packaging, like this tin, that can be re-used over and over, in place of wrapping paper that is often thrown out after a single use.

One selling feature for this Amazon card was that at the time I bought it, it came with free one-day shipping. I'm a Prime member, so I would have gotten "free" 2-day shipping anyway, but as a chronic procrastinator, every little bit helps.

Overall, I was pleased with this gift card/gift tin and thought it looked rather classy, as far as gift card boxes go, and it's likely that I'll purchase another (or similarly packaged gift card) next year.

rating 5.0. Please read my review before.you buy I will say I'm an avid gift card buyer. Some call me the gift card guru. It's fire proof, waterproof, weather proof, child proof, people proof the works. If you were trapped on a deserted island with this gift card know that all your worries would wash away knowing you could rest comfortably at night having this gift card by your side.

I took one of these cards on a trip to the Himalayas and was trapped on the Chinese and indian border in heavy fire, if it hadn't been for this gift card i would not be here today writing this review. The Chinese were firing on the indian elite forces and i was smack dab in the middle of this with no salvation in sight. I whipped out my sat phone and dialed into Amazon prime - low and behold bezos himself airdropped in not only support to get me out but free prime video subscriptions and kindles for the troops. The firing stopped snd the soldiers were out into a state of euphoria.

Jeff called to me from his prime black hawk helicopter snd thru me a best seller rope ladder. Luckily this gift card was there otherwise i would be a dead man.

Thanks Jeff you truly have conquered the hearts of men and country.

I now buy these cards like the catcher in the rye. One is not enough i just need more.

Free Reindeer Ornament (and Box) with Gift Card Purchase [[VIDEOID:6b54148bd4781e6be110f9cfafe8b370]] First thing to note is that this is one of Amazon's Premium gift cards. $50 minimum on gift card for purchase. Gift cards easy to slide out and replace with another one if you think $50 is too much to give

Has a nice detachable reindeer ornament (via Velcro) to a box with ribbon design

Inside there's a gift card with checkered red design background with Amazon logos

No blank space for name of person of sender and receiver. Best to give to person directly (like proposing with a wedding ring) or combine it as part of other gift

Redeeming gift card is easy. There's a peel off sticker (instead of scratcher), so no need to worry about scratching off the text of the code. If you use the amazon app you can redeem the amount in seconds: click the camera option and align the boxes with the text code.

Doesn't show the amount on the card seeing it's customizable on the website. But it does give a pack in slip of paper to verify amount.

rating 1.0. DO NOT BUY GIFT CARD DIRECTLY FROM AMAZON I purchased this gift card as a gift and the recipient used a small portion of it. Someone hacked into the remaining balance and used it to buy items and have them shipped to a different state. This is fraud and Amazon has been very in helpful in giving us our money back. We have called many times and have not gotten our money back. DO NOT BUY A GIFT CARD DIRECTLY FROM AMAZON! Reviews show this has happened many times to customers and occurs frequently.  I have purchased Amazon cards from local stores and have NEVER had someone steal my money. It has only happened when I bought directly from Amazon.  BUYER BEWARE. If I could have given 0 stars I would have.

rating 2.0. Amazon Reload: Marketing: Meh; Technology: Yeesh! Amazon has changed up its Gift Card Reload Program. The Eye Candy leaves me, a regular Gift card reloader, underwhelmed.

Indeed, it looks and feels more confusing.

The underlying tech seems to have changed.
Previously both the amount of the reload and the 2% cashback showed up quicker.

Now the reload transaction took a little longer to show up. The 2% Cash Back took even longer to show up.

Amazon failed to have announced the changes ahead of time!

Given the changes, the transaction should not slowed down and the 2% Cash Back should have been instanteous as it was previously.

Can't say I can blame other customers more alarming bad reviews. I join those saying, if it wasn't broke, why change it?

The explanation could have been given in the announcement that didn't happen.

I'll continue to use the program and monitor the situation.

rating 5.0. Sturdy cardboard gift card affixed within an adorable tin! I ordered three of these cute, snowman gift card tins this year. One was sent to someone out of state, and two were sent to my home for local gifts. All of the tins (3 separate orders) arrived with the card well-affixed inside, and all of the tins arrived in perfect condition. The card is made of a heavy card board rather than the plastic ones of the past. This is better for the environment folks!  This particular tin has a ribbon on top so that it can be used as an ornament or easily tied to a wrapped gift. The tin opens well and closes firmly. It has a window in the back which reveals the number, but with the security code hidden. This adorable tin is free from Amazon with the purchase of your gift card, and the card has no expiration date. We all know that you can buy just about anything from Amazon, so these cute gift tins make really good gifts!!!

rating 4.0. Consistent Breakfast; Pancakes This was a holiday lightning deal that I couldn't pass up. I don't know why but I have always liked IHOP a lot. Sometimes you just want breakfast and you can get it there day or night.

Sure, it's not always perfect but it's pretty consistent from one franchise restaurant to the next. Sometimes it's good to know that you are going to find just about the same thing at whichever one you find yourself sitting. Plus they have pancakes just about any way you want.

On the rest of the menu, I can't recall a time I've ever had anything that wouldn't be considered breakfast in the hundreds of times I've eaten in an IHOP. So if you've read this far, take this review with a grain-of-salt (pun intended) knowing that I've only eaten breakfast here.

rating 2.0. Do not purchase multiples at the same time If you are only buying one (or several of the same quantity), you can ignore this low review.

I purchased several of these gift cards as gifts for my son's teachers and therapists for end of the school year gifts. I purchased six cards in three different amounts ($15,$20,$40). When the cards arrived, there was no way to tell which amount was on which card. There was a note that said that I could line up the card number with the order number. Unfortunately as they were all connected to same order number, it was not possible to tell. I contacted customer service for help, however they were very reluctant to assist. I'm not sure if it was just the agent, or an issue with gift cards in general, but it was not good customer service, that's for sure.

Anyhow, if you are purchasing multiples in different amounts, purchase each card separately to avoid such issues.

The card itself is cute.

rating 1.0. Don't believe Amazon promotional ads in the emails I purchased these gift cards as part of a promotion Amazon was running. I will upload a photo of the email I received. In the email it stated "Get $15.00 Amazon credit with a $50 Amazon gift card purchase". It's the end of the school year, so I purchased 3 $50.00 gift card for my kids teachers. I figured that was a great deal for both of us, I spend $35.00 and they get $50.00 for their classroom.

Four days later I attempt to purchase a birthday present and was reminded to check on my $45.00 credit I thought I had coming. I couldn't find it, used all their "self help" links, and finally found a phone number to speak to a human and get help. First lady was wonderful, told me the reason I didn't get it was because I had purchased all three in one transaction, instead of 3. I thought that was silly so I asked for a supervisor.

The supervisor, Deo located in the Philippines, was the supervisor I was connected to. He informed me what the email really meant was ONE $15.00 credit for purchasing OVER $50.00 in gift cards. Clearly that email is open to interpretation. He said it was written in the terms and conditions...however when I tried to read them for myself I was sent to a different screen (picture attached). I asked to speak with his supervisor, to which he informed me he is the very top of the customer service center. I asked for his boss. He said he was only authorized to give me a mailing address. Dumbfounded I asked him if Amazon corporate office in Washington does not have phones or email. He said they are only allowed to contact them via their computer system.

Most of my conversation with Deo was in silence. I'm assuming he was typing up everything I was saying for amazon's records. 54 minutes into my conversation the line disconnected and they never called back. I called back to get amazon's address and was surprisingly connected to a customer service rep who worked in the same department as Deo. He spoke with Deo (who was unable to speak with me since he was busy with another customer). The new customer service rep, Ian, could not have been kinder and more professional. I felt like he was actually listening to my complaint verse Deo who just wanted to record every little thing I said.

Ian helped me get the contact information I needed, and also gave me the email address of where I could forward a copy of the email in question. Ian also provided me with the PO Box for Amazon Corporate Office in Seattle. Since I can not voice my compliant to Amazon corporate office directly, and I must write a letter, I figured I would share it with all who would listen. I hope your Amazon experience is better than mine. And Deo, you might want to pay attention to Ian and Mercy - they are customer service pros.

Long story short, Amazon - who is worth $430 BILLION - would rather lose a long time customer over $30. But as for the product, it was adorable and looked great in my candy bouquets. I'll attach a picture of that too.

***UPDATE*** After I emailed Amazon as part of a survey they sent, I received the following response:
Hello,

I understand you're concerned about $15 credit for purchasing $50 gift cards on our website.

I can completely understand how frustrating and annoying the situation can be when something like this happens but believe us it was never our intention.

After reviewing your previous correspondences with our customer service, I can confirm the information provided to you was correct. As my colleague mentioned, the promotional credit will be provided only one per customer.

You can view the restrictions for promotion code here:

http://www.amazon.com/promos

I realize this is frustrating; however we aren't able to offer any additional insight or action on this matter and any further inquiries regarding this issue won't receive a response.

We appreciate your understanding.

Best regards,
Madhan P.
Amazon.com

rating 5.0. Perfect way to convert used (or new) giftcards to Amazon with no fees. I had a small stack of used giftcards sitting around. You know, when the balance gets to like $2.34, and you don't know what to do with them? Easy to "recharge gift balance" with all of those cards sitting around!

1. Look up the balance.
2. Click Account & Lists
3. Select Gift Cards
4. Click the yellow "Reload Your Balance" button
5. Enter Gift Card balance under the $(Other) block.
6. Click Buy Now
7. Under Payment Method, Select Change and enter gift card information.
8. Verify and click "Place Order"

You can repeat with all of your applicable gift cards. Just remember to go through and clean up your payment methods when you are done, so you don't have 13 random debit cards to choose from in the future.

rating 5.0. Reload your Amazon.com Gift Card with the balance from another gift card. Great way to use up small balances on Visa, MC, AE, or any other gift card that Amazon accepts for payment. Add a gift card to your payment account. Then reload your Amazon.com Gift card with the exact balance of that other gift card. After confirmation is received that the balance was transferred to your Amazon.com gift card you can remove from your account the other card as it will now have a zero balance. There is no minimum amt. that needs to be transferred. So gather up all your old gift cards and put that money onto an Amazon.com Gift Card. Why let money go to waste when you can spend it at Amazon.com

rating 5.0. Free Socks (size 10-13) With Gift Card Purchase Update: They now added sock size in description

First thing to note is that the sock size is 10-13 US. Both my friend and I received the same size.
Description on product page states "Gift Card is affixed inside a gift box with a pair of Happy Socks (one size)" so I assume everyone will receive the same. If not, or want to confirm (if purchased) please put a comment to let others know.

Quality of the socks are standard; not too thin and not too sweat absorbent (like sport socks). Great for formal wear and those who want to draw attention to their feet, ankles, and/or shoes with the bright colors/patterns.

Happy socks usually go for around $16 a pair, so that's how much you are potentially saving

Gift card design is very basic; just red with amazon logos on it. Redeeming card is easy. There's a peel off sticker (instead of scratcher), so no need to worry about scratching off the text of the code. If you use the amazon app you can redeem the amount in seconds: click the camera option next to search bar, swipe up for gift card redeem photo option and align the boxes with the text code.

Box is very presentable; very good gift idea for a family or friends

Overall if you're going to spend money on Amazon throughout the year, or generous enough to give someone a pair of socks + $100+ gift card; then this is a excellent purchase; just keep in mind the sock size

rating 3.0. Useful Gift Card Information If you receive a gift card, you must register it in the "Your Account" section of your online Amazon account before it can be used. There is nothing in the information that comes with your gift card to tell you this step. Once you have registered your gift card whatever you next purchase or purchases involve, the cost will be first deducted from your gift card balance including Kindle e-book purchases until the balance of the gift card is depleted. Too bad Amazon didn't think to include this information with the gift card.

rating 1.0. Beware of incorrect amount loaded on gift card Amazon totally messed up our gift cards we ordered for our employees. Of the 13 cards received, six cards were actually for a different amount than what was listed by the serial #'s on the packing slip. Our employees only realized the actual amount was wrong when they redeemed them into their respective Amazon account and then it was too late. So we have employees that have to pay us back $ and we have to do more gift cards for employees that were shorted. DO NOT PURCHASE MULTIPLE GIFT CARDS FROM AMAZON! Amazon has not provided a resolution to this problem.

input: rating 5.0. Not sure what's up with the negative reviews I received this card on time and was able to use it without any problems. You have to register online, however. rating 5.0. Fast, Easy Setup, No Hassle!!! My husband lost his debit card and needed gas for the week. I was able to pay for this giftcard via bank account on Amazon to solve the issue. The card came quickly and was easy to set up over the phone. The webpage is easy to use too. I recommend registering your card there. You can see a record of your purchases, like you can at a bank. It also can protect you against theft. It’s not a scam. Always look for the Amazon Verified Purchase near headings of reviews, when buying items like these. Amazon doesn’t require any additional information.
Note: Cannot be used at the ATM, no cash back, and gas must be purchased inside, instead of at the pump. Enjoy.

rating 4.0. Southwest website gift card policy Gift card came relatively quickly and was easy to use.

Be aware, Southwest.com only allows you to use 3 total forms of payment per transaction. Which means if you are intending to pay entirely with gift cards, and three gift cards don't add up to the total balance of your purchase, you are only allowed to use 2 gift cards plus a 1 credit card (3 total forms) to cover the entirety of the purchase.

If you try to checkout and enter three gift cards that do not cover the entirety of the purchase the system automatically removes one of the cards to give you the ability to enter a form that covers the remaining balance as the final 3rd form of payment. Given that you are only able to buy a maximum of $200 card denominations on this website, the maximum ticket price you can buy via these gift cards is $600.

Long story short, if you intend to buy these gift cards on Amazon, buy the largest denominations you can to make your life easier, and plan on paying some out of pocket if you can't cover the ticket price with $600 ($200/card x 3 cards).

Also, there is no way (on the website or on the phone with Southwest customer service) to combine the balance of any gift cards.

rating 1.0. Terrible product, difficult to use, no customer support You're probably thinking the prepaid Mastercard (MetaBank) is the same as the prepaid Visa (Sunrise), right?  Nope.  The prepaid MC (MetaBank) is barely supported by MasterCard.  My card would not work, so I called the number on the back on the card.  They have no live support.  Then I called MasterCard directly and was disconnected multiple times.  After 3-4 attempts I was able to speak to a live rep who transferred me to multiple outside lines.  It was a nightmare.  Finally I got a young, unprofessional person, presumably working out of a basement in Utah, who informed me that I couldn't send money across PayPal using this card.  Why on Earth not?!?  It's my money and my card.  Why can't I send money over PayPal?  I had to request an invoice just to transfer funds with the card.  I had previously used this card and the Visa sold on Amazon several times.  I will never, ever use this card again though.  It took several phone calls and hours of my time to finally use this card.  They're supposed to be activated at the time of purchase, yet this card was blocked and flagged, before I could finally use it.  Don't even think about trying to use it at a gas station or store.  Avoid MetaBank and the Blackhawk Network if possible.

rating 1.0. Terrible service - Not printing out the gift message and did not indicate who sent the gift to the receiver Socially it is rude that a gift receiver does not acknowledge to the sender after receiving the gift. After not receiving any word from the receiver, I waited very long time before making a step to check with the receiver about the gift. It turned out that my written message for good wishes was not delivered by Amazon.com. Socially it could be very damage to the future relationship between a gift giver and its receiver, had it not been handled correctly. It could defeat the good intention of sending a gift.

rating 1.0. One-Day Delivery Didn't Happen This card was billed as one-day delivery.  It arrived in our city within the specified time, but was delivered at least 22 hours after that.  I normally don't choose rush delivery, but we wanted to include the gift card in a package.  Because we tried to wait for the card, we had to pay very pricey delivery charges on the main package -- and we still have to mail the gift card.  I know we could have done it direct but we were trying to surprise someone.  Lesson learned.

rating 4.0. This does not act as a hotel key for some reason At first I assumed this was a a multifunction card used for credit as well as other typical card functions.  When staying at a Comfort Inn in Pittsburgh, I tried to use this to unlock my hotel room door.  I must have inserted the card into the face of my door half a dozen times before realizing Amazon had ripped me off.  I went to the hotel’s front desk, borderline hysterical, and said “There must have been some kind of mistake!”  My handler found me and told me everything was alright and it turns out that these actually aren’t used for opening hotel room doors.  It turns out the hotel gives you a free card every time you stay with them, but when you check out, it doesn’t work anymore.  However, I was able to buy products from Amazon using the $25 I had already spent on the card.  That made me feel better.  Thanks Amazon!

rating 5.0. Ummmmm.... Well, how exactly do you review a gift card? I paid $25 and received a piece of plastic that was *worth* $25. So....5 stars!!!

In all seriousness though, I buy at least 3-5 of these every year for Christmas presents. Pretty much everyone (including my 74 year old mom, who literally can't even remember what the Start button is on her Windows laptop) shops on Amazon now, so these are just as good as cash in my opinion, without the ridiculous "activation" fees of buying Visa or MC gift cards. I seriously never understood why people fall for that nonsense. Plus, Amazon usually does free next day shipping around Christmas which makes it great for those last minute gifts and you don't feel like fighting with 10 other people for the last couple gift cards at Walgreens or CVS.

rating 4.0. Pretty Enough Box and Bow for Any Occasion It's a $25 gift card. I bought it to take advantage of an Alexa/Echo offer. The box and bow are attractive but they were in the garbage in five minutes and a few minutes after that the credit balance was in my account. Sending the pretty box and bow was nice and only other option was a white gift box with bow. Why I couldn't have been emailed the $25 code I don't know but I actually feel a little guilty for Amazon wrapping this up nicely and shipping it overnight. I'd have been happy with a code to copy, paste and redeem. Now all Amazon had to do, as promised, is email my $5 voucher for buying this $25 card.

rating 5.0. Gift Card With Style Just your standard Amazon Giftcard inside a box that has the word 'Amazon' embossed on the front cover with an orange bow.

There are finger grooves for the front cover and inside the box for easy access.

Claiming the gift card once opened is also very easy. There's a peel off sticker so you don't need to bother with getting a coin or sharp metal object. Also you don't need to worry about scratching off the text for the gift card code.

If the person you gift it to have the Amazon app; they can click on the camera icon (after logging in) next to the search bar, and scan the code instead of typing each letter/number individually to claim the total amount.

Overall very simplistic design and a has great presentation. Great if you're tired of gifting gift cards in a standard envelope format.

current_product_reviews: rating 5.0. Physical card is sometimes preferred It's a gift card in a small box with an orange ribbon and bow on the box top. You can send an Amazon gift card by email or hand deliver one like this. They all work the same, money which is applied to a person's Amazon account.  Only problem I have with gift cards is that purchases don't register as "verified purchase" when the gift card balance is used for payment and you leave a review.
other_product_reviews: rating 4.0. Pay attention if you purchase with an incentive I like having a gift card balance from which payment is made for small ticket items and tend to uncheck the box for applying a gift card balance for purchases where a credit card payment is more beneficial, as when an extended warranty is offered.  Since this has no expiration date, I find that it allows me to save time when balancing my credit card statements at the end of the month, particularly since I can check my gift card balance and see exactly when and for what amount the payment was made.  When Amazon offers an incentive to reload is when I typically make my purchases, and although it has not applied the additional amount instantaneously, I have found that after a phone call, it miraculously tends to appear.  For this reason, I have to deduct a star from my review.

input:
rating 5.0. Elderly aunt was very grateful for this gift I gave it to my 86-year-old aunt who I didn't know well until she was elderly. I always thought that when she was younger, she had lived a middle-to-upper class life, but found out recently that she has had a tough life financially.

Anyway, she was so overwhelmed with the gift card (I think she was surprised by the amount) that she exclaimed and then started to cry. She got up and gave me a huge hug (and she is not physically expressive) and kept thanking me over and over. This is one of the best reactions to giving a gift I have ever gotten. I wish I had given her more (and I wish I had gotten to know her a long time ago).

rating 1.0. Don't buy these!!! I ordered 2 $100 Visa gift cards and I'm beyond disappointed. When I received my cards I tried to use them and they both were declined. I also tried to check my balance through Visa and was told that Amazon canceled my cards so they were no longer activated (there was no answer to why they were deactivated.) After going back and forth Visa said that Amazon can only activate the card and then Amazon's customer service claimed they cannot activate the cards. After multiple hours on the phone, Amazon finally canceled both cards and refunded me through with an "Amazon gift card" linked to my account instead of refunding me through my original payment method (credit card) which was better than nothing at the time. Two weeks later Amazon re-ordered the Visa Gift cards and charged my credit card without my approval!! I had to call and tell them I didn't know why or how they re-ordered these Visa Gift cards for me and I demanded that they refund my money onto my credit card that they used without my consent and they finally did. Amazon and Visa made it very clear that their customer services don't work together to solve basic issues, it's disappointing as there was no direction and not even managers knew how to deal with their product or services and were not willing to contact each other. This was one of the worst experiences I have ever had with Visa and Amazons customer service. They need to figure out how to reactivate Visa Gift cards and how to work together as two businesses :(!!

rating 5.0. Taco Bell Gift Cards make a satisfying gift! What can I say, you can't go wrong with gift cards for occasions and this gift card is a favorite of college kids!

I gave this gift card as a gift and was really pleased with it. It is just a plain gift card but trust me, your recipient will love it! When I gave this card to my son, he was delighted. You know how many meals he can get especially if he eats off the dollar menu?! Taco Bell has a really nice dollar menu and the food is filling. Two burritos and a drink will cost you three dollars and will make a pretty filling and satisfying lunch!

All in all, he LOVED this gift card. There was no guessing the right size, getting the wrong item and even if he has a double of these, he'll still be happy! I highly recommend a Taco Bell card for anyone! Male, Female, old or young...it makes the perfect gift!

Disclaimer - I paid full price for this item and this is my honest and unbiased review. All opinions are my own based on personal experience.

rating 1.0. DO NOT BUY GIFT CARD DIRECTLY FROM AMAZON I purchased this gift card as a gift and the recipient used a small portion of it. Someone hacked into the remaining balance and used it to buy items and have them shipped to a different state. This is fraud and Amazon has been very in helpful in giving us our money back. We have called many times and have not gotten our money back. DO NOT BUY A GIFT CARD DIRECTLY FROM AMAZON! Reviews show this has happened many times to customers and occurs frequently.  I have purchased Amazon cards from local stores and have NEVER had someone steal my money. It has only happened when I bought directly from Amazon.  BUYER BEWARE. If I could have given 0 stars I would have.

rating 1.0. HORRIBLE CUSTOMER SERVICE. 1K CARD WAS STOLEN AND THEY WON'T CREDIT MY ACCT! I cannot begin to chronicle my horrible experience with amazon customer service. I purchased this card and it said it was delivered but it wasn't. so I called immediately and told AMAZON that the card was stolen. I was told the would shut off that card, and that I would be reimbursed my 1K and to then order a new card. Two days later, I ordered a replacement card.  I received that card and thought all was okay. However, AMAZON turned off the WRONG card. they turned off and refunded the REPLACEMENT card that I ordered and NOT the STOLEN CARD. so the person I gave the replacement card to was never able to use it.  I have been calling, emailing, etc. to AMAZON for over a month now -  have all my documentation for this including their email where they say they will fix this (multiple times) but never do.  it just sits in limbo.....They have YET to fix it and refund my 1K for their mistake. I have to tell the entire story over and over again every time I call just to be told (and with a follow up email) that someone in gift card escalation will resolve this in 48 hrs and get back to me. It has been over a MONTH, I have NEVER been contacted and this still is NOT resolved. it is infuriating how much time I have spent trying to get AMAZON To merely rectify their mistake and refund me for my 1K gift card that my person cannot even use. as I type this, I am on hold (again) with AMAZON customer service STILL trying to get resolution........but I am not holding my breath. So, the gift card is GREAT as long as AMAZON doesn't screw up. but if they do - don't expect them to resolve it anytime in your lifetime. HORRIBLE CUSTOMER SERVICE!

rating 4.0. If I hadn't read the reviews..... I ordered 2 of these $100 Mastercard Gift cards, I ordered last night and received them today. I realized there was nothing stating the cards were activated.  They were purchased as Christmas gifts and I wanted to make sure they were usable; as well as, ensure there were no issues once the recipients went to use.  I immediately jumped online to look at reviews for help....If I hadn't read the reviews I would not have known you have to go online and register each card (Include your name, email and address ~ creating a pin is optional). The card is activated once you register it and the available balance shows up. LOVE IT!!!! I thought I was going to have to argue with Amazon for a refund. I gave 4 stars because Amazon should note in their description that card can only be activated for use by registering it online.

rating 4.0. old great grandson who lives over 4 hrs In August I sent one to my 9 yr. old great grandson who lives over 4 hrs. away from us for his birthday.  I know his parents have Amazon Prime, so I told him he could pick out a DVD, books (he loves to read!), or a toy. rating 2.0. Would be fine except... The box is very pliable aluminum or something similar.  This would be fine as it is a cute design however they ship it totally unprotected.  It is just shipped in a padded envelope which has no supportive structure so of course it is squished and mangled when you get it.  If you do not really need the box it is worth giving it a try as you may get lucky.  But, do not count on it if you really need it for a gift!

rating 1.0. Terminate the packer !!! I love Amazon goods & services...however, the packaging was the worst I have experienced for any on-line retailer and I purchase a lot on line. Worse yet, Amazon shipped via USPS and I got the lazy driver who simply dropped the cardboard package at the curb. The cardboard was NOT sealed at either end and the contents were exposed, hanging out of the package.
I now went to the original site and read the numerous reviews. All customers complained about the terrible packaging and many experienced more severe problems with the contents being crushed, etc. Amazon apparently does not read customer reviews because they continue to use an inappropriate package. All Amazon has to do to correct the poor packaging is to add package tape to both ends of the package. Next year I will buy the gift cards at my local church...no packaging required.
The rating reflects the packaging, not the item.

rating 5.0. Stars are nice addition to gift card! I turned the card around so the Happy Holiday message would be what shows in little window instead of a bar code and the amount. Nice way to present a gift card. However, of course could be put in a little box and wrapped if you prefer. What I WOULD like to see is more, different messages. Happy Holidays is not my preference, better to say what holiday it is, such as MERRY CHRISTMAS, JOY TO THE WORLD, BLESSINGS, PEACE, that type. A nice extra to the gift card though. Thanks Amazon!

rating 5.0. Taco Bell Gift Cards make a satisfying gift! What can I say, you can't go wrong with gift cards for occasions and this gift card is a favorite of college kids!

I gave this gift card as a gift and was really pleased with it. It is just a plain gift card but trust me, your recipient will love it! When I gave this card to my son, he was delighted. You know how many meals he can get especially if he eats off the dollar menu?! Taco Bell has a really nice dollar menu and the food is filling. Two burritos and a drink will cost you three dollars and will make a pretty filling and satisfying lunch!

All in all, he LOVED this gift card. There was no guessing the right size, getting the wrong item and even if he has a double of these, he'll still be happy! I highly recommend a Taco Bell card for anyone! Male, Female, old or young...it makes the perfect gift!

Disclaimer - I paid full price for this item and this is my honest and unbiased review. All opinions are my own based on personal experience.

Good Only for Use in the USA and not for Internet/telephone/mail orders outside I think there should be a clear description in the Amazon webpage product information, that is lacking, and unfortunately found out after 3 separate purchases of the card and thought a merchant was at fault. The 3rd time, I read the microprint sheet that accompanied the mastercard and read the following:

"Your card is valid in the U.S. only...it cannot be used at merchants outside the United States, including internet and mail/telephone order merchants outside the United States..."

So the purchase of all 3 cards was worthless and the extra money that you pay to get this card was for naught. Extremely disappointing I lost $5.95 on this card, $4.95 on another, and $4.95 on another - charges to buy the card on top of its value.

rating 1.0. Do not buy this, save your self from troubles. I bought 10 cards. They don't work, I call the 800 # at the back, ask me to email them. I call Amazon for refund, somehow Amazon support said those cards were fully and partially used. I have 7 cards that was not even open or give away yet, how could those be fully or partially used. There is fraud/theft happening at the either gift card company or Amazon end. After 3 phone calls with 4 support and at least 2-3 hours of holding, the last support refunded me as "1 time exception" using different transition that I have in my Amazon account. I took it as 1 time exception because I am tired of explaining over and over again. This should be not "1 time exception", this should be fully refunded to customer and escalate this to fraud dept to prevent future issues like that so that customer like me don't have to face this embarrassment. Those cards are not good.
Because of this problem, I don't trust gift card any more as option for giving gifts.

rating 1.0. Do not buy it So here it is Christmas morning and I’ve been on the phone for an hour trying to get this card to work. We activated the card on the phone, it said it was active... go online, and no matter what website, it does not accept the card. It says invalid. Even going to the website stated on the back of the card doesn’t recognize the card. For all of the rude people assuming that those of us who have not had luck with the card working, have not followed directions... there aren’t any real directions to follow outside the normal “activating” process. When you can’t even register the card on the website stated on the back of the card, clearly there is an issue with these cards. I’ve purchased these gifts cards before and they are hit or miss. This is ridiculous that Amazon continues to sell these on their site knowing there are so many issues with it. I could have walked to the store 5 houses down and purchased a gift card, but instead purchased thru Amazon and now my son is sitting here on Christmas bummed out because he can’t buy a game for his new oculus quest 2. Shame on Amazon.

rating 1.0. Scam, fake website Do not order these cards! Somehow, someone has created a scam. I went to the website listed on the card to see if there was still a balance but the website was fake and I couldn't log in. I had to be persistent when chatting with an Amazon representative, but after giving proof of purchase and explaining multiple times what happened, I was given an email to write to. That resolved the issue and thankfully I was given a refund. But very disappointing for my daughter, she wasn't able to use her gift.

rating 4.0. The product itself is great and worked perfectly, but Amazon falsified the shipping time. To those who are having trouble with their cards make sure you receive an activation slip in your package so you know that it was scanned and will work. Next, you want to make sure you go to the website on the back of the card, enter your card information and register the card and setup your pin. Also make sure you check the card balance afterwards to make sure your card was activated and has the amount it's supposed to have.

I received my card yesterday morning, I followed the instructions on the website on the back of the card to register. It was very easy. No problems at the store using the card. However, when ordering my card Amazon said Guaranteed delivery by June 4th so I bought. Upon reviewing my order after purchase Amazon said Guaranteed delivery June 5th by 5pm. I attempted to contact Amazon via live chat but he was just making excuses.
The next day I reviewed my order yet again and it said Guaranteed delivery June 5th 9pm. Also, I'm a prime customer so I should have received it in a days time (I ordered it on the 3rd) but didn't receive it until the 5th. So basically it took over 2 days to get the package.

other_product_reviews: rating 5.0. Amazon Gift Cards Are Versatile For Getting Gift Cards Overnite To Instant Gifts In Minutes! These gift cards are great for anytime during the year. My daughter  and her now husband had moved in together in 2013 and since she had a who apt of everything because she had a place with a couple of friends until the homeowner became ill and had to sell the house. So in 2013 they had a lot and bought some things together. They were married in 2017,  so they registered here in Amazon, BBB, Target and Walmart so some of their friends purchased gifts and most of the others gave gift cards and they learned the great speed of the Instant Pot, a new microwave and the great Air Fryer ( they bought me the Air Fryer for Christmas and I love it!)! . So now they have some of their older apt stuff is packed up in the garage. My new "son" found the wonder of Amazon Gift Cards , so for holidays, all year I get them cards which they load onto their Amazon account and when they want to buy something, Amazon ,has  everything, so they have the  funds and they don't have to wait til they can afford whatever it is. There are the regular cards that look like credit cards so I give them to friends and family for bigger holidays and Amazon includes gift  boxes that are like wrapped gifts and I don't need to wrap. They also have email where you find e-mail cards, ( some are animated and are so cute and funny) and the recipient can either just take the code out and use use it or they can actually print and fold it and they have a card or you can print it and fold it put it in a small envelope and you can give it to them. Amazon also has cards for merchants like bigger brick and mortar cards, restaurants, movie theaters, etc.  There might even be more, but right now they escape me. They are so quick, that if you find you need a gift quick, you can actually put the info in your computer in Amazon, print the card out, fold it and put it in an envelope and you have instant gifts.
